samples.title: labhire / metagpt-custom/qwen3-coder-480b-a35b-instruct

project_context.introduction:
### Product Scope

LabHire is a research internship information integration platform for university teachers and students. It supports research internship project publication, recommendation, retrieval, application, management, mutual evaluation, personal information, resumes, mentor search, community communication, internship referrals, and management-side review. The system scope is limited to university research internship information and community referral posts, and does not include social enterprise recruitment information or headhunting advertisements.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for developers, testers, project managers, customer representatives, and project-related personnel such as marketing, operations, and maintenance staff.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Performance | The system shall provide timely feedback for any user input. | For any user input, the system gives feedback within 1 second. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Robustness | The system shall remain normally operational under all possible user inputs. | It does not crash because of abnormal input and provides timely feedback for erroneous operations. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Access Control | The system shall ensure that each type of user can only obtain and modify information within their permissions. | Mentors can only view resumes of students selected for their projects; students cannot view each other's resumes; group members can view basic information but cannot view resumes. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Data Confidentiality | The system shall protect login, registration, and chat data. | User login/registration and chat content must be encrypted; the specification does not specify an encryption algorithm. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-005 | Abuse Prevention | The system shall restrict abnormal login and operation behavior. | Supports login and operation frequency limits based on IP and user behavior, graphical verification codes, SMS verification codes, and abnormal behavior detection. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Auditability | The system shall support back-end risk-control logs and behavior audit. | Risk-control logs and behavior audit modules support manual analysis and alerting. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-007 | Usability | The system shall allow most new users to naturally find functional modules and learn how to use them. | Usage instructions are provided to guide the remaining users. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-008 | User Interface Quality | The system interface shall improve efficiency and friendliness around user roles. | The student side may be more attractive; the mentor side should be concise, clear, and aesthetically pleasing; input/output methods, interaction methods, and functional distribution should support work efficiency. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-009 | Correctness | System operation and presentation logic shall conform to functional requirement goals. | Key functional flows are consistent with the functional requirements in this SRS. | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | Clarity | System descriptions shall be clear and functional boundaries shall be clearly divided. | Requirements, pages, and module responsibilities are not confused. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-011 | Extensibility | The system shall reduce coupling between components and support extension. | Component coupling is reduced, and new functions do not require broad changes to existing modules. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-012 | Browser Compatibility | The system shall be normally accessible through mainstream browsers. | Chrome, Firefox, or Microsoft Edge can access core functions normally. | High | Test |
| NFR-013 | Portability | The system shall support browsing on multiple device types while maintaining consistent page logic and functions. | Core page logic and functions are consistent between mobile and desktop. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Legal Compliance | User-published content displayed by the system shall comply with relevant laws. | Violating posts or comments can be reviewed and deleted, and displayed content complies with relevant laws. | High | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | The system scope is limited to university research internship information and community internship referrals, excluding social enterprise recruitment and headhunting advertisements. |
| C-002 | Registered users shall complete student or mentor identity certification through university email domains and verification codes. |
| C-003 | Enterprise-certified students must pass enterprise email verification before publishing internship referral posts. |
| C-004 | Administrator account passwords are directly assigned by the back-end database, and the system has no administrator registration entry. |
| C-005 | Manual identity review shall be completed by administrators within 72 hours. |
| C-006 | Student resume upload supports formats such as PDF, with maximum file size 10 MB. |
| C-007 | Community posts are limited to within 400 words and support text formats such as HTML and Markdown. |
| C-008 | Mobile devices shall satisfy quad-core 1.5 GHz CPU, 1 GB RAM + 4 GB ROM, Android 7.0 or iOS 8.0 and above, and WeChat 6.5.6 and above. |
| C-009 | Desktop devices shall satisfy at least 2 GB memory, broadband network, Windows 7 or Mac OS X 10.9 and above; Chrome, Firefox, or Microsoft Edge is recommended. |
| C-010 | The system and requirements documents shall follow GB/T 8566-2007, GB/T 8567-2006, and GB/T 9385-2008, and Python code shall follow PEP8. |
| C-011 | Display of user-published content must comply with relevant laws. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Mobile hardware | Quad-core 1.5 GHz CPU, 1 GB RAM + 4 GB ROM. |
| Mobile software | Android 7.0 and above, iOS 8.0 and above, WeChat 6.5.6 and above. |
| Desktop hardware/network | At least 2 GB memory and broadband network. |
| Desktop software | Windows 7 and above, Mac OS X 10.9 and above; Chrome, Firefox, or Microsoft Edge is recommended. |

### Software/API Interfaces

| Interface | Requirement Summary |
| --- | --- |
| Email verification-code interface | Registration must send a verification code to a university email address; enterprise certification must send a verification code to an enterprise-domain email address. |
| Notification interface | Favorite updates, mentor messages, comment replies, project applications, and review states need to trigger notifications; review feedback can be sent through in-site messages and email push. |
| Chat interface | Mentors chat with applicant students in pro
...[truncated]

samples.title: AwesomeMusic / direct/gpt-5

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
Its purpose is to provide structured input for architecture generation, test design, and requirements traceability for an AI music creation and sharing platform.

### Product Scope

The AI music creation and sharing platform is a Web system for music enthusiasts. After registration, users can use the platform for AI music creation, score generation, score upload and sharing, favorites, follows, recommendations, search, hot charts, and downloads. The system contains three modules: Web frontend, Web backend, and algorithm service. The algorithm service generates music from user-provided source files and includes online music generation and offline model iterative upgrade services.

This document uses the specification definitions of MQ, score, track seed, and midi music. A score is an abc-format plain-text file that can generate midi music. A track seed is the beginning part of an abc score and is less than 10 seconds long. Midi music is a music file playable by midi-capable players.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for customers, product managers, developers, testers, and downstream architecture generation and requirements review workflows. Target end users are music enthusiasts, especially users interested in AI music creation, music sharing, and novel approaches to music creation.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Performance | Average account registration latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Performance | Average login latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Performance | Average score favorite latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Performance | Average follow-user latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | Performance | Average score upload latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 3000ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Performance | Average model favorite latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-007 | Performance | Average Web-side score generation latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-008 | Performance | Average score download latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 3000ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-009 | Performance | Average recommendation retrieval latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 500ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | Performance | Average score search latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 1000ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-011 | Performance | Average hot-chart viewing latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-012 | Performance | Algorithm training for generation models shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-013 | Performance | Algorithm score generation shall satisfy throughput and latency requirements. | Throughput 5ops, average latency <= 1min. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Performance | Classifying all scores shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-015 | Performance | Song recommendation shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-016 | Scalability | The Web system shall support the preset concurrent access throughput. | Concurrent access throughput is no less than 2000QPS. | High | Test |
| NFR-017 | Scalability | The Web system shall support transaction change throughput. | Transaction changes are no less than 100TPS. | High | Test |
| NFR-018 | Usability | System interactions shall be suitable for music production beginners. | Interactions are simple and understandable and do not require professional function design. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-019 | Usability | The system shall provide user instructions, prompts, and guidance. | Comprehensive instructions, prompts, and guidance exist to help users get started quickly. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-020 | Security | The gateway shall isolate internal and external network boundaries. | Internal systems are not exposed externally due to loose security configuration. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-021 | Reliability | Important data shall be stored in multiple replicas. | Important data is stored in 3 replicas. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-022 | Availability | The system shall remain available under partial failures. | The system remains usable when some internal nodes are upgraded, crash, or experience a certain level of network partition. | High | Test |
| NFR-023 | Maintainability | Code shall be readable. | Code follows naming conventions, and names correspond to actual meaning. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-024 | Maintainability | Code shall avoid excessive coupling. | Code supports later iterative optimization, extension of existing functions, and addition of new functions. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-025 | Load Balancing | The system shall maintain stable response and reasonable resource utilization under high access volume. | Load balancing technology is provided when user access volume is high. | High | Test |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Frontend languages are HTML, CSS, and JavaScript; the frontend framework is Bootstrap. |
| C-002 | Backend language is Golang; backend framework is Gin. |
| C-003 | Webpage response time shall be within 1 second. |
| C-004 | Music generation model training time shall be within 2 hours. |
| C-005 | The algorithm model shall be able to generate enough varieties of music styles. |
| C-006 | Generated music quality shall be above the baseline. |
| C-007 | Algorithm model response time shall be controlled within 1 minute. |
| C-008 | Product interfaces shall reflect user friendliness and have a certain degree of aesthetics. |
| C-009 | Project R&D shall strictly follow the project management process to avoid phase delays affecting later development. |
| C-010 | System hardware environment consists of 4 virtual machines, each with CPU 4 * 2400MHz, 8GB memory, and 200GB storage. |
| C-011 | System software environment includes Ubuntu 18.04 LTS, Hadoop 3.2.2, RabbitMQ 3.8.14, etcd 3.4.15, and Nginx 1.19.8. |
| C-012 | Communication protocols are HTTPS and TCP/IP. |
| C-013 | Task metadata must conform to database constraints and limit task size. |
| C-014 | When task submission fails, erroneous data needs to be automatically skipped and retried. |
| C-015 | All requirements listed in use-case diagrams are planned for the current version and do not need to be assigned to future versions. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Hardware | 4 virtual machines, each with CPU 4 * 2400MHz, 8GB memory, and 200GB storage. |
| Operating system | Ubuntu 18.04 LTS. |
| Software interfaces | Apache Hadoop 3.2.2, RabbitMQ 3.8.14, etcd 3.4.15, and Nginx 1.19.8. |
| Communication protocols | HTTPS and TCP/IP. |
| Frontend technology |
...[truncated]

completeness.reasoning: The architecture diagram covers the vast majority of functional (FRs), non-functional (NFRs), and data requirements (DRs). All 18 FRs, 25 NFRs, and 16 DRs are addressed either explicitly in notes or implicitly through component design. Most constraints (C-s) are also covered, particularly those related to technology stack, performance, and protocols. A minor discrepancy exists with Constraint C-010 (4 virtual machines), as the diagram uses `VM(s)` for scalability, which is a rational interpretation given other NFRs but deviates from a strict count. Usability (NFR-018, NFR-019) and Maintainability (NFR-023, NFR-024) are acknowledged in notes but are less directly reflected in the structural architecture itself, as they are often code-level concerns.

faithfulness.reasoning: The diagram faithfully represents the core modules (Web frontend, Web backend, algorithm service, scheduling) and their interactions as described in the SRS. All specified technologies (Nginx, RabbitMQ, etcd, Hadoop, Golang/Gin, HTML/CSS/JS/Bootstrap, Ubuntu 18.04) and communication protocols (HTTPS, TCP/IP) are correctly incorporated. The interpretation of the '4 virtual machines' constraint (C-010) as logical groupings that can scale (using `VM(s)`) is a reasonable adaptation to support scalability NFRs, but it is a slight deviation from a literal interpretation of the constraint.

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The architectural design decisions are highly rational and well-justified by the requirements. The modular separation of concerns (frontend, backend, scheduler, distinct algorithm workers) supports maintainability and independent scaling. The use of Nginx for load balancing and boundary isolation (NFR-020, NFR-025), RabbitMQ for asynchronous task processing and reliable messaging (NFR-012 to NFR-015, C-014), and separate data stores (Metadata Store for structured data, HDFS for large files) are all sound choices that directly address performance, scalability, reliability, and data management requirements. The decomposition of the algorithm service into specialized workers is a rational approach to manage distinct algorithmic tasks (FR-012 to FR-015). The inferences made (e.g., specific role of etcd, generic database for metadata) are standard practices in distributed systems and enhance the architecture's robustness and clarity.

traceability.reasoning: Traceability is exceptionally strong. The diagram makes excellent use of notes to explicitly link architectural components, their responsibilities, and design decisions back to specific Functional Requirements (FRs), Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs), Data Requirements (DRs), and Constraints (C-s). This clear annotation allows for easy verification of requirement coverage and understanding of the rationale behind each architectural element. The legend also effectively traces communication protocols.

readability.reasoning: The diagram is highly readable and well-organized. It uses clear hierarchical structuring with logical zones (External, DMZ, Internal) and distinct component types (nodes for VMs, components for software services). The `left to right direction` and `skinparam` settings enhance visual clarity. Labels for components and connections are precise and informative. The concise notes provide valuable context and traceability without cluttering the main diagram, making it easy to understand the system's structure and its relationship to the requirements.

samples.title: ModelSharePlatform / mini-swe-agent/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
The goal is to provide structured input for architecture generation, test design, and requirements tracing for the Deep Learning Model Sharing Platform.

### Product Scope

The Deep Learning Model Sharing Platform targets deep learning enthusiasts and professionals, providing a one-stop cloud service for communication, learning, creation, and application of deep learning models. The platform supports a Q&A community, blog sharing, model library management, dataset management, online invocation experiences, paid model sales revenue, follow relationships, and administrator governance.

This document uses the specification terminology for general users, administrators, Q&A community, blogs, models, datasets, online invocation, paid sales, SQL, MySQL, TypeScript, UI, Spring Boot, and Vue.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for platform general users, administrators, developers, testers, requirements reviewers, and later architecture generation and acceptance testing workflows. General users are the primary platform users, and administrators manage platform-related data.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Metric / Acceptance | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Security | The system shall use multiple security mechanisms to prevent malicious operations from affecting the platform. | Malicious operations cannot damage core functions or data. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Security | The system shall prevent common injection attacks. | Common injection attacks are blocked. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Security | The system shall securely authenticate users and allow administrators to restrict functions for violating users. | After authentication, administrators can delete illegal information or cancel violating user accounts. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Consistency | The system shall keep server data and front-end data consistent. | Server data and front-end data are consistent within a 3-second time interval. | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | Usability | The system interface shall follow modern aesthetics, maintain a consistent style, and use a minimalist theme. | Main interfaces are stylistically consistent and match the minimalist theme. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-006 | Usability | System operation entries shall be obvious, and access cost shall match usage frequency. | High-frequency operation entries are easier to access; when there are too many interfaces, classification aggregation or quick search is supported. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-007 | Reliability | The system shall meet a long-running target when the hardware and software runtime environments remain unchanged. | Expected time to failure is greater than 1 year. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-008 | Availability | System maintenance windows shall be scheduled during low-impact periods. | 1:00 to 4:00 may be used as the system maintenance time. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-009 | Capacity | The system shall support the required scale of user data storage. | Stores data for more than 10,000 users. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-010 | Performance | The system shall support application request throughput. | Supports 1,000 application requests per second. | High | Test |
| NFR-011 | Recoverability | The system shall be repaired promptly when it crashes because the data load exceeds the processing limit. | Repair is completed within 1 hour. | High | Demonstration |
| NFR-012 | Internationalization | The system interface shall support switching between Chinese and English. | Users can switch between Chinese and English interfaces. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-013 | Maintainability | The system shall have stability and partial self-troubleshooting ability without manual intervention. | Faults can be automatically identified or partially handled by the system. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-014 | Extensibility | The system shall provide extensible interfaces through object orientation, abstraction, and modular code. | Later development can extend business requirements through interfaces. | Medium | Analysis |
| NFR-015 | Recommendation Quality | The system shall add intelligent recommendation algorithms on top of traditional recommendation algorithms. | Recommended model content better matches users' personalized needs. | Medium | Test |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Reference technologies include MySQL, Spring Boot, Vue, TypeScript, SQL, and UI-related technologies. |
| C-002 | Runtime hardware CPU shall use x86-64 or ARM architecture. |
| C-003 | The runtime environment shall have at least 4 GB of memory. |
| C-004 | The runtime environment shall have at least 30 GB of available disk space. |
| C-005 | The runtime environment shall be all modern browsers. |
| C-006 | The system shall not support Internet Explorer. |
| C-007 | The development deadline is limited to completion in early May. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| CPU | x86-64 or ARM architecture. |
| Memory | At least 4 GB. |
| Disk | At least 30 GB of available space. |
| Browser | All modern browsers. |
| Unsupported Browser | Internet Explorer is not supported. |

### Software/API Interfaces

The specification lists MySQL, Spring Boot, Vue, TypeScript, SQL, UI-related terms, and reference materials, but does not explicitly define API paths, interface protocols, database schemas, upload/download protocols, model online invocation protocols, or payment interfaces.

### Communication Interfaces

The specification does not explicitly define HTTP, HTTPS, WebSocket, RPC, message queues, or third-party payment communication interfaces. It can only be determined that the system runs in modern browsers and does not support Internet Explorer.

### Assumptions and Dependencies

| ID | Assumption / Dependency |
| --- | --- |
| AD-001 | Core capabilities such as models, datasets, Q&A, blogs, follows, and purchases depend on the general user being logged in. |
| AD-002 | Administrator governance capabilities depend on the user being logged in as an administrator. |
| AD-003 | Model recommendation depends on user browsing history and registration information. |
| AD-004 | Dataset download, deletion, and similar operations depend on corresponding dataset information existing in the database. |
| AD-005 | The specification does not define the model review process, payment channel, API schema, or database table structure; later design must not assume these details are complete. |

' --- Configuration & Styles ---

<style>
    .client {
        BackgroundColor #e1f5fe
        LineColor #01579b
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .service {
        BackgroundColor #e8f5e9
        LineColor #2e7d32
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .db {
        BackgroundColor #fff9c4
        LineColor #fbc02d
        LineThickness 2
    }
</style>

' --- Client Layer ---
package "Browser / Client Layer" as Browser_Layer {
    rectangle "Frontend Interface\n(Bootstrap-Vue)" as FE_Interface <<client>>
}

' --- Server Layer ---
package "Server Layer\n(Spring Boot)" as Server_Layer {
    
    package "Core Modules" as Core_Modules {
        ' Layout is TB within the package
        rectangle "Account Management Module" as AccountMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Q&A Community Module" as QAMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Blog Sharing Module" as BlogMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Model Management Module" as ModelMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Dataset Management Module" as DatasetMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Following Management Module" as FollowMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Admin Module" as AdminMod <<service>>
    }
}

' --- Database Layer ---
package "Database Layer\n(MySQL)" as Database_Layer {
    database "User Data" as UserDB <<db>>
    database "Q&A Community Data" as QADB <<db>>
    database "Blog Data" as BlogDB <<db>>
    database "Model Data" as ModelDB <<db>>
    database "Dataset Data" as DatasetDB <<db>>
    database "Following Data" as FollowDB <<db>>
}

' --- Relationships ---
FE_Interface --> Core_Modules
Core_Modules --> Database_Layer

@enduml

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose
This Software Requirements Specification defines normalized requirements for the Activiti 7 developer-guide examples and the platform behaviors they demonstrate, including authentication, process runtime interaction, task runtime authorization, audit observation, cloud deployment access, and scalability configuration.

### 1.2 Scope
This SRS describes Activiti platform behaviors, example processes, and deployment patterns documented or demonstrated by the developer guide. These requirements are not software obligations implemented by the guide content itself.

The SRS covers:
- authentication token acquisition and use for user endpoint access,
- process definition lookup and `SimpleProcess` instance start behavior,
- audit observation for a just-started process instance,
- `TaskRuntime` access control through a logged-in user with the required role,
- a complete example that combines `ProcessRuntime` and `TaskRuntime` APIs with a human participant,
- Kubernetes/GKE ingress-based external access,
- deployment descriptor parameters for scalability experiments,
- architecture background needed for downstream architecture view generation.

The SRS does not define:
- a graphical user interface,
- a quantified performance target for cloud connector transaction handling,
- an ongoing build-artifact version obligation for the developer guide itself,
- guaranteed executability of the full example beyond the documented example/module presence.

### 1.3 Intended Audience
- Developers following Activiti 7 examples.
- Integrators invoking runtime, task, audit, or query APIs.
- Operators deploying example services to Kubernetes or GKE.
- Architecture-generation tools that need normalized requirements without provenance metadata.

### 1.4 Definitions and Abbreviations
| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Activiti Cloud | Activiti cloud-oriented runtime and service environment used by the examples. |
| Example Runtime Bundle | Runtime bundle that hosts deployed process definitions and process instances in the examples. |
| Keycloak | Identity provider used to obtain authentication tokens. |
| `ProcessRuntime` | API used for process runtime interaction. |
| `TaskRuntime` | API used for task interaction and requiring a logged-in user with the proper role. |
| `ACTIVITI_USER` | Required user role for interacting with the `TaskRuntime` API. |
| `ROLE_ACTIVITI_USER` | Granted authority form of the `ACTIVITI_USER` role. |
| Ingress Controller | Kubernetes component that routes external traffic to selected internal services. |
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Quality attribute | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Authentication tokens used for protected endpoint access shall be time-limited and shall become invalid after their validity period or invalidation event. | Security | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | The example deployment shall support scalability experimentation by allowing data input rate and cloud component replica counts to be configured through deployment descriptors. | Scalability | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-003 | Protected endpoint and task API access shall preserve role-based authorization boundaries through token authentication and logged-in user context. | Security | High | Test |

No measurable latency, throughput, or performance-improvement threshold is specified for cloud connector transaction handling in this SRS.

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint | Type |
|---|---|---|
| C-001 | Security, user identity, groups, and roles shall rely on Spring Security modules within a Spring Boot application context. | Technology |
| C-002 | `TaskRuntime` access shall require the `ACTIVITI_USER` role and its `ROLE_ACTIVITI_USER` granted-authority form. | Access control |
| C-003 | External access to selected Kubernetes/GKE services shall depend on NGINX Ingress Controller configuration and public IP availability. | Deployment/network |
| C-004 | For Activiti 7.0.0-EA-201802 compatibility context, Activiti project artifacts are aligned with Spring Boot `2.0.0.RELEASE` and Spring Cloud `Finchley.M8`; this shall not be treated as an ongoing build constraint on the developer guide itself. | Compatibility |
| C-005 | The developer guide content shall not be treated as the runtime implementation of Activiti, Keycloak, Spring Security, or Kubernetes behavior. | Scope |
| C-006 | Architecture background components shall not be expanded into mandatory functional behavior unless a functional requirement assigns behavior to them. | Architecture scope |

### 2.4 Operating Environment
| Environment aspect | Requirement context |
|---|---|
| Spring Boot application | Provides the application context for security and user setup in the examples. |
| Spring Security | Provides user, group, role, and logged-in user context handling. |
| Keycloak | Issues authentication tokens for subsequent user endpoint requests. |
| Activiti Runtime Bundle | Hosts process definitions and started process instances. |
| Audit Service | Displays audit events for the just-started process instance. |
| Query Service | Provides process execution information as read-oriented process state. |
| Kubernetes / GKE | Hosts cloud-oriented services in the deployment examples. |
| NGINX Ingress Controller | Routes external requests from a public IP to selected internal services. |
| Deployment descriptors | Hold configurable data input rate and cloud component replica counts. |

### 3.3 Communication Interfaces
| ID | Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|---|
| CI-001 | REST authorization | REST authorization shall establish the currently logged-in user context for protected endpoint interaction. |
| CI-002 | Ingress routing | NGINX ingress shall create routes from external access points to selected internal Kubernetes services. |
| CI-003 | Token-based authentication | User endpoint requests shall include a valid token when protected endpoints are accessed. |

### 2.6 Assumptions and Dependencies
| ID | Assumption or dependency |
|---|---|
| AD-001 | Authentication tokens are time-limited and may need to be reacquired after expiration or invalidation. |
| AD-002 | User endpoint access depends on obtaining and supplying a valid Keycloak token. |
| AD-003 | `TaskRuntime` interaction depends on the current logged-in user context and the `ACTIVITI_USER` / `ROLE_ACTIVITI_USER` role. |
| AD-004 | External interaction with selected cluster services depends on ingress configuration and public IP availability. |
| AD-005 | Scalability experiments depend on deployment descriptor parameters for input rate and replica count. |
| AD-006 | Version alignment for Activiti project artifacts is a point-in-time compatibility context and not an ongoing build obligation for the developer guide itself. |

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #5B8F35
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #111111
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #D8EAFB
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

rectangle "Client" as Client
rectangle "SSO/IDM\n(Keycloak)" as Keycloak

package "Activiti Cloud Infrastructure" as CloudInfrastructure {
    rectangle "Gateway" as Gateway
    rectangle "Registry\n(Kubernetes)" as Registry
    rectangle "Config Maps\n(Kubernetes)" as ConfigMaps

    package "Activiti Cloud Application" as CloudApplication {
        rectangle "Query\nService" as QueryService
        rectangle "Audit\nService" as AuditService
        rectangle "Notification\nService" as NotificationService

        package "Cloud Connectors" as CloudConnectors {
            rectangle "Customer\nLogic" as CustomerLogic
            rectangle "Out of the\nBox Services" as OutOfBoxServices
        }

        package "Runtime Bundle" as RuntimeBundle {
            rectangle "Process\nRuntime" as ProcessRuntime
            rectangle "BPMN, Form\nand Rule Defs" as BpmnFormRuleDefs
        }
    }
}

Client --> Gateway : WebFlow
Keycloak --> Client : Direct Access Grant
Gateway --> Keycloak : WebFlow
Gateway --> CloudApplication : routed service access
Gateway --> Registry : service discovery
Registry --> ConfigMaps : configuration lookup
ConfigMaps --> CloudConnectors : Kubernetes configuration
QueryService --> RuntimeBundle : process queries
AuditService --> RuntimeBundle : audit events
NotificationService --> RuntimeBundle : notifications
CloudConnectors --> RuntimeBundle : business integration
ProcessRuntime --> BpmnFormRuleDefs : executes definitions
RuntimeBundle --> Keycloak : identity and token validation
@enduml

completeness.reasoning: The diagram includes most components specified in the requirements, such as the Runtime Bundle, Audit/Query services, Keycloak, and NGINX Ingress. It also represents high-level requirements like scalability configuration (NFR-002) and ingress-based access (FR-007). However, it is incomplete because it fails to model the user-facing interaction with the Query Service (API-006) and does not clearly depict the initial token acquisition flow by a user (API-001).

faithfulness.reasoning: The diagram is fundamentally unfaithful in its representation of security-related control flows. It depicts API services (e.g., `ProcessRuntime API`) calling an `Authentication Gateway` after being invoked, and shows `Spring Security` (a framework) as an active caller of the APIs. This misrepresents the specified roles of token validation (FR-001), authorization (FR-005, C-002), and the Spring Security context (C-001), where security checks are preconditions for access, not subsequent actions.

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The architecture is irrational. While the layered decomposition is a logical starting point, the depicted interactions violate fundamental design principles. The security model is unsound, with services invoking authentication post-call rather than being protected by a gateway pre-call. Representing the `Spring Security` framework as an external actor that calls APIs demonstrates a critical misunderstanding of its role as an embedded library that provides context and enforces rules within the service runtime.

traceability.reasoning: A reasonable number of requirements can be traced to elements in the diagram. For example, FR-007 traces to the `NGINX Ingress Controller`, and NFR-002 traces to `Deployment Descriptors`. However, traceability is weak for critical behaviors. Requirements like FR-005 (`TaskRuntime` authorization) are linked to a confusing and incorrect interaction with `Spring Security`, making the trace misleading and failing to demonstrate how the architecture actually satisfies the requirement.

readability.reasoning: The diagram's use of layers and packages provides a basic organizational structure. However, readability is significantly hindered by non-standard and illogical flow representations. The tangled arrows in the security layer, the backward flow from `Spring Security` to the APIs, and unclear distinction in the roles of `API Gateway` and `Authentication Gateway` make it difficult to understand the system's actual behavior.

samples.title: AwesomeMusic / openhands/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
Its purpose is to provide structured input for architecture generation, test design, and requirements traceability for an AI music creation and sharing platform.

### Product Scope

The AI music creation and sharing platform is a Web system for music enthusiasts. After registration, users can use the platform for AI music creation, score generation, score upload and sharing, favorites, follows, recommendations, search, hot charts, and downloads. The system contains three modules: Web frontend, Web backend, and algorithm service. The algorithm service generates music from user-provided source files and includes online music generation and offline model iterative upgrade services.

This document uses the specification definitions of MQ, score, track seed, and midi music. A score is an abc-format plain-text file that can generate midi music. A track seed is the beginning part of an abc score and is less than 10 seconds long. Midi music is a music file playable by midi-capable players.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for customers, product managers, developers, testers, and downstream architecture generation and requirements review workflows. Target end users are music enthusiasts, especially users interested in AI music creation, music sharing, and novel approaches to music creation.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Performance | Average account registration latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Performance | Average login latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Performance | Average score favorite latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Performance | Average follow-user latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | Performance | Average score upload latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 3000ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Performance | Average model favorite latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-007 | Performance | Average Web-side score generation latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-008 | Performance | Average score download latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 3000ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-009 | Performance | Average recommendation retrieval latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 500ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | Performance | Average score search latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 1000ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-011 | Performance | Average hot-chart viewing latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-012 | Performance | Algorithm training for generation models shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-013 | Performance | Algorithm score generation shall satisfy throughput and latency requirements. | Throughput 5ops, average latency <= 1min. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Performance | Classifying all scores shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-015 | Performance | Song recommendation shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-016 | Scalability | The Web system shall support the preset concurrent access throughput. | Concurrent access throughput is no less than 2000QPS. | High | Test |
| NFR-017 | Scalability | The Web system shall support transaction change throughput. | Transaction changes are no less than 100TPS. | High | Test |
| NFR-018 | Usability | System interactions shall be suitable for music production beginners. | Interactions are simple and understandable and do not require professional function design. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-019 | Usability | The system shall provide user instructions, prompts, and guidance. | Comprehensive instructions, prompts, and guidance exist to help users get started quickly. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-020 | Security | The gateway shall isolate internal and external network boundaries. | Internal systems are not exposed externally due to loose security configuration. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-021 | Reliability | Important data shall be stored in multiple replicas. | Important data is stored in 3 replicas. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-022 | Availability | The system shall remain available under partial failures. | The system remains usable when some internal nodes are upgraded, crash, or experience a certain level of network partition. | High | Test |
| NFR-023 | Maintainability | Code shall be readable. | Code follows naming conventions, and names correspond to actual meaning. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-024 | Maintainability | Code shall avoid excessive coupling. | Code supports later iterative optimization, extension of existing functions, and addition of new functions. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-025 | Load Balancing | The system shall maintain stable response and reasonable resource utilization under high access volume. | Load balancing technology is provided when user access volume is high. | High | Test |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Frontend languages are HTML, CSS, and JavaScript; the frontend framework is Bootstrap. |
| C-002 | Backend language is Golang; backend framework is Gin. |
| C-003 | Webpage response time shall be within 1 second. |
| C-004 | Music generation model training time shall be within 2 hours. |
| C-005 | The algorithm model shall be able to generate enough varieties of music styles. |
| C-006 | Generated music quality shall be above the baseline. |
| C-007 | Algorithm model response time shall be controlled within 1 minute. |
| C-008 | Product interfaces shall reflect user friendliness and have a certain degree of aesthetics. |
| C-009 | Project R&D shall strictly follow the project management process to avoid phase delays affecting later development. |
| C-010 | System hardware environment consists of 4 virtual machines, each with CPU 4 * 2400MHz, 8GB memory, and 200GB storage. |
| C-011 | System software environment includes Ubuntu 18.04 LTS, Hadoop 3.2.2, RabbitMQ 3.8.14, etcd 3.4.15, and Nginx 1.19.8. |
| C-012 | Communication protocols are HTTPS and TCP/IP. |
| C-013 | Task metadata must conform to database constraints and limit task size. |
| C-014 | When task submission fails, erroneous data needs to be automatically skipped and retried. |
| C-015 | All requirements listed in use-case diagrams are planned for the current version and do not need to be assigned to future versions. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Hardware | 4 virtual machines, each with CPU 4 * 2400MHz, 8GB memory, and 200GB storage. |
| Operating system | Ubuntu 18.04 LTS. |
| Software interfaces | Apache Hadoop 3.2.2, RabbitMQ 3.8.14, etcd 3.4.15, and Nginx 1.19.8. |
| Communication protocols | HTTPS and TCP/IP. |
| Frontend technology |
...[truncated]

Separating relational metadata into a database and large files (MIDI/Scores) into Hadoop HDFS is standard and robust.

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS defines the software requirements for sample `s000050_275af3e0`, covering the repository’s supported fuzzing workflows, interfaces, data, constraints, and verification criteria.

### Product Scope
hAFL2 is a kAFL-based hypervisor fuzzing system. The primary supported scope includes fuzzing of the Hyper-V virtualization stack, especially the Hyper-V virtual switch (`vmswitch.sys`) path. The currently specified repository capabilities include:
- A standalone entry point for running `kAFL-Fuzzer` test suites
- A Linux userspace fuzzing workflow that packages target binaries into an `initrd`
- An embedded forkserver that maps kAFL hypercalls to AFL-style guest I/O
- Windows helper binaries used in the Hyper-V fuzzing workflow
- A harness-driver and crash-monitoring workflow used with child and root partition virtual machines

### Intended Audience
- Fuzzing operators using hAFL2 and kAFL workflows
- Developers maintaining test and launch workflows
- Integrators preparing Linux userspace or Windows Hyper-V fuzzing environments

### References
- Repository: `SafeBreach-Labs/hAFL2`
- Architecture documentation: `Architecture.png`
- Tutorial and workflow documentation: `tutorial.md`
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality Attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Verifiability | The system shall support execution of regular `kAFL-Fuzzer` tests via `pytest` from the `kAFL-Fuzzer/` directory and via a standalone script entry point. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Data integrity | Deterministic mutation routines shall restore the original payload contents on exit, even when mutating the payload directly during processing. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Mutation correctness | Deterministic bitflip mutators shall produce outputs whose Hamming-distance change matches the mutator’s expected flipped-bit count under test. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Interoperability | The Windows fuzzing workflow shall operate across child partition and root partition virtual-machine roles. | High | Demonstration |
| NFR-005 | Configurability | The Windows workflow shall allow Windows-build-specific offset configuration when required for the target build. | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint | Type |
|---|---|---|
| C-001 | Use or redistribution shall comply with the repository-level BSD 3-Clause license and any applicable subfile license notices, including AGPL-3.0-or-later headers present in kAFL-derived files. | License |
| C-002 | Windows fuzzing binaries must be compiled by running `bash ./hAFL2/targets/windows_86_64/compile.sh`. | Build/Tooling |
| C-003 | The Windows harness and crash-monitoring drivers must be compiled with Visual Studio. | Build/Tooling |
| C-004 | For Windows builds different from the documented one, the operator must obtain the required offset and update the corresponding source value before compilation. | Platform compatibility |
| C-005 | Child Partition DSE must be disabled from an elevated command prompt, and the child partition VM must be restarted afterward. | Operational |
| C-006 | Child partition VM configuration steps require the child partition VM to be turned off before configuration begins. | Operational |

### Operating Environment
- Python 3 for the standalone test entry point
- `pytest` for regular test execution
- `bash` for build and launch-related scripts
- Linux environments for userspace target packaging and launch workflows
- Windows environments for helper binaries, harness, crash monitoring, and VM-based fuzzing setup
- Root partition and child partition virtual machines for the Hyper-V fuzzing workflow

### Software/API Interfaces
- The Linux userspace fuzzing workflow shall interface with guest execution through an embedded forkserver that maps kAFL hypercalls to AFL-style I/O.
- The Windows fuzzing workflow shall provide helper binaries that interact with the harness and snapshot-loading flow.
- The Windows Hyper-V fuzzing workflow shall support harness, crash-monitoring, and VM-based integration across child and root partitions.

### Communication Interfaces
- The Windows workflow shall support packet-sending through an IOCTL triggered by `packet_sender.exe`.
- The Windows Hyper-V fuzzing workflow shall support sending fuzzing payloads from the child partition harness to `VMSwitch` in the root partition.
- This communication path shall depend on netvsc or VMBus-related network channels.
- The crash-monitoring workflow shall communicate crash results back to hAFL2.

### Assumptions and Dependencies
- Linux userspace fuzzing assumes the target binary can be packaged into an `initrd`
- Shared libraries required by the target are available on the host for copying into the `initrd`
- Windows setup depends on compiling helper binaries and drivers and on Windows-build-specific offset configuration
- Hyper-V fuzzing depends on correct child partition and root partition VM setup
- The documented architecture and workflow assume VMBus or netvsc-related channels are available for the root-partition communication path

This provides a comprehensive overview of the system's structure.

The introduction of an abstract `Hypercall/IOCTL Translation` component is also applied incorrectly to the Windows harness, further obscuring the true interactions.

samples.title: easylatex / mini-swe-agent/gpt-5

project_context.introduction:
### Product scope
EasyLaTex is an online system for converting mathematical formulas, tables, images, CSV/Excel/PDF files, and handwritten inputs into LaTex code. The system also provides real-time rendering, project/workspace management, collaboration, version control, comments, and export capabilities.

### Intended audience
- Product managers and requirements reviewers validating the EasyLaTex requirement set.
- Developers implementing user management, file processing, AI recognition, rendering, collaboration, and version control modules.
- Test engineers deriving verification scenarios from functional, interface, performance, compatibility, and security requirements.
- Architecture and evaluation agents that need structured requirements for architecture view generation.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Quality attribute | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | For common handwritten formulas, standard formula images, and regular CSV/Excel/PDF files, LaTex conversion accuracy shall be at least 88%. | Accuracy | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | The LaTex rendering module shall keep rendering latency below 2 seconds. | Performance | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Each user operation such as page navigation or loading shall respond within 2 seconds. | Performance | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | The system shall concurrently process at least 100 user requests. | Scalability | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | The system shall support normal access through Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari on PC and mobile browsers. | Compatibility / portability | High | Demonstration |
| NFR-006 | File input compatibility shall include at least JPG, PNG, PDF, CSV, and Excel `.xlsx`. | Compatibility | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Generated LaTex results shall be compatible with mainstream LaTex editors and compilation environments such as Overleaf and TeXstudio. | Compatibility | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-008 | User information including email, username, and password shall be encrypted during storage and transmission. | Security / privacy | High | Inspection |
| NFR-009 | Project collaboration shall enforce permission control with at least project owner and collaborator roles. | Security / access control | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | The system shall provide defenses against common Web vulnerabilities including SQL injection, XSS, and CSRF. | Security | High | Security test |
| NFR-011 | The user interface shall be concise and consistent with user operation habits so users can quickly get started. | Usability | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-012 | The system shall use modular design, standardized API design, complete interface documentation, unit tests, integration tests, and deployment documentation to support maintainability and iteration. | Maintainability | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Users are assumed to use modern browsers that support WebSocket technology. |
| C-002 | The AI model is assumed to reach at least 90% image recognition accuracy during design and development. |
| C-003 | Upload and download workflows depend on stable user network connections. |
| C-004 | Identity security depends on the existing user authentication mechanism. |
| C-005 | Minimum client hardware includes Intel Core i3 or equivalent CPU, 4 GB RAM, 20 GB free disk space, integrated graphics, and a 1024x768 display. |
| C-006 | Recommended client hardware includes Intel Core i5 or higher CPU, 8 GB RAM or more, 50 GB free disk space with SSD recommended, graphics acceleration, and 1920x1080 or higher display. |
| C-007 | Supported operating systems include Windows 10 or higher, macOS 10.14 or higher, and Ubuntu 18.04 or higher. |
| C-008 | Supported database management systems include MySQL 8.0 or higher, PostgreSQL 12 or higher, or another compatible relational database. |
| C-009 | Supported web servers include Apache HTTP Server 2.4 or higher and Nginx 1.18 or higher. |
| C-010 | Development/runtime dependencies include Python 3.8 or higher, Node.js 14.x or higher, and TeX Live, MiKTeX, or another compatible LaTex engine. |
| C-011 | External application interfaces use HTTP/REST API. |
| C-012 | The system shall follow privacy-protection regulations such as GDPR and other data-protection regulations. |

### Operating environment
The system is intended to run through modern browsers that support WebSocket. Target clients include Windows, macOS, and Linux environments, with Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge. Server-side and development dependencies include relational databases, web servers, Python, Node.js, and a compatible LaTex engine.

### Software/API interfaces
The system shall expose HTTP/REST APIs for user registration, login, user information management, project management, file upload, download, collaboration sharing, real-time preview, version management, and comments. It shall also provide internal APIs for user verification, file storage, image recognition, LaTex generation, rendering, collaboration management, and version control.

### Communication interfaces
The specification explicitly identifies HTTP/REST API for external module interactions. It also assumes WebSocket-capable browsers, supporting web interaction scenarios where real-time preview or collaborative updates may require browser communication support.

### Assumptions and dependencies
- Users operate through modern WebSocket-capable browsers.
- AI model image recognition accuracy is expected to reach at least 90%.
- User network connections are stable enough for uploads and downloads.
- Identity security depends on the existing user authentication mechanism.
- Initial user scale and storage demand are relatively small.

It includes all major functional modules such as Authentication, File Ingestion, Recognition (OCR+DL), LaTeX Generation, Rendering, Collaboration/Version Control, and Exporting. It also successfully addresses data storage and external dependency requirements.

samples.title: retool / metagpt-custom/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### Product Scope

ReTool is a requirements management tool for continuously evolving software projects. It supports user management, project management, and requirements management. Requirements management includes requirement itemization, requirement structuring, requirement conflict detection, common requirement identification, requirement association analysis, and forward tracing from requirements to design modules and test cases. The system adopts a front-end/back-end separated mode; the client uses Vue.js and the server uses a microservice architecture.

This document follows the terminology definitions in the requirements specification for microservices, Flask, NLP, PyTorch, Vue.js, and MongoDB. The specification does not provide REST paths, database collection structures, or message protocol details.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for ReTool developers, testers, project managers, project leaders, ordinary project members, system administrators, requirements reviewers, and later architecture generation and acceptance testing workflows.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Performance | The time from user click to first interface display shall be controlled. | Time from click to first interface display does not exceed 1 second. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Reliability | The system mean time to failure shall satisfy the threshold. | Mean time to failure > 720 h. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-003 | Reliability | The system mean time to repair shall satisfy the threshold. | Mean time to repair < 30 min. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-004 | Reliability | The data loss rate after system failure shall conform to the specification threshold. | Data loss rate after failure < 99%; this threshold is preserved according to the original source wording and should be confirmed during review. | Medium | Analysis |
| NFR-005 | Reliability | The system shall have automatic release and fault removal capability after errors occur. | After fault injection, the system can automatically recover or enter a recoverable state. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Reliability | User-uploaded information shall be persisted quickly. | Time for saving uploaded user information to disk < 1 s. | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Resource Usage | System memory usage shall be controlled during runtime. | Memory usage < 80%. | High | Test |
| NFR-008 | Resource Usage | System CPU usage shall be controlled during runtime. | CPU usage < 80%. | High | Test |
| NFR-009 | Reliability | The database shall be backed up periodically and support offsite disaster recovery. | The database is backed up periodically, with three-copy offsite disaster recovery backup. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-010 | Security | The login system shall comply with Web security specifications. | Login Web interfaces pass security specification checks. | High | Test |
| NFR-011 | Privacy | The system shall protect user privacy information and personalized information. | Privacy and personalized data are protected by access control and protection measures. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-012 | Security | The system shall identify illegal user inputs and malicious behavior. | Illegal inputs and malicious behavior are identified and recorded. | High | Test |
| NFR-013 | Security | The system shall prevent requirement input from causing database injection attacks. | Requirement input content is restricted and validated, and injection attacks are blocked. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Security | Front-end code shall reduce the risk that captured request packets lead to requirement information copying or tampering. | Front-end code uses protection strategies such as minimization and mixing/obfuscation. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-015 | Security | The system shall ensure functional module security. | Network transmission, data storage, file storage, and open Web interfaces have security measures. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-016 | Security | The system shall establish permissions for developers and sensitive commands. | Developer and sensitive command permissions can be checked. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-017 | Maintainability | Software structure and documents shall support performance adjustment. | The system has good structure and complete documents, and performance adjustment points can be identified. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-018 | Maintainability | Documents shall be clear, readable, and uniformly written. | Documents pass standardization and readability checks. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-019 | Maintainability | Server deployment and logs shall support issue tracing. | Deployment process is concise and standardized, and logs are persistently saved, readable, and traceable. | High | Test |
| NFR-020 | Maintainability | The product shall reserve upgrade interfaces and upgrade space. | Design descriptions reflect upgrade interfaces and extension space. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-021 | Usability | Interaction design shall reduce user thinking and operation costs. | Users can obtain target information in three clicks or fewer, and important information is presented on the main interface or in obvious locations. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-022 | Usability | The system shall reduce user input and support personalized experience. | Selection lists and search input autocomplete are used, personalized information is recorded, and direct login is supported on revisits. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-023 | UI Design | Interface design shall be unified, adaptive, and compliant with Web design specifications. | Flat style is unified, screen size is adaptive, visual effect is harmonious, icons are concise and intuitive, and colors and text satisfy the specification requirements. | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Requirement changes must strictly follow the requirement change process, and the requirements specification must be reasonably modified and reviewed. |
| C-002 | The overall tool adopts a front-end/back-end separated mode. |
| C-003 | The front end shall use a lightweight, community-supported, and maintainable framework; the product description explicitly states that the client uses Vue.js. |
| C-004 | The server uses a microservice architecture. |
| C-005 | During runtime, the system needs to limit simultaneous access volume; when the peak is exceeded, some user processes are blocked until the current carrying capacity can handle new processes. |
| C-006 | Gateway, user management service, project management service, and requirements management service run on a local computer. |
| C-007 | The algorithm service runs on a server because of machine performance requirements. |
| C-008 | When users cannot connect to the network, all requirements cannot be realized. |
| C-009 | Client hardware shall satisfy Intel Core i7-6700HQ CPU, more than 100 MB available storage, complete network support, and at least 8 GB memory. |
| C-0
...[truncated]

completeness.reasoning: The architecture diagram successfully represents a vast majority of the Architecturally Significant Requirements (ASRs). It correctly models the front-end/back-end separation (C-002), the specific microservices named in the SRS (Gateway, User, Project, Requirements, Algorithm), the prescribed technologies (Vue.js, MongoDB, NLP/PyTorch), and the critical deployment split between a local computer and a remote server (C-006, C-007). Key data flows, like Word document processing (FR-027), are also depicted. The diagram falls slightly short of perfect completeness by not explicitly representing mechanisms for some non-functional requirements like database backup/reliability (NFR-009) or system logging (NFR-019), but its coverage of the core structural ASRs is comprehensive.

faithfulness.reasoning: The diagram is highly faithful to the requirements specification. Every component and relationship shown directly corresponds to or is a logical implementation of a stated requirement. For instance, the 'Local Computer Deployment' and 'Server Deployment' rectangles perfectly match the constraints C-006 and C-007. samples.title: mytorch / direct/gpt-5

project_context.introduction:
### Product Scope

The MyTorch project targets deep learning learners. By continuously improving and extending the MyTorch/NNE framework, it provides framework enhancement capabilities and a visual training/inference system. The system scope includes model configuration, data loading, model training, model inference, GPU acceleration, model weight initialization, classic model introduction, preset datasets, back-end interfaces, front-end interfaces, and local database persistence.

This document follows the terminology definitions in the requirements specification for deep learning, neural networks, data augmentation, data loaders, model structures, loss functions, optimizers, learning rates, weight initialization, GPU acceleration, batches, and model deployment.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for developers, team members, teachers, classmates, project stakeholders, and later architecture generation and test review workflows. The specification particularly emphasizes unifying understanding during development and testing, communicating requirements, and evaluating project progress.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Numerical Precision | System floating-point calculation precision shall meet the specified standard. | All floating-point calculation precision is the same as or higher than `numpy.float32`, with at least 7 decimal significant digits. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Time Precision | System time-related records shall reach second-level precision. | Log time and other time-related precision is accurate to the second. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-003 | UI Response Time | On recommended hardware configuration, the interaction interface shall respond promptly to user instructions. | Maximum response time is less than 1 second, and average response time is less than 0.75 seconds. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Training Engine Response Time | On recommended hardware configuration, the training engine shall respond promptly to training or data-loading instructions. | Maximum response time is less than 3 seconds, and average response time is less than 2.5 seconds. | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | Training Efficiency | System training time shall improve with device performance and GPU acceleration capability. | When processor performance is higher or higher-performance GPU acceleration is used for training, the system shall have correspondingly higher training efficiency. | Medium | Analysis |
| NFR-006 | Device Variability | Under non-recommended hardware configuration, system response time may vary with device performance. | The test report shall explain that response time under non-recommended configuration is related to device performance. | Low | Analysis |
| NFR-007 | UI Intuitiveness | The interface shall be as simple and intuitive as possible to reduce the learning curve for beginners. | Clear icons, symbols, and workflows are used, and functional areas are reasonably laid out. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-008 | Guidance and Help | The interface shall provide step guidance, prompts, examples, and suggestions. | Users can view explanations of components and parameter roles, as well as help needed to build neural networks. | High | Demonstration |
| NFR-009 | Real-Time Feedback | The interface shall provide real-time feedback and detailed visualization. | After parameter changes or component additions, the interface updates immediately; when waiting for framework response, it shows a loading animation or progress bar. | High | Demonstration |
| NFR-010 | UI Consistency | Interface design shall remain consistent and concise. | Colors, fonts, buttons, and icon styles are consistent, reducing visual fatigue. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-011 | Fault Monitoring | The system shall monitor training and inference runtime exceptions. | It monitors exceptions such as gradient explosion and inference memory overflow. | High | Test |
| NFR-012 | Resource Monitoring | The system shall monitor key performance indicators. | It monitors GPU utilization, memory usage, and training speed. | High | Test |
| NFR-013 | Fault Handling | The system shall define fault handling strategies. | Training faults such as gradient explosion shall trigger strategies such as automatic training termination. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Recovery | The system shall support model backup and automatic recovery mechanisms. | When hardware failure occurs, model availability can be guaranteed through backup and recovery mechanisms. | High | Test |
| NFR-015 | Error Reporting | The system shall record and report error information and provide troubleshooting suggestions. | Users can understand problems and perform troubleshooting. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-016 | Security and Privacy | The system shall protect user data and models from potential security threats. | Security and privacy protection mechanisms are adopted to prevent potential vulnerabilities or attacks. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-017 | Confidentiality and Integrity | The system shall ensure confidentiality and integrity of user data. | Unauthorized access or tampering shall not compromise user data and models. | High | Test |
| NFR-018 | Privacy Compliance | The system shall comply with relevant privacy regulations and standards. | Privacy protection design conforms to applicable regulations and standards. | High | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | The project enhances the existing NNE framework code. |
| C-002 | Project development and testing shall be performed on computer devices that support deep learning tasks and include at least one computer with a GPU. |
| C-003 | The project uses publicly available datasets or datasets satisfying course requirements for development and testing. |
| C-004 | Project development progress and delivery are constrained by the course semester end date. |
| C-005 | Developers shall have basic deep learning, programming, teamwork, and communication skills. |
| C-006 | The supporting software environment is Python 3.8.x or above with corresponding libraries. |
| C-007 | Concrete library versions and configuration shall be listed in `requirements.txt`. |
| C-008 | The database is SQLite and is deployed in the user's local storage space with the system. |
| C-009 | The system shall automatically back up the SQLite database and support automatic recovery on exception and manual recovery by users. |
| C-010 | All floating-point computation precision must be no lower than `numpy.float32`. |
| C-011 | Time precision shall be accurate to the second. |
| C-012 | System security design shall comply with relevant privacy regulations and standards. |
| C-013 | This document references GB/T 8567-1988 Computer Software Requirements Specification. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Development and test device | Computer devices that support deep learning tasks, including at least one computer with a GPU
...[truncated]

samples.title: SmartRecipe / mini-swe-agent/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
Its purpose is to provide structured input for architecture generation, test design, and requirements traceability for a deep-learning-based smart recipe system.

### Product Scope

The Smart Recipe System is a deep-learning-based photo recipe generation app. It allows users to recognize food by taking photos, generate ingredient lists and cooking steps, and obtain calorie judgment, food-combination taboo checks, nutrition analysis, diet plans, daily check-ins, community sharing, and personalized recommendations. The specification states that the system is an Android application, while the non-functional requirements require the foreground interface to be usable on common mobile terminals such as Android and iOS devices.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for general users, health-conscious users, food-making users, food critics, catering companies, system administrators, developers, testers, and downstream architecture generation workflows. Administrators are responsible for daily operations and maintenance, upgrades, fault handling, user management, recipe database management, user feedback handling, and review of user-published data.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Metric / Acceptance | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Usability | The system shall be simple and easy to use. | Users can conveniently upload photos and obtain information. | High | Demonstration |
| NFR-002 | Usability | The system interface shall be concise, clear, and smooth to operate. | Key process interfaces are clear, and operations have no obvious blocking. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-003 | Performance | The system shall quickly recognize food and generate recipes. | Photo recognition and recipe generation support a smooth user experience. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Performance | The system shall have high processing efficiency. | The backend uses Redis caching to improve performance. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-005 | Reliability | Data storage shall be reliable. | Persistent data is not lost after restart following non-hardware faults. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Security | User personal information and uploaded photos shall be strictly protected. | Personal information and photos must not be leaked to third parties. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-007 | Security | Management system access shall only allow account-password authentication. | Any illegal access shall be rejected. | High | Test |
| NFR-008 | Security / Integrity | The system shall reject improper operations and erroneous data writes. | Improper operations are not executed, and erroneous data is not written. | High | Test |
| NFR-009 | Availability | The system shall be immediately restartable after non-hardware faults. | The system can restart immediately after non-hardware faults. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-010 | Atomicity | Uploading images and uploading recipes shall be atomic operations. | Incomplete recipe entry does not occur. | High | Test |
| NFR-011 | Portability | The backend program shall run on various mainstream operating systems. | The backend can be deployed on mainstream operating systems. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-012 | Portability | The foreground interface program shall be usable on common mobile terminals. | Common mobile terminals such as Android and iOS systems are supported. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-013 | Extensibility | The system shall be modifiable and extensible. | Underlying data representation, backend interfaces, and frontend layout support future business growth and change. | Medium | Analysis |
| NFR-014 | Functional Completeness | The system shall be technically advanced, functionally complete, and highly extensible. | Architecture and module design cover existing functions and preserve room for extension. | Medium | Analysis |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | The system is developed as an Android application. |
| C-002 | The foreground interface program shall be usable on common mobile terminals such as Android and iOS. |
| C-003 | The system includes a mobile app interface and backend services. |
| C-004 | The mobile app and backend services communicate through Ajax interaction and direct interaction. |
| C-005 | The backend service layer includes the business layer, control layer, service layer, and data access layer. |
| C-006 | The backend uses Spring Boot for main business logic. |
| C-007 | The backend uses Flask to build the model inference service. |
| C-008 | The backend uses MySQL to store structured data. |
| C-009 | The backend uses Redis to cache data for improved performance. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Mobile app | The specification states that the system is developed as an Android application. |
| Mobile terminals | The foreground interface program shall be usable on common user mobile terminals, such as Android and iOS systems. |
| Backend OS | The backend program can run on various mainstream operating systems. |
| Backend frameworks | Spring Boot is used for the main business logic, and Flask is used for the model inference service. |
| Data storage | MySQL stores structured data, and Redis is used as a cache. |

### Software/API Interfaces

| Interface | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Ajax / direct backend interaction | The mobile app interface communicates with the backend service layer through Ajax interaction and direct interaction. |
| Spring Boot service | The backend Spring Boot service is responsible for main business logic processing. |
| Flask inference service | Flask is used to build the model inference service. |
| MySQL | MySQL stores structured data such as user data, recipe information, and nutrition data. |
| Redis | Redis caches data to improve performance. |
| Image recognition model | The backend model receives preprocessed image matrix vectors and calculates recipes. |

### Communication Interfaces

The specification states that the mobile app and backend services communicate through Ajax interaction and direct interaction, but does not provide HTTP methods, URL paths, authentication headers, WebSocket, RPC, or third-party interface protocols.

### Assumptions and Dependencies

| ID | Assumption / Dependency |
| --- | --- |
| AD-001 | Login is a precondition for most business use cases, including photo recognition, text search, sharing, recommendation, administrator adding, and administrator review. |
| AD-002 | Photo recognition depends on the backend model inference service and existing recipe data. |
| AD-003 | Health analysis depends on recipe data, nutrition data, and user health data. |
| AD-004 | Intelligent recommendation depends on user history records, query data, follows, and recipe sharing records. |
| AD-005 | Publication of community sharing content depends on administrator review. |
| AD-006 | The specification does not specify model metrics, API schema, image size limits, privacy compliance, or backup strategy. |
