project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS defines repository-backed software requirements for the Udacity Self-Driving Car Engineer Capstone system integration project. The requirements are derived from the project rubric and documented setup workflow and therefore represent evaluation and acceptance goals supported by the repository materials, rather than independently verified claims of implemented performance.

### Product scope
The product is a ROS-based self-driving car system integration project intended to:
- launch in simulator mode and site mode through repository-provided launch entry points,
- operate within the documented Docker and ROS workflow,
- follow provided waypoints in the simulator,
- respect waypoint-defined target speed,
- detect and classify traffic lights on real images during site-mode playback,
- produce stop-related behavior for red or yellow traffic lights through traffic waypoint outputs,
- support real-world testing using a recorded training bag.

### Intended audience
This document is intended for:
- developers maintaining or extending the project,
- evaluators validating rubric-aligned behavior,
- operators running simulator-mode or site-mode demonstrations.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

### NFR-001 Compatibility
The system shall be compatible with the repository-provided launch files `launch/styx.launch` and `launch/site.launch`.

Verification: Inspection  
Acceptance criteria: The documented evaluation entry points are the supported launch interfaces.

### NFR-002 Deployability
The system shall be deployable within the documented Docker-based environment.

Verification: Demonstration  
Acceptance criteria: The documented Docker setup produces a runnable environment.

## 7. System Constraints

### C-001 Launch procedure constraint
Evaluation is constrained to the repository-provided launch files; alternative launch procedures are not assumed.

### C-002 Submission workflow constraint
Evaluation does not accommodate special launch instructions or additional scripts to download files beyond the documented workflow.

### C-003 Environment prerequisite
Docker installation is a prerequisite for environment setup.

### C-004 Runtime workflow constraint
ROS must be launched from within the prepared container workflow.

### C-005 Simulator dependency
Simulator testing depends on the simulator being downloaded, extracted, and made runnable.

### C-006 Real-world test dependency
Real-world testing depends on obtaining and playing back the recorded training bag in site mode.

### C-007 Deliverable size constraint
The submitted deliverable size shall not exceed 2 GB.

### Operating environment
- Docker environment for project build and execution.
- ROS environment inside the container.
- Udacity Capstone Project Simulator for simulator-based execution.
- Site mode with recorded training bag for real-world test playback.

### Communication interfaces
- The system shall support playback-based interaction with a recorded training bag during site-mode testing.

### Assumptions and dependencies
- Docker must be installed before building and running the project container.
- The simulator must be downloaded, extracted, and made runnable before simulator testing.
- Real-world testing depends on availability of the recorded training bag and bag playback tooling.
- Evaluation uses `launch/styx.launch` for simulator testing and `launch/site.launch` for site-mode testing.

    }

        rectangle "DBW Node\nDrive-by-wire" as DbwNode
        rectangle "Waypoint Follower\nController" as WaypointFollower
    }
}

CarSimulator --> ObstacleDetection : /image_color
CarSimulator --> TrafficLightDetection : /image_color
CarSimulator --> ObstacleDetection : /current_pose
CarSimulator --> TrafficLightDetection : /current_pose
CarSimulator --> WaypointUpdater : /current_pose
CarSimulator --> DbwNode : /current_velocity

WaypointLoader --> ObstacleDetection : /base_waypoints
WaypointLoader --> TrafficLightDetection : /base_waypoints

ObstacleDetection --> WaypointUpdater : /obstacle_waypoint
TrafficLightDetection --> WaypointUpdater : /traffic_waypoint

WaypointUpdater --> WaypointFollower : /final_waypoints
WaypointFollower --> DbwNode : /twist_cmd

DbwNode --> CarSimulator : /vehicle/steering_cmd
@enduml

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS defines the externally observable software requirements for the `squatm3gator` API component.

### Product scope
Squatm3gator is a web system for security assessment that generates typosquatting and lookalike domain candidates from a user-supplied domain. Based on an original domain entered by the user, the system generates candidate domain variants that may be used for typosquatting or cybersquatting, including substitution, flipping, homoglyph fast, and homoglyph complete attack types. The system can be used by penetration testers to identify phishing-exercise domains and by security analysts to detect and reduce domain-squatting risk.

The product includes:
- a web entry page,
- session-based submission of domain-generation requests,
- asynchronous job dispatch through Redis-backed communication,
- asynchronous progress or result reporting over WebSocket,
- optional GoDaddy-based domain availability checks using configured credentials and endpoints.

### Intended audience
This document is intended for maintainers, integrators, testers, and operators of the API service and its dependent processing components.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Performance and responsiveness | The system shall asynchronously process domain-generation tasks through the Redis job channel and background consumer thread, decoupling HTTP request submission from actual domain enumeration execution. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | Security and reliability | The system shall ensure result isolation between users by correlating reporting messages with the requesting session key and job ID before emitting them to clients. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Compatibility | When the JSON output option is requested, the system shall generate job options specifying `--output=json`. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-004 | Usability | The system shall return human-readable plain-text error responses for HTTP 400, 404, and 500 errors, and shall also return a parsing-failure message when query string parsing fails. | Medium | Test |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | Redis integration is constrained to the configured Redis endpoint and communication model used by the application. |
| C-002 | GoDaddy integration is constrained to configured API endpoints and `sso-key` authorization using a configured key and secret. |
| C-003 | Domain-generation capability depends on a worker or consumer component that listens to the Redis job channel. In the current implementation, this consumer is started by an in-process background thread in the API process and invokes a `squatm3` subprocess to execute jobs. |
| C-004 | Domain-generation capability depends on the wrapped third-party `squatm3` CLI engine, and options constructed by the API shall remain compatible with command-line parameters supported by that engine. |

### Operating environment
- A server runtime hosting an HTTP application and WebSocket support.
- Redis connectivity at the configured message-queue URI.
- Local availability of the wrapped `squatm3` engine used for domain-generation execution.
- Optional outbound HTTP connectivity to GoDaddy APIs using configured credentials.

### Software interfaces
- The system shall provide HTTP GET `/` to return the main page and create a session.
- The system shall expose at least one GET API resource for retrieving domains generated by the complete homoglyphs attack for a supplied domain value.
- The system shall accept attack-related request inputs including attack codes, `godaddy`, and `output`.
- The system shall emit asynchronous job messages or results through a WebSocket-compatible channel associated with the requesting session.
- The system shall communicate with Redis using a configured Redis URI.
- When optional GoDaddy integration is enabled, the system shall call GoDaddy-related endpoints using configured API key, secret, base URL, and endpoint definitions.

### Communication interfaces
- Redis shall be used for job publication and reporting communication.
- The system shall support asynchronous client updates over WebSocket or Socket.IO-compatible communication.
- GoDaddy HTTP requests shall use `Authorization: sso-key <key>:<secret>` when GoDaddy integration is enabled.

### Assumptions and dependencies
- Redis is reachable through the configured connection URI.
- Session creation and session validation are required for job submission and result correlation.
- The wrapped `squatm3` command-line engine is present and callable by the consumer flow.
- GoDaddy credentials and endpoint configuration are available when GoDaddy-based checking is enabled.

rectangle "Web UI\nindex.html" as WebUi
rectangle "HTTP API\nSession and attack requests" as Api
rectangle "Socket.io\nWebSocket result channel" as SocketIo
queue "Redis\nCommand and reporting queues" as Redis
rectangle "Workers\nCommand consumers" as Workers
rectangle "Squatm3\nDomain generation engine" as Squatm3
cloud "GoDaddy API\nDomain availability" as GoDaddyApi

WebUi --> Api : HTTP requests\ncreate session and queue job
Api --> WebUi : rendered page\nplain-text errors
WebUi <--> SocketIo : progress and results\nsession socket

Api --> Redis : enqueue job\nsession key / job UUID
SocketIo <--> Redis : read reporting queue\nWORKER#JOB#MESSAGE
Redis <--> Workers : command jobs\nworker reports

Workers --> Squatm3 : attack options\nhomoglyph / flip / add / replace
Squatm3 --> Workers : generated domains
Workers --> GoDaddyApi : optional availability check\nsso-key auth
GoDaddyApi --> Workers : availability result
@enduml

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose
This SRS defines the final checked requirements for the Prediction Cards system. It is structured as a clean requirements input for downstream architecture diagram generation.

### 1.2 Product Scope
Prediction Cards is a blockchain-backed prediction market and NFT application. The system includes:
- A frontend application for wallet-connected prediction creation, prediction viewing, outcome NFT fund operations, withdrawal, and reward claiming.
- An on-chain PredictionCards smart contract deployed on the Aeternity blockchain and located through an AENS name.
- An oracle service that registers and maintains an Aeternity oracle, checks pending oracle queries, obtains price-comparison results, and submits oracle responses.
- A prediction-processing service that drives closed predictions into the outcome determination workflow and completes result processing after oracle responses are available.
- Prediction media storage for outcome NFT images or animations.
- A local contract integration-test environment with CORS/proxy support for browser-style contract testing.

### 1.3 Intended Audience
- Product owners and maintainers.
- Frontend and smart-contract developers.
- Oracle-service and prediction-processing-service developers.
- Test engineers and integration engineers.
- Architecture reviewers generating or validating system diagrams.

### 1.4 Terminology
| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Prediction Cards | Product and codebase name; "card" is treated as a product/UI/NFT metaphor, not as a separate multi-prediction domain entity. |
| Prediction | A market-like event with an asset, target price, prediction time range, and two possible outcome NFTs. |
| Outcome NFT | One of the prediction outcome assets, represented by result categories such as `higher` and `lower_or_equal`. |
| Prediction participant | A wallet-connected user who creates, views, funds, rents, withdraws from, or claims rewards related to predictions and outcome NFTs. |
| Oracle service | The service responsible for maintaining the Aeternity oracle and submitting result responses for oracle queries. |
| Prediction-processing service | The service responsible for initiating oracle queries for closed predictions and driving on-chain result processing after oracle responses are available. |
| AENS name | The Aeternity naming mechanism used to locate the deployed contract. |
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality Attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Interoperability | The frontend integration shall operate with an Aeternity wallet client and Aeternity SDK-based contract interaction components. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | Portability | The oracle service shall support both development-mode execution and packaged execution. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-003 | Configurability | The oracle service shall externalize required runtime properties through mandatory runtime configuration. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-004 | Local test web compatibility | The local contract integration-test environment shall support browser cross-origin access with configured methods and headers. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-005 | Scheduled processing availability | The oracle service and prediction-processing service shall perform periodic processing rather than relying only on manual invocation. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-006 | Oracle availability | The system shall maintain oracle availability before oracle registration expires by checking validity and extending it when configured thresholds are reached. | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Time interpretation correctness | The system shall define and verify the timezone interpretation rule for frontend local-time input to avoid prediction time shifts across runtime environments. | Medium | Analysis |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | The oracle service is constrained to a Quarkus-based implementation and runtime model. |
| C-002 | The on-chain prediction logic is constrained by the Aeternity blockchain, the Sophia smart-contract runtime, the AENS contract-location mechanism, and the responsibility split between the oracle service and prediction-processing service. |
| C-003 | Frontend contract interaction is constrained to Aeternity wallet and SDK components. |
| C-004 | Oracle-service operation is constrained by complete runtime configuration before startup. |
| C-005 | Oracle registration, validity thresholds, validity-extension strategy, scheduling periods, oracle fees, node connection, compiler-service connection, network environment, and key configuration are constrained by runtime configuration. |
| C-006 | The local CORS/proxy configuration shall not be treated as a production nginx or production reverse-proxy requirement without additional deployment specification. |
| C-007 | The term "card" shall not be modeled as an independent data entity or multi-prediction container unless a future domain model explicitly defines such an entity. |
| C-008 | Frontend local-time input shall have an explicit timezone interpretation rule before production use. |

### 2.6 Operating Environment
- Browser frontend using React and Aeternity wallet integration.
- Aeternity blockchain runtime for the on-chain PredictionCards contract.
- AENS contract-location mechanism for resolving the deployed contract.
- Quarkus-based oracle service.
- Prediction-processing service for scheduled closed-prediction handling.
- External price provider used by oracle outcome determination.
- Prediction media storage, such as IPFS-compatible storage.
- Local contract integration-test environment with Aeternity node, compiler, index, debug endpoint, and test proxy/CORS configuration.

### 3.2 Software/API Interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Frontend wallet interface | The frontend prediction API shall use a connected wallet as its contract interaction dependency. |
| Frontend context interface | The frontend shall provide prediction operation capabilities to child components through a provider/context mechanism. |
| Frontend-contract interface | The frontend shall submit prediction creation, read prediction state, and perform fund/reward operations through the on-chain contract. |
| AENS contract-location interface | The frontend and services shall locate the deployed on-chain contract through the configured AENS name. |
| Prediction-processing-service contract interface | The prediction-processing service shall initiate oracle queries for closed predictions and complete result processing after oracle responses are available. |
| Oracle-service Aeternity oracle interface | The oracle service shall maintain the Aeternity oracle and submit result responses for pending oracle queries. |
| Oracle-service price-provider interface | The oracle service shall obtain external price information needed for price-comparison outcome determination. |
| Frontend media-storage interface | The frontend shall load or reference prediction outcome NFT media from prediction media storage. |
| Local test proxy interface | The local contract integration-test environment shall expose the configured test services with browser-compatible CORS behavior. |

### 3.3 Communication Interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Wallet-mediated blockchain calls | Frontend contract operations shall be submitted through wallet-mediated Aeternity interactions. |
| Scheduled service processing | Oracle and prediction-processing service behavior shall run periodically according to runtime configuration. |
| Oracle query/response communication | The oracle service shall read pending oracle queries and submit result responses in the expected oracle response format. |
| Media retrieval | Prediction media references shall be retrievable by the frontend from media storage. |
| Local CORS communication | The local integration-test proxy shall allow configured browser cross-origin methods and headers for test flows. |

### 2.7 Assumptions and Dependencies
- Prediction participants have access to a compatible wallet.
- On-chain contract interaction depends on the Aeternity SDK and wallet client.
- The deployed contract is reachable through its AENS name.
- The oracle service has complete runtime configuration before startup.
- The prediction-processing service can discover closed predicti
...[truncated]

@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
top to bottom direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #111111
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam rectangle {
    BorderColor #777777
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam package {
    BorderColor #D45D5D
    BorderThickness 1
}

rectangle "process-prediction-\nservice" as PredictionProcess #BDCCD9
rectangle "oracle-service" as OracleService #FFF1C9

package "aeternity blockchain" as Blockchain #F8CCCC {
    rectangle "PredictionCards\nSmart Contract" as PredictionContract #D8EFD5
}

rectangle "User" as User #FFFFFF
rectangle "Frontend" as Frontend #FFFFFF
rectangle "IPFS" as IPFS #D8E8FA

User --> Frontend

PredictionProcess --> PredictionContract : poll for predictions with state "CLOSED"\ncall function "ask_for_winning_option"\ncall function "process_oracle_response" if oracle has responded

OracleService --> PredictionContract : poll for oracle queries\nget price from coingecko API\ndetermine outcome\nrespond to query with outcome (higher/lower)

Frontend --> PredictionContract : create prediction\ndeposit / rent NFT\nwithdraw / claim rewards
PredictionContract --> Frontend : get data\nvia dry-runs

Frontend --> IPFS : load GIFs of NFTs
@enduml

completeness.reasoning: All major components, actors, and interactions from the SRS are represented in the diagram, including the frontend, wallet, blockchain contract, both backend services (Oracle and Prediction-Processing), external dependencies, and the local test environment. The internal decomposition of components is also thorough and maps to specific functional requirements (e.g., FR-003, FR-005, FR-010).

faithfulness.reasoning: The diagram accurately models the system's structure and data flows as described in the requirements. Key architectural patterns, such as wallet-mediated user transactions (FR-007), the decoupled two-service model for outcome determination (FR-010), off-chain media storage (FR-012), and the specific composition of the local test environment (FR-008, FR-009), are all represented with high fidelity.

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The diagram clearly expresses the reasoning behind the architectural choices by separating concerns into logical components (e.g., Oracle Registration vs. Query Checking) and explicitly labeling constraints (e.g., Quarkus, scheduled services, CORS rules). The structure directly addresses key quality attributes like availability (NFR-006 via Oracle Validity Manager) and testability (NFR-004 via the Test Proxy).

traceability.reasoning: Components, relationships, and data flows in the diagram are highly traceable to specific requirements. samples.title: eivas / mini-swe-agent/qwen3-coder-480b-a35b-instruct

project_context.introduction:
### Product Scope

EIVAS is a Web application for epidemiological investigation scenarios. Through formatted data entry, storage, query, and visual association display, the system assists epidemiological form fillers in collecting case information and assists epidemiological analysts in analyzing case itineraries, associated cases, overall outbreaks, and statistical characteristics.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for software designers, developers, testers, system users, and project stakeholders.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Robustness | The system shall handle input cases outside the specified standard requirements. | Abnormal input shall not cause the system to crash. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Performance | The system shall support concurrent submission requests. | When 100 users submit requests simultaneously, page response time is less than 1 second. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Reliability / Availability | The system shall be able to run stably for long periods and support fast deployment switching. | When an unexpected event occurs, the system switches to the backup server within 20 minutes. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-004 | Security / Privacy | Query requests involving user personal information shall be encrypted. | Personal information query requests must be encrypted; the specification does not specify an encryption algorithm or protocol. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-005 | Resource Protection | The system shall cool down IP addresses that submit frequent query requests within a short period. | Frequently querying IP addresses are cooled down to avoid excessive server resource consumption and impact on normal investigation reporting and analysis. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Compatibility | The system shall support operation on various operating systems and browsers. | Core flows can be completed on target operating system and browser combinations; specific versions are to be defined by the project. | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Maintainability | The system code architecture shall support later maintenance. | Supports bug fixing, function modification, and new function addition; the team uses a unified coding style and adds code comments. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-008 | Usability | The system interface shall be concise and clear for professional epidemiological workers. | The interface primarily targets user understanding and use and avoids excessive artistic design. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-009 | Operational Usability | The system shall provide operational assistance for grassroots workers who lack software experience. | The system includes many operation prompts, error-operation feedback, and may include user documentation. | High | Demonstration |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | The system product form is a Web application. |
| C-002 | The scope of investigation reporting information shall refer to cited materials such as infectious disease information reporting management specifications. |
| C-003 | The system operating environment needs to cover various operating systems and browsers; specific target versions are not provided in the specification. |
| C-004 | Query requests involving personal information must be encrypted, and frequent query IP addresses must be cooled down. |
| C-005 | When an unexpected event occurs, the system shall switch to the backup server within 20 minutes. |
| C-006 | The user interface shall target understanding and use by professional epidemiological workers and remain concise and clear. |
| C-007 | The system shall provide operation prompts and error feedback for grassroots workers and may provide user documentation. |

### Operating Environment

The system is provided as a Web application and is required to run on various operating systems and browsers. Specific browser versions, server operating systems, database products, and deployment environments are not specified in the specification.

### Software/API Interfaces

| Interface | Requirement Summary |
| --- | --- |
| Front-end page to back-end database interface | Front-end pages transmit analyst or filler requests to the back-end database through interfaces, and the back-end database extracts information and feeds it back to front-end components. |
| Database storage interface | After the reporting form is submitted, case investigation information is sent to the back-end database for storage and analysis. |
| Algorithm module interface | The algorithm module calculates risks, clusters cases, and generates corresponding charts based on case investigation information. |

### Communication Interfaces

The system is a Web application. The specification only states that front-end pages interact with the back-end database through interfaces, and does not specify protocols, ports, message formats, authentication methods, or encrypted transport protocols. Query requests involving personal information must be encrypted.

### Assumptions and Dependencies

| ID | Assumption / Dependency |
| --- | --- |
| AD-001 | The system depends on a back-end database to store data required for cases, itineraries, and association analysis. |
| AD-002 | The system depends on an algorithm module to generate regional flow maps and case clustering graphs. |
| AD-003 | The system needs map or equivalent geographic visualization capability to display locations and itinerary paths. |
| AD-004 | The specification does not specify identity authentication, permission model, database type, map service provider, or deployment topology. |

project_context.introduction:
### Product scope
EasyLaTex is an online system for converting mathematical formulas, tables, images, CSV/Excel/PDF files, and handwritten inputs into LaTex code. The system also provides real-time rendering, project/workspace management, collaboration, version control, comments, and export capabilities.

### Intended audience
- Product managers and requirements reviewers validating the EasyLaTex requirement set.
- Developers implementing user management, file processing, AI recognition, rendering, collaboration, and version control modules.
- Test engineers deriving verification scenarios from functional, interface, performance, compatibility, and security requirements.
- Architecture and evaluation agents that need structured requirements for architecture view generation.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Quality attribute | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | For common handwritten formulas, standard formula images, and regular CSV/Excel/PDF files, LaTex conversion accuracy shall be at least 88%. | Accuracy | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | The LaTex rendering module shall keep rendering latency below 2 seconds. | Performance | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Each user operation such as page navigation or loading shall respond within 2 seconds. | Performance | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | The system shall concurrently process at least 100 user requests. | Scalability | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | The system shall support normal access through Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari on PC and mobile browsers. | Compatibility / portability | High | Demonstration |
| NFR-006 | File input compatibility shall include at least JPG, PNG, PDF, CSV, and Excel `.xlsx`. | Compatibility | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Generated LaTex results shall be compatible with mainstream LaTex editors and compilation environments such as Overleaf and TeXstudio. | Compatibility | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-008 | User information including email, username, and password shall be encrypted during storage and transmission. | Security / privacy | High | Inspection |
| NFR-009 | Project collaboration shall enforce permission control with at least project owner and collaborator roles. | Security / access control | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | The system shall provide defenses against common Web vulnerabilities including SQL injection, XSS, and CSRF. | Security | High | Security test |
| NFR-011 | The user interface shall be concise and consistent with user operation habits so users can quickly get started. | Usability | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-012 | The system shall use modular design, standardized API design, complete interface documentation, unit tests, integration tests, and deployment documentation to support maintainability and iteration. | Maintainability | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Users are assumed to use modern browsers that support WebSocket technology. |
| C-002 | The AI model is assumed to reach at least 90% image recognition accuracy during design and development. |
| C-003 | Upload and download workflows depend on stable user network connections. |
| C-004 | Identity security depends on the existing user authentication mechanism. |
| C-005 | Minimum client hardware includes Intel Core i3 or equivalent CPU, 4 GB RAM, 20 GB free disk space, integrated graphics, and a 1024x768 display. |
| C-006 | Recommended client hardware includes Intel Core i5 or higher CPU, 8 GB RAM or more, 50 GB free disk space with SSD recommended, graphics acceleration, and 1920x1080 or higher display. |
| C-007 | Supported operating systems include Windows 10 or higher, macOS 10.14 or higher, and Ubuntu 18.04 or higher. |
| C-008 | Supported database management systems include MySQL 8.0 or higher, PostgreSQL 12 or higher, or another compatible relational database. |
| C-009 | Supported web servers include Apache HTTP Server 2.4 or higher and Nginx 1.18 or higher. |
| C-010 | Development/runtime dependencies include Python 3.8 or higher, Node.js 14.x or higher, and TeX Live, MiKTeX, or another compatible LaTex engine. |
| C-011 | External application interfaces use HTTP/REST API. |
| C-012 | The system shall follow privacy-protection regulations such as GDPR and other data-protection regulations. |

### Operating environment
The system is intended to run through modern browsers that support WebSocket. Target clients include Windows, macOS, and Linux environments, with Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge. Server-side and development dependencies include relational databases, web servers, Python, Node.js, and a compatible LaTex engine.

### Software/API interfaces
The system shall expose HTTP/REST APIs for user registration, login, user information management, project management, file upload, download, collaboration sharing, real-time preview, version management, and comments. It shall also provide internal APIs for user verification, file storage, image recognition, LaTex generation, rendering, collaboration management, and version control.

### Communication interfaces
The specification explicitly identifies HTTP/REST API for external module interactions. It also assumes WebSocket-capable browsers, supporting web interaction scenarios where real-time preview or collaborative updates may require browser communication support.

### Assumptions and dependencies
- Users operate through modern WebSocket-capable browsers.
- AI model image recognition accuracy is expected to reach at least 90%.
- User network connections are stable enough for uploads and downloads.
- Identity security depends on the existing user authentication mechanism.
- Initial user scale and storage demand are relatively small.

samples.title: s000026_5d153cf4 / metagpt-custom/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose

This Software Requirements Specification defines the checked requirements for a Google Kubernetes Engine deployment that distributes Google Cloud Pub/Sub topic messages to web clients over WebSockets.

The document is prepared for architecture view generation and omits review notes, provenance fields, and supporting-material identifiers.

### 1.2 Product Scope

The product is a WebSocket adapter and deployment package that:

- Mirrors messages from a configured Google Cloud Pub/Sub topic to connected WebSocket clients.
- Lets web clients receive topic data without directly using the Pub/Sub API or SDK.
- Runs as a containerized runtime in Google Kubernetes Engine.
- Exposes the adapter through a Kubernetes `LoadBalancer` service.
- Uses a Kubernetes ConfigMap value to bind a deployment instance to one Pub/Sub topic.
- Uses a `websocketd` bridge with a Node.js `pulltop` process to serve topic data to clients.
- Supports horizontal pod autoscaling through Kubernetes autoscaling configuration.

Each deployment instance binds one externally reachable WebSocket endpoint to one configured Pub/Sub topic. Multiple topics shall be served by multiple independently configured deployment instances.

### 1.3 Intended Audience

The intended audience includes deployment operators, Kubernetes operators, GCP project administrators, web-client integrators, testers, and architecture evaluators.

### 1.4 Definitions and Abbreviations

- GCP: Google Cloud Platform.
- GKE: Google Kubernetes Engine.
- HPA: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler.
- Pub/Sub: Google Cloud Pub/Sub.
- SRS: Software Requirements Specification.
- VM: Virtual machine.
- WebSocket: Full-duplex network communication protocol used by connected clients.
project_context.asr:
## 6. Non-Functional Requirements

### NFR-001 Horizontal Scalability

The deployment shall support horizontal pod scaling through Kubernetes HPA configuration.

**Metric:** The HPA targets the adapter Deployment with min replicas `1`, max replicas `2`, and CPU utilization target `80`.

### NFR-002 Subscription Efficiency

The adapter shall support many WebSocket clients while using one Pub/Sub subscription per running VM or pod instance.

**Metric:** For `N` running instances and `M` connected clients, subscription consumption scales with `N` rather than `M`.

### NFR-003 Client Data Continuity

The adapter shall provide recent cached messages so client displays can show meaningful data during low-traffic periods.

**Metric:** A client can receive up to the last 10 cached messages for the configured topic.

### NFR-004 Deployment Compatibility

The adapter runtime shall be deployable as a container exposing port `8080` behind a Kubernetes `LoadBalancer` service exposing TCP port `80`.

**Metric:** The running service maps TCP `80` to adapter port `8080`.

### NFR-005 Client Simplicity

Web clients shall receive mirrored Pub/Sub topic data through WebSockets without direct Pub/Sub API or SDK integration.

**Metric:** Client integration requires the WebSocket endpoint and does not require Pub/Sub client credentials.

## 7. System Constraints

### CON-001 GCP Project Constraint

Deployment requires a GCP project with billing enabled.

### CON-002 Topic Access Constraint

Deployment requires read access to the configured Pub/Sub topic. The topic may be in another project if the deployment identity can read it.

### CON-003 Subscription Permission Constraint

Deployment requires permission to create Pub/Sub subscriptions.

### CON-004 GKE Deployment Constraint

The adapter shall run in Google Kubernetes Engine.

### CON-005 LoadBalancer Service Constraint

External access shall be provided through a Kubernetes `LoadBalancer` service using TCP port `80` and adapter port `8080`.

### CON-006 Runtime Composition Constraint

The checked runtime architecture shall include `pulltop`, `exec.sh`, and `websocketd` as runtime elements.

### CON-007 Topic Configuration Constraint

The configured topic value shall be passed from Kubernetes configuration into the adapter runtime through the `SYMBOL` environment variable.

### CON-008 Autoscaling Metric Constraint

Connected-client-count autoscaling shall not be assumed unless the deployment includes an explicit metric path for connected client count.

### 2.4 Operating Environment

The product shall operate in a GCP project with billing enabled and a GKE cluster available. It shall run as a containerized workload exposed through a Kubernetes `LoadBalancer` service using TCP port `80` and adapter port `8080`.

The deployment shall include a Kubernetes ConfigMap for the topic identifier, a Kubernetes Deployment for adapter pods, a Kubernetes Service for external access, and an HPA for horizontal scaling of the Deployment.

### 2.5 Assumptions and Dependencies

- The configured Pub/Sub topic is readable by the deploying user or service account.
- The deployment identity can create Pub/Sub subscriptions.
- The configured topic value is injected into the runtime as the `SYMBOL` environment variable.
- Web clients can establish WebSocket connections to the exposed endpoint.
- Horizontal scaling by connected-client count is not assumed unless an explicit connection-count metric path is configured.

It also notes the 10-message cache.

This severely misrepresents the deployment topology.

It also notes the 10-message cache.

samples.title: s000053_ec192d86 / direct/gpt-5

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS defines the checked software requirements for the `openshift/configuration-anomaly-detection` product based on repository-supported behavior and reviewed corrections. It is intended to capture externally observable capabilities and constraints suitable for downstream architecture generation.

### Product scope
Configuration Anomaly Detection (CAD) is an operational tool intended to reduce manual SRE investigation by detecting cluster anomalies, executing investigation and disposition workflows, and sending relevant communications to cluster owners or related operations systems. The repository includes:
- A CLI tool, `cadctl`, for CAD workflows
- Integration code used by the CLI
- Deployment assets for Tekton-based execution
- A skip-webhook execution path that creates a `PipelineRun` directly
- Template update utility assets

### Intended audience
This document is intended for:
- SRE and operations users running CAD workflows
- Deployment engineers operating Tekton-based execution paths
- Developers extending CLI endpoints, integrations, and deployment assets
- Architects deriving component and interface views from repository-supported behavior
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Portability | The CLI runtime artifact shall be packaged as a container image based on a UBI minimal runtime image and shall include `/bin/cadctl`. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | Build compatibility | The documented container build for the CLI shall use a Go 1.17 builder image. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-003 | Build metadata | The container image shall expose standard image labels for vendor, name, description, display name, version, build date, VCS reference, and Dockerfile path. | Low | Inspection |
| NFR-004 | Maintainability | Integration logic intended for CLI use shall be organized in a package library so CLI endpoints can call required integration code. | Low | Inspection |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | The runtime delivery form is constrained to a container image containing `/bin/cadctl`. |
| C-002 | The documented build process is constrained to a Go 1.17 builder image. |
| C-003 | The documented runtime base image is constrained to a UBI minimal runtime image. |
| C-004 | Direct Tekton execution is constrained to Tekton `v1beta1` `PipelineRun` manifests as documented in repository deployment assets. |
| C-005 | In the repository's documented default/example direct-execution path, the `PipelineRun` references `cad-checks-pipeline` in the `configuration-anomaly-detection` namespace. |
| C-006 | The skip-webhook execution path is constrained to bypass the event listener rather than use the normal trigger flow. |

### Operating environment
| Aspect | Description |
|---|---|
| Runtime form | Containerized CLI runtime containing `/bin/cadctl` |
| Build environment | Container build using a Go 1.17 builder image |
| Runtime base image | UBI minimal-based runtime image |
| Pipeline environment | Tekton `PipelineRun` resources in the CAD deployment namespace |
| Trigger environment | Tekton EventListener and Trigger for webhook-driven execution |

### Communication interfaces
| Interface | Requirement summary |
|---|---|
| Alert webhook input | The system shall support receiving external alert webhooks through a Tekton EventListener. |
| Triggered pipeline execution | The system shall trigger Tekton pipeline execution from the event-listener path. |
| Direct pipeline invocation | The system shall support a skip-webhook path that bypasses the event listener and creates a `PipelineRun` directly. |

### Assumptions and dependencies
- CAD depends on external systems and integrations including PagerDuty, AWS, and OCM.
- Tekton-based execution depends on deployment of the referenced pipeline and trigger resources.
- Direct execution depends on submission of a valid `PipelineRun` manifest.
- Template update behavior depends on repository-maintained template assets.

package "PagerDuty Team" as PagerDutyTeam {

rectangle "Tekton\nEvent\nListener" as TektonEventListener
rectangle "Tekton Interceptor:\nAlert investigation\nimplemented in CAD?" as TektonInterceptor

package "cadctl" as Cadctl {
    rectangle "switch(alerttype)" as SwitchAlertType
    rectangle "CHGM investigation" as ChgmInvestigation
    rectangle "CPD investigation" as CpdInvestigation
    rectangle "Future investigations" as FutureInvestigations
}

rectangle "Update Incident\nNotes" as UpdateIncidentNotes
rectangle "Escalate alert to\non-call SRE" as EscalateOnCallSre

Deadmanssnitch --> ChgmAlert
AppSreAlertmanager --> CpdAlert
ClusterAlertmanager --> AllClusterAlerts

CpdAlert --> PagerDutyAppSre
AllClusterAlerts --> HiveClusterService
PagerDutyTeam --> TektonEventListener : webhook
TektonEventListener --> TektonInterceptor
TektonInterceptor --> Cadctl : yes\ntekton pipeline run\ncadctl investigate --payload
TektonInterceptor --> EscalateOnCallSre : no
Cadctl --> SwitchAlertType
SwitchAlertType --> ChgmInvestigation
SwitchAlertType --> CpdInvestigation
SwitchAlertType --> FutureInvestigations
Cadctl --> UpdateIncidentNotes
UpdateIncidentNotes --> EscalateOnCallSre
@enduml

samples.title: s000350_a0cfb4f2 / direct/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS defines the externally observable software requirements for the `squatm3gator` API component.

### Product scope
Squatm3gator is a web system for security assessment that generates typosquatting and lookalike domain candidates from a user-supplied domain. Based on an original domain entered by the user, the system generates candidate domain variants that may be used for typosquatting or cybersquatting, including substitution, flipping, homoglyph fast, and homoglyph complete attack types. The system can be used by penetration testers to identify phishing-exercise domains and by security analysts to detect and reduce domain-squatting risk.

The product includes:
- a web entry page,
- session-based submission of domain-generation requests,
- asynchronous job dispatch through Redis-backed communication,
- asynchronous progress or result reporting over WebSocket,
- optional GoDaddy-based domain availability checks using configured credentials and endpoints.

### Intended audience
This document is intended for maintainers, integrators, testers, and operators of the API service and its dependent processing components.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Performance and responsiveness | The system shall asynchronously process domain-generation tasks through the Redis job channel and background consumer thread, decoupling HTTP request submission from actual domain enumeration execution. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | Security and reliability | The system shall ensure result isolation between users by correlating reporting messages with the requesting session key and job ID before emitting them to clients. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Compatibility | When the JSON output option is requested, the system shall generate job options specifying `--output=json`. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-004 | Usability | The system shall return human-readable plain-text error responses for HTTP 400, 404, and 500 errors, and shall also return a parsing-failure message when query string parsing fails. | Medium | Test |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | Redis integration is constrained to the configured Redis endpoint and communication model used by the application. |
| C-002 | GoDaddy integration is constrained to configured API endpoints and `sso-key` authorization using a configured key and secret. |
| C-003 | Domain-generation capability depends on a worker or consumer component that listens to the Redis job channel. In the current implementation, this consumer is started by an in-process background thread in the API process and invokes a `squatm3` subprocess to execute jobs. |
| C-004 | Domain-generation capability depends on the wrapped third-party `squatm3` CLI engine, and options constructed by the API shall remain compatible with command-line parameters supported by that engine. |

### Operating environment
- A server runtime hosting an HTTP application and WebSocket support.
- Redis connectivity at the configured message-queue URI.
- Local availability of the wrapped `squatm3` engine used for domain-generation execution.
- Optional outbound HTTP connectivity to GoDaddy APIs using configured credentials.

### Software interfaces
- The system shall provide HTTP GET `/` to return the main page and create a session.
- The system shall expose at least one GET API resource for retrieving domains generated by the complete homoglyphs attack for a supplied domain value.
- The system shall accept attack-related request inputs including attack codes, `godaddy`, and `output`.
- The system shall emit asynchronous job messages or results through a WebSocket-compatible channel associated with the requesting session.
- The system shall communicate with Redis using a configured Redis URI.
- When optional GoDaddy integration is enabled, the system shall call GoDaddy-related endpoints using configured API key, secret, base URL, and endpoint definitions.

### Communication interfaces
- Redis shall be used for job publication and reporting communication.
- The system shall support asynchronous client updates over WebSocket or Socket.IO-compatible communication.
- GoDaddy HTTP requests shall use `Authorization: sso-key <key>:<secret>` when GoDaddy integration is enabled.

### Assumptions and dependencies
- Redis is reachable through the configured connection URI.
- Session creation and session validation are required for job submission and result correlation.
- The wrapped `squatm3` command-line engine is present and callable by the consumer flow.
- GoDaddy credentials and endpoint configuration are available when GoDaddy-based checking is enabled.

rectangle "Socket.io\nWebSocket result channel" as SocketIo
queue "Redis\nCommand and reporting queues" as Redis
rectangle "Workers\nCommand consumers" as Workers
rectangle "Squatm3\nDomain generation engine" as Squatm3
cloud "GoDaddy API\nDomain availability" as GoDaddyApi

WebUi --> Api : HTTP requests\ncreate session and queue job
Api --> WebUi : rendered page\nplain-text errors
WebUi <--> SocketIo : progress and results\nsession socket

Api --> Redis : enqueue job\nsession key / job UUID
SocketIo <--> Redis : read reporting queue\nWORKER#JOB#MESSAGE
Redis <--> Workers : command jobs\nworker reports

Workers --> Squatm3 : attack options\nhomoglyph / flip / add / replace
Squatm3 --> Workers : generated domains
Workers --> GoDaddyApi : optional availability check\nsso-key auth
GoDaddyApi --> Workers : availability result
@enduml

samples.title: AwesomeMusic / direct/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
Its purpose is to provide structured input for architecture generation, test design, and requirements traceability for an AI music creation and sharing platform.

### Product Scope

The AI music creation and sharing platform is a Web system for music enthusiasts. After registration, users can use the platform for AI music creation, score generation, score upload and sharing, favorites, follows, recommendations, search, hot charts, and downloads. The system contains three modules: Web frontend, Web backend, and algorithm service. The algorithm service generates music from user-provided source files and includes online music generation and offline model iterative upgrade services.

This document uses the specification definitions of MQ, score, track seed, and midi music. A score is an abc-format plain-text file that can generate midi music. A track seed is the beginning part of an abc score and is less than 10 seconds long. Midi music is a music file playable by midi-capable players.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for customers, product managers, developers, testers, and downstream architecture generation and requirements review workflows. Target end users are music enthusiasts, especially users interested in AI music creation, music sharing, and novel approaches to music creation.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Performance | Average account registration latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Performance | Average login latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Performance | Average score favorite latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Performance | Average follow-user latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | Performance | Average score upload latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 3000ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Performance | Average model favorite latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-007 | Performance | Average Web-side score generation latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-008 | Performance | Average score download latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 3000ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-009 | Performance | Average recommendation retrieval latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 500ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | Performance | Average score search latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 1000ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-011 | Performance | Average hot-chart viewing latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-012 | Performance | Algorithm training for generation models shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-013 | Performance | Algorithm score generation shall satisfy throughput and latency requirements. | Throughput 5ops, average latency <= 1min. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Performance | Classifying all scores shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-015 | Performance | Song recommendation shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-016 | Scalability | The Web system shall support the preset concurrent access throughput. | Concurrent access throughput is no less than 2000QPS. | High | Test |
| NFR-017 | Scalability | The Web system shall support transaction change throughput. | Transaction changes are no less than 100TPS. | High | Test |
| NFR-018 | Usability | System interactions shall be suitable for music production beginners. | Interactions are simple and understandable and do not require professional function design. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-019 | Usability | The system shall provide user instructions, prompts, and guidance. | Comprehensive instructions, prompts, and guidance exist to help users get started quickly. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-020 | Security | The gateway shall isolate internal and external network boundaries. | Internal systems are not exposed externally due to loose security configuration. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-021 | Reliability | Important data shall be stored in multiple replicas. | Important data is stored in 3 replicas. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-022 | Availability | The system shall remain available under partial failures. | The system remains usable when some internal nodes are upgraded, crash, or experience a certain level of network partition. | High | Test |
| NFR-023 | Maintainability | Code shall be readable. | Code follows naming conventions, and names correspond to actual meaning. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-024 | Maintainability | Code shall avoid excessive coupling. | Code supports later iterative optimization, extension of existing functions, and addition of new functions. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-025 | Load Balancing | The system shall maintain stable response and reasonable resource utilization under high access volume. | Load balancing technology is provided when user access volume is high. | High | Test |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Frontend languages are HTML, CSS, and JavaScript; the frontend framework is Bootstrap. |
| C-002 | Backend language is Golang; backend framework is Gin. |
| C-003 | Webpage response time shall be within 1 second. |
| C-004 | Music generation model training time shall be within 2 hours. |
| C-005 | The algorithm model shall be able to generate enough varieties of music styles. |
| C-006 | Generated music quality shall be above the baseline. |
| C-007 | Algorithm model response time shall be controlled within 1 minute. |
| C-008 | Product interfaces shall reflect user friendliness and have a certain degree of aesthetics. |
| C-009 | Project R&D shall strictly follow the project management process to avoid phase delays affecting later development. |
| C-010 | System hardware environment consists of 4 virtual machines, each with CPU 4 * 2400MHz, 8GB memory, and 200GB storage. |
| C-011 | System software environment includes Ubuntu 18.04 LTS, Hadoop 3.2.2, RabbitMQ 3.8.14, etcd 3.4.15, and Nginx 1.19.8. |
| C-012 | Communication protocols are HTTPS and TCP/IP. |
| C-013 | Task metadata must conform to database constraints and limit task size. |
| C-014 | When task submission fails, erroneous data needs to be automatically skipped and retried. |
| C-015 | All requirements listed in use-case diagrams are planned for the current version and do not need to be assigned to future versions. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Hardware | 4 virtual machines, each with CPU 4 * 2400MHz, 8GB memory, and 200GB storage. |
| Operating system | Ubuntu 18.04 LTS. |
| Software interfaces | Apache Hadoop 3.2.2, RabbitMQ 3.8.14, etcd 3.4.15, and Nginx 1.19.8. |
| Communication protocols | HTTPS and TCP/IP. |
| Frontend technology |

samples.title: Indoor_Fitness_App / metagpt-custom/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### Product Scope

Indoor Fitness App is an indoor exercise and health application based on pose estimation. It targets exercise scenarios with available activity space, such as homes, offices, gyms, and outdoor playgrounds. The system provides professional posture evaluation, intelligent training plan recommendation, training poster and professional report generation, and exercise screen casting. Its goal is to let users receive guidance similar to a personal trainer during indoor exercise.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for application users, developers, testers, architects, and project stakeholders. The specification explicitly expects users and developers to fully understand the software requirements.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | User Satisfaction | The final user survey satisfaction for the system shall reach the target value. | Satisfaction reaches above 90%. | Medium | Analysis |
| NFR-002 | Pose Recognition Accuracy | Under normal scenarios, system pose recognition shall achieve high accuracy. | When lighting, background, person size are normal and no occlusion or missing parts exist, pose recognition accuracy is above 95%. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Occlusion Robustness | Under occlusion or missing-part scenarios, the system shall maintain recognition accuracy for visible parts. | Accuracy for unoccluded and non-missing parts is above 90%; accuracy for occluded parts reaches 50%. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Model Runtime Performance | Pose estimation model runtime speed shall satisfy real-time evaluation needs. | Model runtime speed reaches above 20 fps. | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | Pose Evaluation Accuracy | When pose estimation has no errors, user posture evaluation shall achieve high accuracy. | Posture evaluation accuracy is above 95%. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Pose Error Tolerance | When pose estimation contains errors, the evaluation algorithm shall identify errors and maintain accuracy. | The algorithm can identify errors most of the time and guarantee accuracy no lower than 95%; "most of the time" must be quantified in the test plan. | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Body-Proportion Robustness | Posture similarity estimation shall reduce the impact of user body shape and body proportion on scoring. | The specification only requires "eliminating the impact to a certain extent" and does not provide a quantitative threshold. | Medium | Analysis |
| NFR-008 | Recommendation Latency | Recommended plan generation shall meet the response-time constraint. | Each recommended plan is generated within 1 second. | High | Test |
| NFR-009 | Report Generation Latency | Exercise report generation shall meet the response-time constraint. | Exercise report generation time does not exceed 3 seconds. | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | Casting Video Latency | Casting video stream transmission latency shall meet real-time display requirements. | Video stream transmission latency between devices does not exceed 100 ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-011 | Casting Command Latency | Response latency after the Android server sends a command to the client shall meet real-time control requirements. | Android client response latency does not exceed 50 ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-012 | Data Leakage Protection | The system shall take leakage-protection measures to prevent user data leakage. | Leakage risk is reduced through clipboard control, drag-and-drop control, screen copy/screenshot control, memory theft control, and related techniques. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-013 | SQL Injection Protection | The system shall prevent SQL injection. | Variable data types and formats are checked, special symbols are filtered, variables are bound, and precompiled statements are used. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-014 | Portability | The system shall support a certain degree of module portability. | It adopts a modular design concept, and modules rely on interfaces for data exchange. | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | A precondition for professional evaluation is that the user's mobile phone supports video recording at 30 fps or above. |
| C-002 | Exercise screen casting requires the current Android device and searched Android device to be in the same LAN. |
| C-003 | Server hardware shall satisfy CPU Pentium 900M, recommended Pentium 4 1.2G, and at least 256 MB RAM. |
| C-004 | PC hardware shall satisfy CPU Pentium 133M or higher and at least 64 MB RAM. |
| C-005 | Mobile hardware shall reach an octa-core 2.45 GHz CPU and 4 GB RAM + 32 GB ROM or above. |
| C-006 | The PC operating system shall be Windows. |
| C-007 | The mobile operating system shall be Android 8.0 or iOS 11.0 and above; the casting target is limited to Android devices in the specification. |
| C-008 | User exercise location is not restricted to a specific place, but activity space must be available. |
| C-009 | The system shall prepare and verify software requirements according to the software requirements, testing, life-cycle, and measurement standards listed in the specification. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Usage scenario | Available 24 hours a day, with no restriction on usage time, duration, or location as long as activity space is available. |
| Server hardware | CPU Pentium 900M, recommended Pentium 4 1.2G; at least 256 MB RAM. |
| PC hardware | CPU Pentium 133M or higher; at least 64 MB RAM. |
| Mobile hardware | Octa-core 2.45 GHz CPU, 4 GB RAM + 32 GB ROM or above. |
| PC software | Windows operating system. |
| Mobile software | Android 8.0 or iOS 11.0 and above. |

### Software/API Interfaces

| Interface | Requirement Summary |
| --- | --- |
| Camera capture interface | The App uses a fixed mobile phone camera to monitor body posture in real time and capture video during exercise. |
| Posture evaluation processing interface | The terminal device feeds back skeleton extraction information in real time and compares it with standard skeleton movements for scoring. |
| Server-side processing interface | Plan recommendation and poster/report material collection and processing involve server-side participation. |
| Inter-module data exchange interface | The project adopts a modular design concept, and modules rely on interfaces for data exchange. |
| SQL data access interface | The specification requires SQL injection prevention but does not specify the database product, table structure, or access protocol. |

### Communication Interfaces

| Communication | Requirement Summary |
| --- | --- |
| Casting video stream | The
...[truncated]

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
' --- Configuration & Styles ---
skinparam ranksep 50
skinparam nodesep 40
skinparam packageStyle rectangle
left to right direction

' Styling for different layers
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor<<client>> #e1f5fe
    BorderColor<<client>> #01579b
    
    BackgroundColor<<native>> #bbdefb
    BorderColor<<native>> #0d47a1
    
    BackgroundColor<<react>> #c8e6c9
    BorderColor<<react>> #1b5e20
    
    BackgroundColor<<bridge>> #00acc1
    BorderColor<<bridge>> #006064
    FontColor<<bridge>> white
    
    BackgroundColor<<infra>> #f5f5f5
    BorderColor<<infra>> #212121
    BorderStyle<<infra>> dashed
}

' --- Diagram Definitions ---

' Layer 1: Client Layer
package "Client / Platforms" as ClientLayer <<client>> {
    rectangle "Mobile Device" as Mobile <<client>>
    rectangle "Desktop PC" as Desktop <<client>>
    rectangle "Web Browser" as Web <<client>>
    rectangle "Android OS" as Android <<client>>
    rectangle "iOS / Apple" as iOS <<client>>
    rectangle "Smart Watch / Wearables" as IoT <<client>>
}

' Layer 2: Native Module Layer
package "Native Modules" as NativeLayer <<native>> {
    rectangle "Camera Call" as Camera <<native>>
    rectangle "Model Loading / TensorFlow" as ModelLoad <<native>>
    rectangle "Linear Algebra Library" as LinearAlgebra <<native>>
    rectangle "Voice Library Call" as Voice <<native>>
    rectangle "GPU Computing" as GPU <<native>>
    rectangle "Screen Recording" as ScreenRec <<native>>
    rectangle "Socket Communication" as Socket <<native>>
    rectangle "Data Processing" as DataProc <<native>>
    rectangle "Permission Requests" as Permissions <<native>>
}

' Layer 3: Cross-Platform Framework Layer
package "React Native Framework" as RNLayer <<react>> {
    rectangle "Native Module Bridge" as NativeBridge <<bridge>>
    rectangle "Data Caching" as DataCaching <<react>>
    rectangle "Server Access" as ServerAccess <<react>>
    rectangle "Redux State Management" as Redux <<react>>
    rectangle "Interface Packaging" as APIPkg <<react>>
    rectangle "Data Interception" as DataIntercept <<react>>
    rectangle "End-to-End Communication" as P2P <<react>>
}

' Sidebar: Infrastructure
package "Infrastructure & Support" as InfraLayer <<infra>> {
    rectangle "Data Exchange" as DataExchange <<infra>>
    rectangle "Performance Optimization" as Performance <<infra>>
    rectangle "Stability Construction" as Stability <<infra>>
}

' --- Relationships ---

ClientLayer --> NativeLayer
NativeLayer <--> NativeBridge

' Directing bridge to RN components
NativeBridge --- DataCaching
NativeBridge --- ServerAccess
NativeBridge --- Redux
NativeBridge --- APIPkg
NativeBridge --- DataIntercept
NativeBridge --- P2P

' Infrastructure interaction (dashed as per original)
NativeLayer ..> InfraLayer
RNLayer ..> InfraLayer

@enduml

samples.title: labhire / direct/claude-sonnet-4-6

project_context.introduction:
### Product Scope

LabHire is a research internship information integration platform for university teachers and students. It supports research internship project publication, recommendation, retrieval, application, management, mutual evaluation, personal information, resumes, mentor search, community communication, internship referrals, and management-side review. The system scope is limited to university research internship information and community referral posts, and does not include social enterprise recruitment information or headhunting advertisements.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for developers, testers, project managers, customer representatives, and project-related personnel such as marketing, operations, and maintenance staff.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Performance | The system shall provide timely feedback for any user input. | For any user input, the system gives feedback within 1 second. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Robustness | The system shall remain normally operational under all possible user inputs. | It does not crash because of abnormal input and provides timely feedback for erroneous operations. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Access Control | The system shall ensure that each type of user can only obtain and modify information within their permissions. | Mentors can only view resumes of students selected for their projects; students cannot view each other's resumes; group members can view basic information but cannot view resumes. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Data Confidentiality | The system shall protect login, registration, and chat data. | User login/registration and chat content must be encrypted; the specification does not specify an encryption algorithm. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-005 | Abuse Prevention | The system shall restrict abnormal login and operation behavior. | Supports login and operation frequency limits based on IP and user behavior, graphical verification codes, SMS verification codes, and abnormal behavior detection. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Auditability | The system shall support back-end risk-control logs and behavior audit. | Risk-control logs and behavior audit modules support manual analysis and alerting. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-007 | Usability | The system shall allow most new users to naturally find functional modules and learn how to use them. | Usage instructions are provided to guide the remaining users. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-008 | User Interface Quality | The system interface shall improve efficiency and friendliness around user roles. | The student side may be more attractive; the mentor side should be concise, clear, and aesthetically pleasing; input/output methods, interaction methods, and functional distribution should support work efficiency. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-009 | Correctness | System operation and presentation logic shall conform to functional requirement goals. | Key functional flows are consistent with the functional requirements in this SRS. | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | Clarity | System descriptions shall be clear and functional boundaries shall be clearly divided. | Requirements, pages, and module responsibilities are not confused. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-011 | Extensibility | The system shall reduce coupling between components and support extension. | Component coupling is reduced, and new functions do not require broad changes to existing modules. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-012 | Browser Compatibility | The system shall be normally accessible through mainstream browsers. | Chrome, Firefox, or Microsoft Edge can access core functions normally. | High | Test |
| NFR-013 | Portability | The system shall support browsing on multiple device types while maintaining consistent page logic and functions. | Core page logic and functions are consistent between mobile and desktop. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Legal Compliance | User-published content displayed by the system shall comply with relevant laws. | Violating posts or comments can be reviewed and deleted, and displayed content complies with relevant laws. | High | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | The system scope is limited to university research internship information and community internship referrals, excluding social enterprise recruitment and headhunting advertisements. |
| C-002 | Registered users shall complete student or mentor identity certification through university email domains and verification codes. |
| C-003 | Enterprise-certified students must pass enterprise email verification before publishing internship referral posts. |
| C-004 | Administrator account passwords are directly assigned by the back-end database, and the system has no administrator registration entry. |
| C-005 | Manual identity review shall be completed by administrators within 72 hours. |
| C-006 | Student resume upload supports formats such as PDF, with maximum file size 10 MB. |
| C-007 | Community posts are limited to within 400 words and support text formats such as HTML and Markdown. |
| C-008 | Mobile devices shall satisfy quad-core 1.5 GHz CPU, 1 GB RAM + 4 GB ROM, Android 7.0 or iOS 8.0 and above, and WeChat 6.5.6 and above. |
| C-009 | Desktop devices shall satisfy at least 2 GB memory, broadband network, Windows 7 or Mac OS X 10.9 and above; Chrome, Firefox, or Microsoft Edge is recommended. |
| C-010 | The system and requirements documents shall follow GB/T 8566-2007, GB/T 8567-2006, and GB/T 9385-2008, and Python code shall follow PEP8. |
| C-011 | Display of user-published content must comply with relevant laws. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Mobile hardware | Quad-core 1.5 GHz CPU, 1 GB RAM + 4 GB ROM. |
| Mobile software | Android 7.0 and above, iOS 8.0 and above, WeChat 6.5.6 and above. |
| Desktop hardware/network | At least 2 GB memory and broadband network. |
| Desktop software | Windows 7 and above, Mac OS X 10.9 and above; Chrome, Firefox, or Microsoft Edge is recommended. |

### Software/API Interfaces

| Interface | Requirement Summary |
| --- | --- |
| Email verification-code interface | Registration must send a verification code to a university email address; enterprise certification must send a verification code to an enterprise-domain email address. |
| Notification interface | Favorite updates, mentor messages, comment replies, project applications, and review states need to trigger notifications; review feedback can be sent through in-site messages and email push. |
| Chat interface | Mentors chat with applicant students in pro
...[truncated]

samples.title: s000353_eb501ff3 / metagpt-custom/gpt-5

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS defines the checked software requirements for Turing and its related runtime components. It is limited to repository-supported behavior and interfaces reflected in the reviewed source materials.

### Product scope
Turing provides:
- an HTTP API for Turing resources, including routers and ensemblers,
- cluster-facing components for creating, updating, and deleting Turing router deployments in Kubernetes,
- a PyFuncEnsembler webservice runtime for user-defined ensemblers.

### Intended audience
This document is intended for:
- product owners and maintainers of Turing,
- API consumers integrating with Turing endpoints,
- QA and verification engineers,
- deployment and platform engineers operating Turing in Kubernetes-based environments.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Security and validation | The API service shall provide an HTTP middleware layer for authorization checks and OpenAPI-spec-based request validation; authorization middleware shall be mounted when authorization configuration is enabled, and request validation middleware shall be mounted when OpenAPI validation configuration is enabled. | High | Inspection and configuration test |
| NFR-002 | Observability | The Router component shall integrate Jaeger tracing capability; the system shall initialize a Jaeger client to support request tracing, but whether a specific request receives a trace shall be determined by the tracer enabled state or sampling configuration. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-003 | Configurability | The Router user container shall use port `8080` by default and shall allow this port to be overridden through `ports.containerPort` in deployment configuration. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-004 | Interoperability | API specifications shall conform to OpenAPI `3.0.3` for documented endpoints and schemas. | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | The API is constrained to OpenAPI-defined endpoints and schemas, including OpenAPI version `3.0.3`. |
| C-002 | Router deployment lifecycle management is constrained to a Kubernetes cluster environment. |
| C-003 | Local Docker image building for the PyFuncEnsembler service requires prior download of model artifacts from an MLflow model registry. |
| C-004 | Router user containers use port `8080` by default unless overridden through `ports.containerPort`. |

### Operating environment
- HTTP-based API server
- Kubernetes cluster for router deployment lifecycle management
- Docker-based packaging for the PyFuncEnsembler service
- MLflow model registry as an artifact source for local image building of the PyFuncEnsembler service

### Software/API interfaces
| Interface | Requirement summary |
|---|---|
| OpenAPI-defined API | API handlers and routing shall be defined by OpenAPI specifications. |
| Kubernetes cluster interface | The system shall use Kubernetes interfaces for creating, updating, and deleting Turing router deployments. |
| MLflow model registry | The PyFuncEnsembler local image build flow shall use model artifacts downloaded from an MLflow model registry. |
| Middleware layer | The API service shall provide an HTTP middleware layer for authorization checks and OpenAPI-spec-based request validation, subject to configuration. |

### Communication interfaces
| Interface | Requirement summary |
|---|---|
| HTTP | API endpoints shall be exposed over HTTP. |
| JSON payloads | Ensembler API responses shall use `application/json` where documented. |

### Assumptions and dependencies
- API behavior is defined through OpenAPI specifications.
- Router deployment operations depend on Kubernetes cluster access.
- PyFuncEnsembler image creation depends on downloading model artifacts from an MLflow model registry.
- Request tracing may integrate with Jaeger.
- Middleware behavior for authorization and OpenAPI request validation depends on service configuration.

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #555555
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

rectangle "Turing User" as TuringUser
rectangle "Router Clients" as RouterClients
rectangle "Router Endpoints" as RouterEndpoints

package "Turing System" as TuringSystem {
    rectangle "Turing UI\nWeb interface to configure Routers" as TuringUi
    rectangle "Turing API Server\nHTTP REST API to configure Routers" as TuringApiServer
    database "Database\nPersistent storage for Router objects" as RouterDatabase
}

rectangle "Secret Store" as SecretStore
rectangle "Authorization Service" as AuthorizationService

package "Kubernetes Cluster" as KubernetesCluster {
    rectangle "Knative Controller" as KnativeController
    rectangle "Istio" as Istio
}

rectangle "Experimentation Service" as ExperimentationService
rectangle "Machine Learning Platform\nMLP API Server" as MlpApiServer
rectangle "Monitoring and Alerting Service" as MonitoringService

TuringUser --> TuringUi : use
TuringUi --> TuringApiServer : make HTTP calls
TuringApiServer --> RouterDatabase : save and retrieve routers

RouterClients --> RouterEndpoints : requests from router clients
TuringApiServer --> RouterEndpoints : create endpoints\nfor accessing routers

TuringApiServer --> SecretStore : save and retrieve\ncredentials
TuringApiServer --> AuthorizationService : check user\npermissions
TuringApiServer --> KubernetesCluster : deploy router\nworkloads
TuringApiServer --> ExperimentationService : retrieve experiments\nconfigured for routers
TuringApiServer --> MlpApiServer : retrieve projects and\nenvironments for routers
TuringApiServer --> MonitoringService : configure alerts based\non router metrics

KubernetesCluster --> KnativeController : manage router services
KubernetesCluster --> Istio : expose router traffic
Istio --> RouterEndpoints : route client traffic
MonitoringService --> KubernetesCluster : retrieve router metrics
@enduml
