samples.title: s000032_0a49a326 / direct/qwen3-coder-480b-a35b-instruct

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose
This Software Requirements Specification defines normalized requirements for the Activiti 7 developer-guide examples and the platform behaviors they demonstrate, including authentication, process runtime interaction, task runtime authorization, audit observation, cloud deployment access, and scalability configuration.

### 1.2 Scope
This SRS describes Activiti platform behaviors, example processes, and deployment patterns documented or demonstrated by the developer guide. These requirements are not software obligations implemented by the guide content itself.

The SRS covers:
- authentication token acquisition and use for user endpoint access,
- process definition lookup and `SimpleProcess` instance start behavior,
- audit observation for a just-started process instance,
- `TaskRuntime` access control through a logged-in user with the required role,
- a complete example that combines `ProcessRuntime` and `TaskRuntime` APIs with a human participant,
- Kubernetes/GKE ingress-based external access,
- deployment descriptor parameters for scalability experiments,
- architecture background needed for downstream architecture view generation.

The SRS does not define:
- a graphical user interface,
- a quantified performance target for cloud connector transaction handling,
- an ongoing build-artifact version obligation for the developer guide itself,
- guaranteed executability of the full example beyond the documented example/module presence.

### 1.3 Intended Audience
- Developers following Activiti 7 examples.
- Integrators invoking runtime, task, audit, or query APIs.
- Operators deploying example services to Kubernetes or GKE.
- Architecture-generation tools that need normalized requirements without provenance metadata.

### 1.4 Definitions and Abbreviations
| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Activiti Cloud | Activiti cloud-oriented runtime and service environment used by the examples. |
| Example Runtime Bundle | Runtime bundle that hosts deployed process definitions and process instances in the examples. |
| Keycloak | Identity provider used to obtain authentication tokens. |
| `ProcessRuntime` | API used for process runtime interaction. |
| `TaskRuntime` | API used for task interaction and requiring a logged-in user with the proper role. |
| `ACTIVITI_USER` | Required user role for interacting with the `TaskRuntime` API. |
| `ROLE_ACTIVITI_USER` | Granted authority form of the `ACTIVITI_USER` role. |
| Ingress Controller | Kubernetes component that routes external traffic to selected internal services. |
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Quality attribute | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Authentication tokens used for protected endpoint access shall be time-limited and shall become invalid after their validity period or invalidation event. | Security | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | The example deployment shall support scalability experimentation by allowing data input rate and cloud component replica counts to be configured through deployment descriptors. | Scalability | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-003 | Protected endpoint and task API access shall preserve role-based authorization boundaries through token authentication and logged-in user context. | Security | High | Test |

No measurable latency, throughput, or performance-improvement threshold is specified for cloud connector transaction handling in this SRS.

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint | Type |
|---|---|---|
| C-001 | Security, user identity, groups, and roles shall rely on Spring Security modules within a Spring Boot application context. | Technology |
| C-002 | `TaskRuntime` access shall require the `ACTIVITI_USER` role and its `ROLE_ACTIVITI_USER` granted-authority form. | Access control |
| C-003 | External access to selected Kubernetes/GKE services shall depend on NGINX Ingress Controller configuration and public IP availability. | Deployment/network |
| C-004 | For Activiti 7.0.0-EA-201802 compatibility context, Activiti project artifacts are aligned with Spring Boot `2.0.0.RELEASE` and Spring Cloud `Finchley.M8`; this shall not be treated as an ongoing build constraint on the developer guide itself. | Compatibility |
| C-005 | The developer guide content shall not be treated as the runtime implementation of Activiti, Keycloak, Spring Security, or Kubernetes behavior. | Scope |
| C-006 | Architecture background components shall not be expanded into mandatory functional behavior unless a functional requirement assigns behavior to them. | Architecture scope |

### 2.4 Operating Environment
| Environment aspect | Requirement context |
|---|---|
| Spring Boot application | Provides the application context for security and user setup in the examples. |
| Spring Security | Provides user, group, role, and logged-in user context handling. |
| Keycloak | Issues authentication tokens for subsequent user endpoint requests. |
| Activiti Runtime Bundle | Hosts process definitions and started process instances. |
| Audit Service | Displays audit events for the just-started process instance. |
| Query Service | Provides process execution information as read-oriented process state. |
| Kubernetes / GKE | Hosts cloud-oriented services in the deployment examples. |
| NGINX Ingress Controller | Routes external requests from a public IP to selected internal services. |
| Deployment descriptors | Hold configurable data input rate and cloud component replica counts. |

### 3.3 Communication Interfaces
| ID | Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|---|
| CI-001 | REST authorization | REST authorization shall establish the currently logged-in user context for protected endpoint interaction. |
| CI-002 | Ingress routing | NGINX ingress shall create routes from external access points to selected internal Kubernetes services. |
| CI-003 | Token-based authentication | User endpoint requests shall include a valid token when protected endpoints are accessed. |

### 2.6 Assumptions and Dependencies
| ID | Assumption or dependency |
|---|---|
| AD-001 | Authentication tokens are time-limited and may need to be reacquired after expiration or invalidation. |
| AD-002 | User endpoint access depends on obtaining and supplying a valid Keycloak token. |
| AD-003 | `TaskRuntime` interaction depends on the current logged-in user context and the `ACTIVITI_USER` / `ROLE_ACTIVITI_USER` role. |
| AD-004 | External interaction with selected cluster services depends on ingress configuration and public IP availability. |
| AD-005 | Scalability experiments depend on deployment descriptor parameters for input rate and replica count. |
| AD-006 | Version alignment for Activiti project artifacts is a point-in-time compatibility context and not an ongoing build obligation for the developer guide itself. |

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #5B8F35
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #111111
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #D8EAFB
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

rectangle "Client" as Client
rectangle "SSO/IDM\n(Keycloak)" as Keycloak

package "Activiti Cloud Infrastructure" as CloudInfrastructure {
    rectangle "Gateway" as Gateway
    rectangle "Registry\n(Kubernetes)" as Registry
    rectangle "Config Maps\n(Kubernetes)" as ConfigMaps

    package "Activiti Cloud Application" as CloudApplication {
        rectangle "Query\nService" as QueryService
        rectangle "Audit\nService" as AuditService
        rectangle "Notification\nService" as NotificationService

        package "Cloud Connectors" as CloudConnectors {
            rectangle "Customer\nLogic" as CustomerLogic
            rectangle "Out of the\nBox Services" as OutOfBoxServices
        }

        package "Runtime Bundle" as RuntimeBundle {
            rectangle "Process\nRuntime" as ProcessRuntime
            rectangle "BPMN, Form\nand Rule Defs" as BpmnFormRuleDefs
        }
    }
}

Client --> Gateway : WebFlow
Keycloak --> Client : Direct Access Grant
Gateway --> Keycloak : WebFlow
Gateway --> CloudApplication : routed service access
Gateway --> Registry : service discovery
Registry --> ConfigMaps : configuration lookup
ConfigMaps --> CloudConnectors : Kubernetes configuration
QueryService --> RuntimeBundle : process queries
AuditService --> RuntimeBundle : audit events
NotificationService --> RuntimeBundle : notifications
CloudConnectors --> RuntimeBundle : business integration
ProcessRuntime --> BpmnFormRuleDefs : executes definitions
RuntimeBundle --> Keycloak : identity and token validation
@enduml

completeness.reasoning: The diagram is comprehensive, successfully representing all major components specified in the requirements, including the primary services (Runtime Bundle, Keycloak, Audit), infrastructure elements (Kubernetes, Ingress), configuration artifacts (Deployment Descriptors), and even the designated background components (Notification Service, Cloud Connectors). No required architectural element is missing.

faithfulness.reasoning: The diagram misrepresents several fundamental relationships, which significantly impacts its faithfulness. The ProcessRuntime and TaskRuntime APIs are incorrectly depicted as separate components called by the Example Runtime Bundle, whereas they are interfaces provided by it. Furthermore, the ingress data flow is incomplete and modeled incorrectly, showing ingress routing traffic 'to' the Kubernetes cluster as a whole rather than routing external traffic 'to services within' the cluster. These errors contradict standard architectural patterns and the implicit relationships in the SRS.

architectural_rationality.reasoning: While the diagram correctly applies some rational patterns, such as using an API Gateway for centralized access and a dedicated identity provider (Keycloak), it contains significant logical flaws. Modeling the Runtime Bundle as a client of its own APIs is architecturally irrational. The disjointed and incorrect representation of the NGINX ingress flow also demonstrates a weak understanding of cloud-native network routing, undermining the overall rationality of the proposed architecture.

traceability.reasoning: Components are clearly labeled and can be traced back to the SRS (e.g., 'Keycloak' to API-001, 'Deployment Descriptors' to NFR-002). However, the flawed modeling of key interactions makes it difficult to trace behavioral requirements accurately. For instance, tracing the full path for starting a process (FR-003) or external access (FR-007) is ambiguous due to the incorrect and incomplete connections shown for the Runtime Bundle and Ingress Controller.

readability.reasoning: The diagram is visually clean, uses a consistent style for components, and employs clear labels. The use of notes to provide context and dotted lines to distinguish background dependencies from primary interactions is effective and enhances comprehension. Despite its architectural inaccuracies, the diagram's layout and presentation make it easy to read and identify the depicted components and their intended, albeit flawed, connections.

samples.title: labhire / openhands/claude-sonnet-4-6

project_context.introduction:
### Product Scope

LabHire is a research internship information integration platform for university teachers and students. It supports research internship project publication, recommendation, retrieval, application, management, mutual evaluation, personal information, resumes, mentor search, community communication, internship referrals, and management-side review. The system scope is limited to university research internship information and community referral posts, and does not include social enterprise recruitment information or headhunting advertisements.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for developers, testers, project managers, customer representatives, and project-related personnel such as marketing, operations, and maintenance staff.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Performance | The system shall provide timely feedback for any user input. | For any user input, the system gives feedback within 1 second. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Robustness | The system shall remain normally operational under all possible user inputs. | It does not crash because of abnormal input and provides timely feedback for erroneous operations. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Access Control | The system shall ensure that each type of user can only obtain and modify information within their permissions. | Mentors can only view resumes of students selected for their projects; students cannot view each other's resumes; group members can view basic information but cannot view resumes. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Data Confidentiality | The system shall protect login, registration, and chat data. | User login/registration and chat content must be encrypted; the specification does not specify an encryption algorithm. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-005 | Abuse Prevention | The system shall restrict abnormal login and operation behavior. | Supports login and operation frequency limits based on IP and user behavior, graphical verification codes, SMS verification codes, and abnormal behavior detection. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Auditability | The system shall support back-end risk-control logs and behavior audit. | Risk-control logs and behavior audit modules support manual analysis and alerting. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-007 | Usability | The system shall allow most new users to naturally find functional modules and learn how to use them. | Usage instructions are provided to guide the remaining users. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-008 | User Interface Quality | The system interface shall improve efficiency and friendliness around user roles. | The student side may be more attractive; the mentor side should be concise, clear, and aesthetically pleasing; input/output methods, interaction methods, and functional distribution should support work efficiency. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-009 | Correctness | System operation and presentation logic shall conform to functional requirement goals. | Key functional flows are consistent with the functional requirements in this SRS. | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | Clarity | System descriptions shall be clear and functional boundaries shall be clearly divided. | Requirements, pages, and module responsibilities are not confused. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-011 | Extensibility | The system shall reduce coupling between components and support extension. | Component coupling is reduced, and new functions do not require broad changes to existing modules. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-012 | Browser Compatibility | The system shall be normally accessible through mainstream browsers. | Chrome, Firefox, or Microsoft Edge can access core functions normally. | High | Test |
| NFR-013 | Portability | The system shall support browsing on multiple device types while maintaining consistent page logic and functions. | Core page logic and functions are consistent between mobile and desktop. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Legal Compliance | User-published content displayed by the system shall comply with relevant laws. | Violating posts or comments can be reviewed and deleted, and displayed content complies with relevant laws. | High | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | The system scope is limited to university research internship information and community internship referrals, excluding social enterprise recruitment and headhunting advertisements. |
| C-002 | Registered users shall complete student or mentor identity certification through university email domains and verification codes. |
| C-003 | Enterprise-certified students must pass enterprise email verification before publishing internship referral posts. |
| C-004 | Administrator account passwords are directly assigned by the back-end database, and the system has no administrator registration entry. |
| C-005 | Manual identity review shall be completed by administrators within 72 hours. |
| C-006 | Student resume upload supports formats such as PDF, with maximum file size 10 MB. |
| C-007 | Community posts are limited to within 400 words and support text formats such as HTML and Markdown. |
| C-008 | Mobile devices shall satisfy quad-core 1.5 GHz CPU, 1 GB RAM + 4 GB ROM, Android 7.0 or iOS 8.0 and above, and WeChat 6.5.6 and above. |
| C-009 | Desktop devices shall satisfy at least 2 GB memory, broadband network, Windows 7 or Mac OS X 10.9 and above; Chrome, Firefox, or Microsoft Edge is recommended. |
| C-010 | The system and requirements documents shall follow GB/T 8566-2007, GB/T 8567-2006, and GB/T 9385-2008, and Python code shall follow PEP8. |
| C-011 | Display of user-published content must comply with relevant laws. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Mobile hardware | Quad-core 1.5 GHz CPU, 1 GB RAM + 4 GB ROM. |

samples.title: s000058_3ebb884d / metagpt-custom/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS defines software requirements for the `macpro-appian-connector` repository. It covers the repository behaviors supported by the reviewed project materials, including Appian-to-BigMAC data transfer support, connector configuration and runtime maintenance, AWS stage listing, service event alerting, and deployment metrics collection.

### Product scope
This repository supports transfer of Appian data changes to CMS BigMAC and related operations management. The system shall support:
- listing currently running stages in the current AWS account and region,
- configuring and restarting connector definitions for a target service,
- managing data-transfer-related topic and connector operations,
- routing matching service events through AWS event routing to an SNS notification topic,
- supporting manually maintained SNS subscription targets,
- collecting deployment metrics from GitHub Actions workflow runs, and
- collecting pull request merge-time metrics for a branch.

### Intended audience
This document is intended for:
- developers onboarding to or extending the repository,
- operators running repository workflows in AWS,
- maintainers managing connector definitions and operational metrics,
- testers validating documented repository behaviors.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Operability | AWS-account stage listing shall operate only after onboarding completion and AWS CLI credential setup. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | Deployability / maintainability | SNS subscription membership management shall remain decoupled from deployment so users can be added or removed without redeployment. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-003 | Compatibility | CI/CD-related repository workflows shall use GitHub Actions. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-004 | Scalability | GitHub metrics collection shall support paginated retrieval with `per_page: 100` and aggregate all returned pages before computing metrics. | Medium | Test |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | Stage-listing execution is constrained to the current AWS account and region. |
| C-002 | Stage-listing requires prior onboarding completion. |
| C-003 | Stage-listing requires AWS CLI credentials to be obtained and set. |
| C-004 | The repository uses GitHub Actions as its CI/CD tool. |
| C-005 | SNS subscription services are managed manually rather than by deployment automation. |
| C-006 | Deployment metrics are constrained to GitHub workflow runs for `deploy.yml`. |
| C-007 | PR metrics are constrained to closed pull requests for a specified base branch. |

### Operating environment
The system operates in:
- an AWS account with AWS CLI credentials available,
- a terminal-based local environment for onboarding and script execution,
- GitHub Actions as the CI/CD tool,
- AWS services including EventBridge and SNS for alert routing, and
- GitHub API access for workflow-run and pull-request metrics.

### Software/API interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| AWS CLI | Stage-listing operations shall rely on AWS CLI credentials being set in the current environment. |
| AWS event routing | The system shall receive matching service events through AWS event-routing rules defined with event filtering criteria. |
| AWS SNS | The system shall publish matching routed events to an SNS topic authorized for event-routing invocation. |
| Connector management library | Connector configuration shall invoke connector put, delete, and restart operations for a target cluster and service. |
| GitHub Actions API | Deployment metrics shall query workflow runs for `deploy.yml`. |
| GitHub Pull Requests API | Pull request metrics shall query closed pull requests for a specified base branch. |

### Communication interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Event delivery | Matching events shall flow from AWS event routing to SNS when the configured rule pattern matches. |
| GitHub API pagination | Metrics collection shall aggregate paginated GitHub API responses. |

### Assumptions and dependencies
- Users complete onboarding before running stage-listing procedures.
- AWS CLI credentials must be obtained and set before AWS stage-listing operations.
- Connector operations depend on configured `cluster` and `service` environment values and a defined connector set.
- Deployment metrics depend on GitHub workflow-run and pull-request data being available through paginated GitHub API access.

samples.title: Indoor_Fitness_App / openhands/gpt-5

project_context.introduction:
### Product Scope

Indoor Fitness App is an indoor exercise and health application based on pose estimation. It targets exercise scenarios with available activity space, such as homes, offices, gyms, and outdoor playgrounds. The system provides professional posture evaluation, intelligent training plan recommendation, training poster and professional report generation, and exercise screen casting. Its goal is to let users receive guidance similar to a personal trainer during indoor exercise.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for application users, developers, testers, architects, and project stakeholders. The specification explicitly expects users and developers to fully understand the software requirements.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | User Satisfaction | The final user survey satisfaction for the system shall reach the target value. | Satisfaction reaches above 90%. | Medium | Analysis |
| NFR-002 | Pose Recognition Accuracy | Under normal scenarios, system pose recognition shall achieve high accuracy. | When lighting, background, person size are normal and no occlusion or missing parts exist, pose recognition accuracy is above 95%. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Occlusion Robustness | Under occlusion or missing-part scenarios, the system shall maintain recognition accuracy for visible parts. | Accuracy for unoccluded and non-missing parts is above 90%; accuracy for occluded parts reaches 50%. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Model Runtime Performance | Pose estimation model runtime speed shall satisfy real-time evaluation needs. | Model runtime speed reaches above 20 fps. | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | Pose Evaluation Accuracy | When pose estimation has no errors, user posture evaluation shall achieve high accuracy. | Posture evaluation accuracy is above 95%. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Pose Error Tolerance | When pose estimation contains errors, the evaluation algorithm shall identify errors and maintain accuracy. | The algorithm can identify errors most of the time and guarantee accuracy no lower than 95%; "most of the time" must be quantified in the test plan. | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Body-Proportion Robustness | Posture similarity estimation shall reduce the impact of user body shape and body proportion on scoring. | The specification only requires "eliminating the impact to a certain extent" and does not provide a quantitative threshold. | Medium | Analysis |
| NFR-008 | Recommendation Latency | Recommended plan generation shall meet the response-time constraint. | Each recommended plan is generated within 1 second. | High | Test |
| NFR-009 | Report Generation Latency | Exercise report generation shall meet the response-time constraint. | Exercise report generation time does not exceed 3 seconds. | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | Casting Video Latency | Casting video stream transmission latency shall meet real-time display requirements. | Video stream transmission latency between devices does not exceed 100 ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-011 | Casting Command Latency | Response latency after the Android server sends a command to the client shall meet real-time control requirements. | Android client response latency does not exceed 50 ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-012 | Data Leakage Protection | The system shall take leakage-protection measures to prevent user data leakage. | Leakage risk is reduced through clipboard control, drag-and-drop control, screen copy/screenshot control, memory theft control, and related techniques. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-013 | SQL Injection Protection | The system shall prevent SQL injection. | Variable data types and formats are checked, special symbols are filtered, variables are bound, and precompiled statements are used. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-014 | Portability | The system shall support a certain degree of module portability. | It adopts a modular design concept, and modules rely on interfaces for data exchange. | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | A precondition for professional evaluation is that the user's mobile phone supports video recording at 30 fps or above. |
| C-002 | Exercise screen casting requires the current Android device and searched Android device to be in the same LAN. |
| C-003 | Server hardware shall satisfy CPU Pentium 900M, recommended Pentium 4 1.2G, and at least 256 MB RAM. |
| C-004 | PC hardware shall satisfy CPU Pentium 133M or higher and at least 64 MB RAM. |
| C-005 | Mobile hardware shall reach an octa-core 2.45 GHz CPU and 4 GB RAM + 32 GB ROM or above. |
| C-006 | The PC operating system shall be Windows. |
| C-007 | The mobile operating system shall be Android 8.0 or iOS 11.0 and above; the casting target is limited to Android devices in the specification. |
| C-008 | User exercise location is not restricted to a specific place, but activity space must be available. |
| C-009 | The system shall prepare and verify software requirements according to the software requirements, testing, life-cycle, and measurement standards listed in the specification. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Usage scenario | Available 24 hours a day, with no restriction on usage time, duration, or location as long as activity space is available. |
| Server hardware | CPU Pentium 900M, recommended Pentium 4 1.2G; at least 256 MB RAM. |
| PC hardware | CPU Pentium 133M or higher; at least 64 MB RAM. |
| Mobile hardware | Octa-core 2.45 GHz CPU, 4 GB RAM + 32 GB ROM or above. |
| PC software | Windows operating system. |
| Mobile software | Android 8.0 or iOS 11.0 and above. |

### Software/API Interfaces

| Interface | Requirement Summary |
| --- | --- |
| Camera capture interface | The App uses a fixed mobile phone camera to monitor body posture in real time and capture video during exercise. |
| Posture evaluation processing interface | The terminal device feeds back skeleton extraction information in real time and compares it with standard skeleton movements for scoring. |
| Server-side processing interface | Plan recommendation and poster/report material collection and processing involve server-side participation. |
| Inter-module data exchange interface | The project adopts a modular design concept, and modules rely on interfaces for data exchange. |
| SQL data access interface | The specification requires SQL injection prevention but does not specify the database product, table structure, or access protocol. |

### Communication Interfaces

| Communication | Requirement Summary |
| --- | --- |
| Casting video stream | The
...[truncated]

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
' --- Configuration & Styles ---
skinparam ranksep 50
skinparam nodesep 40
skinparam packageStyle rectangle
left to right direction

' Styling for different layers
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor<<client>> #e1f5fe
    BorderColor<<client>> #01579b
    
    BackgroundColor<<native>> #bbdefb
    BorderColor<<native>> #0d47a1
    
    BackgroundColor<<react>> #c8e6c9
    BorderColor<<react>> #1b5e20
    
    BackgroundColor<<bridge>> #00acc1
    BorderColor<<bridge>> #006064
    FontColor<<bridge>> white
    
    BackgroundColor<<infra>> #f5f5f5
    BorderColor<<infra>> #212121
    BorderStyle<<infra>> dashed
}

' --- Diagram Definitions ---

' Layer 1: Client Layer
package "Client / Platforms" as ClientLayer <<client>> {
    rectangle "Mobile Device" as Mobile <<client>>
    rectangle "Desktop PC" as Desktop <<client>>
    rectangle "Web Browser" as Web <<client>>
    rectangle "Android OS" as Android <<client>>
    rectangle "iOS / Apple" as iOS <<client>>
    rectangle "Smart Watch / Wearables" as IoT <<client>>
}

' Layer 2: Native Module Layer
package "Native Modules" as NativeLayer <<native>> {
    rectangle "Camera Call" as Camera <<native>>
    rectangle "Model Loading / TensorFlow" as ModelLoad <<native>>
    rectangle "Linear Algebra Library" as LinearAlgebra <<native>>
    rectangle "Voice Library Call" as Voice <<native>>
    rectangle "GPU Computing" as GPU <<native>>
    rectangle "Screen Recording" as ScreenRec <<native>>
    rectangle "Socket Communication" as Socket <<native>>
    rectangle "Data Processing" as DataProc <<native>>
    rectangle "Permission Requests" as Permissions <<native>>
}

' Layer 3: Cross-Platform Framework Layer
package "React Native Framework" as RNLayer <<react>> {
    rectangle "Native Module Bridge" as NativeBridge <<bridge>>
    rectangle "Data Caching" as DataCaching <<react>>
    rectangle "Server Access" as ServerAccess <<react>>
    rectangle "Redux State Management" as Redux <<react>>
    rectangle "Interface Packaging" as APIPkg <<react>>
    rectangle "Data Interception" as DataIntercept <<react>>
    rectangle "End-to-End Communication" as P2P <<react>>
}

' Sidebar: Infrastructure
package "Infrastructure & Support" as InfraLayer <<infra>> {
    rectangle "Data Exchange" as DataExchange <<infra>>
    rectangle "Performance Optimization" as Performance <<infra>>
    rectangle "Stability Construction" as Stability <<infra>>
}

' --- Relationships ---

ClientLayer --> NativeLayer
NativeLayer <--> NativeBridge

' Directing bridge to RN components
NativeBridge --- DataCaching
NativeBridge --- ServerAccess
NativeBridge --- Redux
NativeBridge --- APIPkg
NativeBridge --- DataIntercept
NativeBridge --- P2P

' Infrastructure interaction (dashed as per original)
NativeLayer ..> InfraLayer
RNLayer ..> InfraLayer

@enduml

samples.title: easylatex / openhands/qwen3-coder-480b-a35b-instruct

project_context.introduction:
### Product scope
EasyLaTex is an online system for converting mathematical formulas, tables, images, CSV/Excel/PDF files, and handwritten inputs into LaTex code. The system also provides real-time rendering, project/workspace management, collaboration, version control, comments, and export capabilities.

### Intended audience
- Product managers and requirements reviewers validating the EasyLaTex requirement set.
- Developers implementing user management, file processing, AI recognition, rendering, collaboration, and version control modules.
- Test engineers deriving verification scenarios from functional, interface, performance, compatibility, and security requirements.
- Architecture and evaluation agents that need structured requirements for architecture view generation.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Quality attribute | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | For common handwritten formulas, standard formula images, and regular CSV/Excel/PDF files, LaTex conversion accuracy shall be at least 88%. | Accuracy | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | The LaTex rendering module shall keep rendering latency below 2 seconds. | Performance | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Each user operation such as page navigation or loading shall respond within 2 seconds. | Performance | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | The system shall concurrently process at least 100 user requests. | Scalability | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | The system shall support normal access through Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari on PC and mobile browsers. | Compatibility / portability | High | Demonstration |
| NFR-006 | File input compatibility shall include at least JPG, PNG, PDF, CSV, and Excel `.xlsx`. | Compatibility | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Generated LaTex results shall be compatible with mainstream LaTex editors and compilation environments such as Overleaf and TeXstudio. | Compatibility | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-008 | User information including email, username, and password shall be encrypted during storage and transmission. | Security / privacy | High | Inspection |
| NFR-009 | Project collaboration shall enforce permission control with at least project owner and collaborator roles. | Security / access control | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | The system shall provide defenses against common Web vulnerabilities including SQL injection, XSS, and CSRF. | Security | High | Security test |
| NFR-011 | The user interface shall be concise and consistent with user operation habits so users can quickly get started. | Usability | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-012 | The system shall use modular design, standardized API design, complete interface documentation, unit tests, integration tests, and deployment documentation to support maintainability and iteration. | Maintainability | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Users are assumed to use modern browsers that support WebSocket technology. |
| C-002 | The AI model is assumed to reach at least 90% image recognition accuracy during design and development. |
| C-003 | Upload and download workflows depend on stable user network connections. |
| C-004 | Identity security depends on the existing user authentication mechanism. |
| C-005 | Minimum client hardware includes Intel Core i3 or equivalent CPU, 4 GB RAM, 20 GB free disk space, integrated graphics, and a 1024x768 display. |
| C-006 | Recommended client hardware includes Intel Core i5 or higher CPU, 8 GB RAM or more, 50 GB free disk space with SSD recommended, graphics acceleration, and 1920x1080 or higher display. |
| C-007 | Supported operating systems include Windows 10 or higher, macOS 10.14 or higher, and Ubuntu 18.04 or higher. |
| C-008 | Supported database management systems include MySQL 8.0 or higher, PostgreSQL 12 or higher, or another compatible relational database. |
| C-009 | Supported web servers include Apache HTTP Server 2.4 or higher and Nginx 1.18 or higher. |
| C-010 | Development/runtime dependencies include Python 3.8 or higher, Node.js 14.x or higher, and TeX Live, MiKTeX, or another compatible LaTex engine. |
| C-011 | External application interfaces use HTTP/REST API. |
| C-012 | The system shall follow privacy-protection regulations such as GDPR and other data-protection regulations. |

### Operating environment
The system is intended to run through modern browsers that support WebSocket. Target clients include Windows, macOS, and Linux environments, with Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge. Server-side and development dependencies include relational databases, web servers, Python, Node.js, and a compatible LaTex engine.

### Software/API interfaces
The system shall expose HTTP/REST APIs for user registration, login, user information management, project management, file upload, download, collaboration sharing, real-time preview, version management, and comments. It shall also provide internal APIs for user verification, file storage, image recognition, LaTex generation, rendering, collaboration management, and version control.

### Communication interfaces
The specification explicitly identifies HTTP/REST API for external module interactions. It also assumes WebSocket-capable browsers, supporting web interaction scenarios where real-time preview or collaborative updates may require browser communication support.

### Assumptions and dependencies
- Users operate through modern WebSocket-capable browsers.
- AI model image recognition accuracy is expected to reach at least 90%.
- User network connections are stable enough for uploads and downloads.
- Identity security depends on the existing user authentication mechanism.
- Initial user scale and storage demand are relatively small.

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
Its purpose is to provide structured input for architecture generation, test design, and requirements traceability for an AI music creation and sharing platform.

### Product Scope

The AI music creation and sharing platform is a Web system for music enthusiasts. After registration, users can use the platform for AI music creation, score generation, score upload and sharing, favorites, follows, recommendations, search, hot charts, and downloads. The system contains three modules: Web frontend, Web backend, and algorithm service. The algorithm service generates music from user-provided source files and includes online music generation and offline model iterative upgrade services.

This document uses the specification definitions of MQ, score, track seed, and midi music. A score is an abc-format plain-text file that can generate midi music. A track seed is the beginning part of an abc score and is less than 10 seconds long. Midi music is a music file playable by midi-capable players.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for customers, product managers, developers, testers, and downstream architecture generation and requirements review workflows. Target end users are music enthusiasts, especially users interested in AI music creation, music sharing, and novel approaches to music creation.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Performance | Average account registration latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Performance | Average login latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Performance | Average score favorite latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Performance | Average follow-user latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | Performance | Average score upload latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 3000ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Performance | Average model favorite latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-007 | Performance | Average Web-side score generation latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-008 | Performance | Average score download latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 3000ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-009 | Performance | Average recommendation retrieval latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 500ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | Performance | Average score search latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 1000ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-011 | Performance | Average hot-chart viewing latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-012 | Performance | Algorithm training for generation models shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-013 | Performance | Algorithm score generation shall satisfy throughput and latency requirements. | Throughput 5ops, average latency <= 1min. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Performance | Classifying all scores shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-015 | Performance | Song recommendation shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-016 | Scalability | The Web system shall support the preset concurrent access throughput. | Concurrent access throughput is no less than 2000QPS. | High | Test |
| NFR-017 | Scalability | The Web system shall support transaction change throughput. | Transaction changes are no less than 100TPS. | High | Test |
| NFR-018 | Usability | System interactions shall be suitable for music production beginners. | Interactions are simple and understandable and do not require professional function design. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-019 | Usability | The system shall provide user instructions, prompts, and guidance. | Comprehensive instructions, prompts, and guidance exist to help users get started quickly. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-020 | Security | The gateway shall isolate internal and external network boundaries. | Internal systems are not exposed externally due to loose security configuration. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-021 | Reliability | Important data shall be stored in multiple replicas. | Important data is stored in 3 replicas. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-022 | Availability | The system shall remain available under partial failures. | The system remains usable when some internal nodes are upgraded, crash, or experience a certain level of network partition. | High | Test |
| NFR-023 | Maintainability | Code shall be readable. | Code follows naming conventions, and names correspond to actual meaning. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-024 | Maintainability | Code shall avoid excessive coupling. | Code supports later iterative optimization, extension of existing functions, and addition of new functions. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-025 | Load Balancing | The system shall maintain stable response and reasonable resource utilization under high access volume. | Load balancing technology is provided when user access volume is high. | High | Test |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Frontend languages are HTML, CSS, and JavaScript; the frontend framework is Bootstrap. |
| C-002 | Backend language is Golang; backend framework is Gin. |
| C-003 | Webpage response time shall be within 1 second. |
| C-004 | Music generation model training time shall be within 2 hours. |
| C-005 | The algorithm model shall be able to generate enough varieties of music styles. |
| C-006 | Generated music quality shall be above the baseline. |
| C-007 | Algorithm model response time shall be controlled within 1 minute. |
| C-008 | Product interfaces shall reflect user friendliness and have a certain degree of aesthetics. |
| C-009 | Project R&D shall strictly follow the project management process to avoid phase delays affecting later development. |
| C-010 | System hardware environment consists of 4 virtual machines, each with CPU 4 * 2400MHz, 8GB memory, and 200GB storage. |
| C-011 | System software environment includes Ubuntu 18.04 LTS, Hadoop 3.2.2, RabbitMQ 3.8.14, etcd 3.4.15, and Nginx 1.19.8. |
| C-012 | Communication protocols are HTTPS and TCP/IP. |
| C-013 | Task metadata must conform to database constraints and limit task size. |
| C-014 | When task submission fails, erroneous data needs to be automatically skipped and retried. |
| C-015 | All requirements listed in use-case diagrams are planned for the current version and do not need to be assigned to future versions. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Hardware | 4 virtual machines, each with CPU 4 * 2400MHz, 8GB memory, and 200GB storage. |
| Operating system | Ubuntu 18.04 LTS. |
| Software interfaces | Apache Hadoop 3.2.2, RabbitMQ 3.8.14, etcd 3.4.15, and Nginx 1.19.8. |
| Communication protocols | HTTPS and TCP/IP. |
| Frontend technology |

samples.title: ModelSharePlatform / mini-swe-agent/qwen3-coder-480b-a35b-instruct

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
The goal is to provide structured input for architecture generation, test design, and requirements tracing for the Deep Learning Model Sharing Platform.

### Product Scope

The Deep Learning Model Sharing Platform targets deep learning enthusiasts and professionals, providing a one-stop cloud service for communication, learning, creation, and application of deep learning models. The platform supports a Q&A community, blog sharing, model library management, dataset management, online invocation experiences, paid model sales revenue, follow relationships, and administrator governance.

This document uses the specification terminology for general users, administrators, Q&A community, blogs, models, datasets, online invocation, paid sales, SQL, MySQL, TypeScript, UI, Spring Boot, and Vue.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for platform general users, administrators, developers, testers, requirements reviewers, and later architecture generation and acceptance testing workflows. General users are the primary platform users, and administrators manage platform-related data.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Metric / Acceptance | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Security | The system shall use multiple security mechanisms to prevent malicious operations from affecting the platform. | Malicious operations cannot damage core functions or data. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Security | The system shall prevent common injection attacks. | Common injection attacks are blocked. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Security | The system shall securely authenticate users and allow administrators to restrict functions for violating users. | After authentication, administrators can delete illegal information or cancel violating user accounts. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Consistency | The system shall keep server data and front-end data consistent. | Server data and front-end data are consistent within a 3-second time interval. | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | Usability | The system interface shall follow modern aesthetics, maintain a consistent style, and use a minimalist theme. | Main interfaces are stylistically consistent and match the minimalist theme. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-006 | Usability | System operation entries shall be obvious, and access cost shall match usage frequency. | High-frequency operation entries are easier to access; when there are too many interfaces, classification aggregation or quick search is supported. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-007 | Reliability | The system shall meet a long-running target when the hardware and software runtime environments remain unchanged. | Expected time to failure is greater than 1 year. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-008 | Availability | System maintenance windows shall be scheduled during low-impact periods. | 1:00 to 4:00 may be used as the system maintenance time. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-009 | Capacity | The system shall support the required scale of user data storage. | Stores data for more than 10,000 users. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-010 | Performance | The system shall support application request throughput. | Supports 1,000 application requests per second. | High | Test |
| NFR-011 | Recoverability | The system shall be repaired promptly when it crashes because the data load exceeds the processing limit. | Repair is completed within 1 hour. | High | Demonstration |
| NFR-012 | Internationalization | The system interface shall support switching between Chinese and English. | Users can switch between Chinese and English interfaces. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-013 | Maintainability | The system shall have stability and partial self-troubleshooting ability without manual intervention. | Faults can be automatically identified or partially handled by the system. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-014 | Extensibility | The system shall provide extensible interfaces through object orientation, abstraction, and modular code. | Later development can extend business requirements through interfaces. | Medium | Analysis |
| NFR-015 | Recommendation Quality | The system shall add intelligent recommendation algorithms on top of traditional recommendation algorithms. | Recommended model content better matches users' personalized needs. | Medium | Test |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Reference technologies include MySQL, Spring Boot, Vue, TypeScript, SQL, and UI-related technologies. |
| C-002 | Runtime hardware CPU shall use x86-64 or ARM architecture. |
| C-003 | The runtime environment shall have at least 4 GB of memory. |
| C-004 | The runtime environment shall have at least 30 GB of available disk space. |
| C-005 | The runtime environment shall be all modern browsers. |
| C-006 | The system shall not support Internet Explorer. |
| C-007 | The development deadline is limited to completion in early May. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| CPU | x86-64 or ARM architecture. |
| Memory | At least 4 GB. |
| Disk | At least 30 GB of available space. |
| Browser | All modern browsers. |
| Unsupported Browser | Internet Explorer is not supported. |

### Software/API Interfaces

The specification lists MySQL, Spring Boot, Vue, TypeScript, SQL, UI-related terms, and reference materials, but does not explicitly define API paths, interface protocols, database schemas, upload/download protocols, model online invocation protocols, or payment interfaces.

### Communication Interfaces

The specification does not explicitly define HTTP, HTTPS, WebSocket, RPC, message queues, or third-party payment communication interfaces. It can only be determined that the system runs in modern browsers and does not support Internet Explorer.

### Assumptions and Dependencies

| ID | Assumption / Dependency |
| --- | --- |
| AD-001 | Core capabilities such as models, datasets, Q&A, blogs, follows, and purchases depend on the general user being logged in. |
| AD-002 | Administrator governance capabilities depend on the user being logged in as an administrator. |
| AD-003 | Model recommendation depends on user browsing history and registration information. |
| AD-004 | Dataset download, deletion, and similar operations depend on corresponding dataset information existing in the database. |
| AD-005 | The specification does not define the model review process, payment channel, API schema, or database table structure; later design must not assume these details are complete. |

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
' --- Configuration & Styles ---
skinparam topboxSelection true
skinparam Direction top to bottom
skinparam packageStyle rectangle
skinparam shadowing false

' Define Styles based on original ClassDefs
<style>
    .client {
        BackgroundColor #e1f5fe
        LineColor #01579b
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .service {
        BackgroundColor #e8f5e9
        LineColor #2e7d32
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .db {
        BackgroundColor #fff9c4
        LineColor #fbc02d
        LineThickness 2
    }
</style>

' --- Client Layer ---
package "Browser / Client Layer" as Browser_Layer {
    rectangle "Frontend Interface\n(Bootstrap-Vue)" as FE_Interface <<client>>
}

' --- Server Layer ---
package "Server Layer\n(Spring Boot)" as Server_Layer {
    
    package "Core Modules" as Core_Modules {
        ' Layout is TB within the package
        rectangle "Account Management Module" as AccountMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Q&A Community Module" as QAMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Blog Sharing Module" as BlogMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Model Management Module" as ModelMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Dataset Management Module" as DatasetMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Following Management Module" as FollowMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Admin Module" as AdminMod <<service>>
    }
}

' --- Database Layer ---
package "Database Layer\n(MySQL)" as Database_Layer {
    database "User Data" as UserDB <<db>>
    database "Q&A Community Data" as QADB <<db>>
    database "Blog Data" as BlogDB <<db>>
    database "Model Data" as ModelDB <<db>>
    database "Dataset Data" as DatasetDB <<db>>
    database "Following Data" as FollowDB <<db>>
}

' --- Relationships ---
FE_Interface --> Core_Modules
Core_Modules --> Database_Layer

@enduml

completeness.reasoning: The architecture comprehensively addresses all functional domains specified in the SRS by providing dedicated microservices for each (User, Q&A, Blog, Model, etc.). It also effectively addresses key non-functional requirements like performance (NFR-010), scalability (NFR-009), and security (NFR-001) through the choice of a microservices pattern with an API Gateway. Data requirements and technology constraints are fully accommodated.

faithfulness.reasoning: The architecture is mostly faithful to the requirements. It correctly infers the need for essential components like an API Gateway, Cloud Storage for large files, and an external Payment Gateway, which are necessary to realize the specified requirements. However, it introduces a 'Mobile App' component which is not mentioned or implied anywhere in the SRS, making it a partially unfaithful representation.

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The design is exceptionally rational. It employs a standard and robust microservices architecture that is well-suited to the system's requirements. The separation of concerns is clear across layers and services. The distinction between storing structured data in MySQL and large files in Cloud Storage demonstrates sound architectural judgment. samples.title: TextAnnotationSystem / openhands/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### Product Scope

ITAS is a web-based system for processing and analyzing large volumes of text data. It contains two connected subsystems:

- Text annotation subsystem: supports paragraph/entity annotation for scientific literature and provides annotation results to the mining subsystem.
- Intelligent mining subsystem: uses large language models and natural language processing techniques to identify and extract predefined information from unannotated text.

### Intended Audience

The intended audience includes ITAS developers, testers, system annotation administrators, data annotators, researchers, and the expert group referenced by the source design document.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Accuracy/Consistency | The system shall use preset annotation rules/templates to improve the accuracy and consistency of paragraph/entity annotation and extraction. | Must | Analysis |
| NFR-002 | Efficiency | The system shall support efficient processing and analysis of large text datasets; the specification does not provide a numeric throughput target. | Must | Analysis |
| NFR-003 | Responsiveness | The system shall use asynchronous front-end/back-end interaction and cache support to improve perceived response speed; the specification does not provide a latency threshold. | Should | Test |
| NFR-004 | Scalability/Extensibility | The system shall use service discovery, configuration management, load balancing, and microservice support to enable service extension and deployment scaling. | Should | Inspection |
| NFR-005 | Security | The system shall support user authentication and authorization through the specified gateway/security stack. | Must | Test |
| NFR-006 | Portability | The system shall use Docker containerization to preserve environment consistency and deployment portability. | Should | Inspection |
| NFR-007 | Usability | The system shall provide rich, intuitive web user interfaces using the specified Vue and Ant Design technology choices. | Should | Demonstration |
| NFR-008 | Maintainability | The system shall use the specified layered web architecture, ORM/data access tooling, and development workflow to support manageable development and testing. | Should | Inspection |
| NFR-009 | Asynchronous Processing | The system shall process asynchronous tasks such as file conversion through RabbitMQ to reduce synchronous workflow delay. | Should | Test |
| NFR-010 | Reliability/Deployment Stability | The system shall deploy on the specified cloud/runtime infrastructure to provide a stable operating environment; the specification does not define availability targets. | Should | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint | Type |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C-001 | The front end shall use Vue.js/Vue 3.0 with vue-router, Pinia, Ant Design/Ant-Design-Vue, and Axios as specified. | Technology |
| C-002 | The back end shall use Spring Boot/Spring Cloud microservice architecture where specified. | Technology |
| C-003 | The gateway/security stack shall use Spring Gateway and Spring Security for authentication and authorization. | Technology/Security |
| C-004 | Nginx shall act as reverse proxy for load balancing and request forwarding. | Deployment |
| C-005 | Mybatis/Mybatis Plus shall be used for database access/ORM operations. | Technology |
| C-006 | MySQL, Minio, and Redis shall be used for structured storage, object storage, and caching respectively. | Data platform |
| C-007 | BERT shall be used for paragraph model capability and GPT/GPT API shall be used for entity model/interface capability as specified. | ML/model |
| C-008 | RabbitMQ shall be used for asynchronous message queue processing such as file conversion. | Infrastructure |
| C-009 | Docker shall be used for containerized deployment and environment consistency. | Deployment |
| C-010 | Tencent Cloud is specified as the deployment/runtime environment and public access hosting platform. | Deployment |
| C-011 | The specification references GB/T 9385-2008 and GB/T 8567-2006 as documentation standards. | Standard |

### Operating Environment

The specification states a modern web environment with Vue-based front end, Spring-based back end, Nginx reverse proxy, MySQL, Minio, Redis, BERT/GPT model integration, RabbitMQ, Docker, and Tencent Cloud deployment.

### Software/API Interfaces

| API ID | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| API-001 | The annotation subsystem shall expose controller-level functions for annotator, file, label, login, paper, project, result export/return, tag, task, and user management. |
| API-002 | The mining subsystem shall expose functions for document upload, model selection, and information extraction. |
| API-003 | The system shall integrate with GPT API or a third-party ChatGPT-like interface for entity annotation or mining model invocation where specified. |
| API-004 | The system shall interact with MySQL for structured records, Minio for object storage, and Redis for cache access. |

### Communication Interfaces

| COM ID | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| COM-001 | The front end shall communicate asynchronously with the back end through Axios HTTP client calls. |
| COM-002 | Nginx shall provide reverse proxy, request forwarding, and load balancing responsibilities. |
| COM-003 | Spring Cloud shall support service discovery, configuration management, load balancing, and inter-service communication concerns. |
| COM-004 | RabbitMQ shall support message-queue-based asynchronous processing for workflows such as file conversion. |

### Assumptions and Dependencies

| ID | Statement |
| --- | --- |
| AD-001 | Annotation outputs are expected to support later intelligent mining and extraction workflows. |
| AD-002 | Third-party model/API availability, such as GPT API or ChatGPT-like interfaces, is required for the implemented mining model invocation approach. |
| AD-003 | Object storage is required for text/unstructured documents, while relational storage keeps structured records and document indexes. |
| AD-004 | RabbitMQ-backed asynchronous processing is used for tasks such as file conversion. |

samples.title: asp / openhands/gpt-5

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS defines requirements for a requirements review checklist and review reporting artifact used for the "Zhiyan Tongjie" software requirements review.

### Product scope
The covered scope is limited to the review process and review artifact:
- defining review objectives and review basis,
- checking requirement completeness, understandability, model accuracy, and extensibility,
- recording requirement problems and improvement suggestions,
- scoring the review result and preserving reviewer metadata.

Business functions of the "Zhiyan Tongjie" platform are outside this SRS because the checklist does not specify them.

### Intended audience
- Requirements reviewers
- Product managers and analysts preparing review material
- Developers and testers consuming review findings
- Project stakeholders who need a concise review result
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Quality attribute | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | The review artifact shall make requirement completeness inspectable through explicit checks for core/basic functions, user needs, exceptions, and auxiliary functions. | Completeness | High | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | The review artifact shall make requirement understandability inspectable through explicit checks for clarity, ease of understanding, specificity, detail, and refinement. | Usability / clarity | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-003 | The review artifact shall make model quality inspectable through explicit checks for conformance, reasonableness, rigor, and accuracy. | Correctness | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-004 | The review artifact shall provide a quantitative review summary using four dimensions worth 10 points each and a total score out of 40. | Measurability | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | The review must use software requirements review and verification methods or strategies. |
| C-002 | The review must consider industry standards, product specifications, business specifications, and documentation specifications. |
| C-003 | Review scoring is constrained to four 10-point dimensions and a total score out of 40. |
| C-004 | Product business requirements are not defined by this checklist and must be obtained from other requirement artifacts. |

### Operating environment
No runtime operating environment is specified. The artifact is represented as a Markdown checklist and can be inspected or edited in a Markdown-capable environment.

### Software/API interfaces
No software or API interface is specified by the checklist.

### Communication interfaces
No communication interface is specified by the checklist.

### Assumptions and dependencies
- The requirements document under review exists before the checklist is applied.
- Reviewers have access to applicable industry standards, product specifications, business rules, and documentation norms.
- Product-specific functional requirements must be obtained from other requirement artifacts, not from this checklist.

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
' --- Configuration ---
top to bottom direction
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam packageStyle rectangle
skinparam componentStyle uml2

' --- Styling ---
skinparam rectangle<<ClientLayer>> {
    BackgroundColor #f9f
    BorderColor #333
}
skinparam rectangle<<NetworkLayer>> {
    BackgroundColor #b2ebf2
    BorderColor #00838f
}
skinparam rectangle<<ServiceLayer>> {
    BackgroundColor #c8e6c9
    BorderColor #2e7d32
}
skinparam database<<StorageLayer>> {
    BackgroundColor #fff9c4
    BorderColor #fbc02d
}

' --- Layer 1: Frontend / Client Layer ---
rectangle "Client Layer" <<ClientLayer>> {
    component [Client Terminal] as Client
    component [Cloud DNS] as DNS
}

' --- Layer 2: Network / Gateway Layer ---
rectangle "Network / Gateway Layer" <<NetworkLayer>> {
    rectangle "Machine 01" {
        component [LVS - Linux Virtual Server] as LVS01
        component [Nginx] as Nginx01
    }
    rectangle "Machine 02" {
        component [LVS - Linux Virtual Server] as LVS02
        component [Nginx] as Nginx02_A
        component [Nginx] as Nginx02_B
    }
    rectangle "Machine 03" {
        component [LVS - Linux Virtual Server] as LVS03
        component [Nginx] as Nginx03
    }
}

' --- Layer 3: Service / Backend Layer ---
rectangle "Service / Backend Layer" <<ServiceLayer>> {
    component [Django Service Clusters] as Django
    component [Socializing Service] as Social
    component [User Information Service] as UserInfo
    component [Literature Service] as Literature
    component [Back-office Management] as BackOffice
}

' --- Layer 4: Storage / Database Layer ---
rectangle "Storage / Database Layer" <<StorageLayer>> {
    database "Redis Cache" as Redis
    database "Elasticsearch" as ES
    database "MySQL Database" as MySQL
}

' --- Connection Relationships ---
Client --> DNS
Client --> LVS01
Client --> LVS02
Client --> LVS03

LVS01 --> Nginx01
LVS02 --> Nginx02_A
LVS02 --> Nginx02_B
LVS03 --> Nginx03

Nginx01 --> Django
Nginx02_A --> Django
Nginx02_B --> Django
Nginx03 --> Django

Django --> Social
Django --> UserInfo
Django --> Literature
Django --> BackOffice

Social --> Redis
Social --> ES
Social --> MySQL

UserInfo --> Redis
UserInfo --> ES
UserInfo --> MySQL

Literature --> Redis
Literature --> ES
Literature --> MySQL

BackOffice --> Redis
BackOffice --> ES
BackOffice --> MySQL

@enduml

faithfulness.reasoning: There are no unsupported elements or hallucinated features. Every component, artifact, and actor in the diagram is directly traceable to the provided SRS document.

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The architecture correctly models a static document (Markdown Checklist) as a package containing logical sections (components) and generated data records (artifacts). The interactions between users and these document sections are logically sound for a template-based review tool.

traceability.reasoning: Excellent traceability is achieved through explicit mapping notes that reference specific Constraint IDs (e.g., C-001 to C-004) and Data Requirement IDs (e.g., DR-003 to DR-005). Component names directly mirror the FR descriptions.

samples.title: s000036_b0b48363 / metagpt-custom/qwen3-coder-480b-a35b-instruct

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose
This Software Requirements Specification defines normalized requirements for HypnoLog Server, a Node.js/Express logging and visualization server. The system receives JSON log messages over HTTP, forwards them to browser clients through Socket.IO, validates incoming browser messages against the HypnoLog Data Object schema, and renders log objects in a browser UI through windows and visualizers.

### 1.2 Scope
The system scope includes:
- HTTP-based log submission from clients implemented in any language or environment capable of sending HTTP requests.
- A JSON logging payload model called the HypnoLog Data Object.
- A Node.js/Express server started through npm or CLI entry points.
- A logging route for JSON POST submissions.
- Browser-based viewing through the server-hosted web UI.
- Socket.IO delivery from the server to connected browser clients.
- Browser-side schema validation and invalid-message wrapping.
- Window dispatch and visualizer dispatch for displaying received log objects.
- Type-based visualizer selection through `canDisplay` and `display` functions.

The system scope does not include explicit requirements for persistence, retention, deletion, privacy controls, authentication, authorization, or migration.

### 1.3 Intended Audience
- Maintainers of HypnoLog Server.
- Integrators building logging clients.
- Users viewing log data through the browser UI.
- Testers verifying HTTP submission, Socket.IO delivery, schema validation, and visualizer rendering.
- Architecture-generation tools consuming a clean SRS without review metadata.

### 1.4 Definitions and Abbreviations
| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| HypnoLog Data Object | JSON object representing one logged item. |
| Visualizer | Browser-side component that can display selected HypnoLog Data Objects. |
| Window | Browser-side display area that receives data and delegates rendering to visualizers. |
| Socket.IO | Real-time client/server communication library used by the browser and server. |
| Ajv | JSON Schema validator used by the browser client. |
| Logging producer | External client that sends log messages to the server over HTTP. |
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Quality attribute | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | The logging interface shall be compatible with clients from any language or environment capable of sending HTTP requests with JSON bodies. | Interoperability | High | Analysis |
| NFR-002 | The visualization UI shall operate in a standard browser. | Usability | High | Demonstration |
| NFR-003 | The browser communication requirement shall be expressed as Socket.IO real-time delivery and shall not depend on raw WebSocket-only transport. | Interoperability | High | Inspection |
| NFR-004 | The browser client shall validate received server messages and expose invalid messages to users as displayable error objects rather than silently discarding them. | Data integrity | Medium | Test |
| NFR-005 | The server shall parse JSON bodies up to the configured request size limit. | Robustness | Medium | Test |
| NFR-006 | The visualizer mechanism shall support extensibility by loading visualizers from configuration and by requiring a small `canDisplay`/`display` contract. | Modifiability | High | Inspection |
| NFR-007 | The system shall not claim persistence, retention, authentication, authorization, or privacy behavior unless those capabilities are separately implemented. | Scope control | High | Inspection |

## 8. Constraints

| ID | Constraint | Type |
|---|---|---|
| C-001 | Basic log submission is constrained to HTTP POST with JSON content. | Interface |
| C-002 | The primary logging endpoint is `/logger/in`. | Interface |
| C-003 | JSON submissions should set `Content-Type: application/json`. | Interface |
| C-004 | Browser delivery uses Socket.IO and must not be specified as raw WebSocket-only. | Communication |
| C-005 | The browser-side data model requires `data` and `type`; server-side route rejection is not asserted. | Data model |
| C-006 | Visualizers must implement `canDisplay` and `display`. | Extension contract |
| C-007 | Visualizer order matters because the first matching visualizer is used. | Rendering |
| C-008 | A user configuration file may override or supplement default visualizer configuration when loadable. | Configuration |
| C-009 | The default local server port is `7000`. | Runtime |
| C-010 | No explicit persistence, retention, deletion, authentication, authorization, or privacy-control behavior is specified. | Scope |

### 2.4 Operating Environment
| Environment aspect | Requirement context |
|---|---|
| Node.js runtime | The server is started with `node ./bin/www`, npm start, or CLI aliases. |
| Express application | HTTP routing, static file hosting, body parsing, and web UI delivery run through Express. |
| HTTP server | The Express app is hosted by a Node HTTP server. |
| Default port | The server listens on port `7000` when no command-line port is provided. |
| Configurable port | The server accepts a command-line port argument. |
| Browser client | The UI runs in a standard browser and loads JavaScript bundles. |
| Socket.IO | The server attaches Socket.IO to the HTTP server; browser clients connect with Socket.IO to the same origin. |
| JSON body parser | The server parses JSON bodies with a configured size limit of `50mb`. |
| Static web files | Browser assets are served from the public web directory. |

### 3.3 Communication Interfaces
| ID | Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|---|
| CI-001 | HTTP POST | Logging producers shall send JSON payloads to the logging endpoint using HTTP POST. |
| CI-002 | JSON request body | The server shall parse JSON request bodies before forwarding them to display clients. |
| CI-003 | Socket.IO delivery | The server shall deliver received data to connected browser clients through Socket.IO; the requirement shall not assume raw WebSocket-only transport. |
| CI-004 | Same-origin browser connection | Browser clients shall connect to the origin from which the UI page was loaded. |

### 2.5 Assumptions and Dependencies
| ID | Assumption or dependency |
|---|---|
| AD-001 | Logging producers can send HTTP POST requests. |
| AD-002 | JSON log submissions use `Content-Type: application/json`. |
| AD-003 | Browser users can access the server-hosted UI. |
| AD-004 | Socket.IO client and server libraries are available. |
| AD-005 | The browser can load configured visualizer modules. |
| AD-006 | Visualizers implement `canDisplay(obj)` and `display(obj, callback)`. |
| AD-007 | No server-side schema rejection is required unless later route-level validation is added. |
