samples.title: modelevolution / openhands/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### Product Scope

The Model Evolution Version Management Visualization Tool targets learners, contributors, and administrators of machine learning models. It visually displays model architecture, performance, and evolution relationships, and supports account management, model search and viewing, model upload and modification, model evolution relationship definition, and user management. The system especially targets models with multiple evolutionary versions, such as YOLO models, helping users understand model structures, source code, and evolution relationships.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for developers, testers, system users, and later architecture generation/review workflows. The specification explicitly uses the requirements document as the basis for design, implementation, and testing phases, and provides users with operation and permission baselines.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Concurrency | The system shall support concurrent online requests. | It can withstand simultaneous online requests from 50 users. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Throughput | The system shall meet throughput requirements for model modification and query requests. | 10 model modification requests per second and 50 query requests per second. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Response Time | Static pages shall respond quickly. | Static page response time does not exceed 1 second. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Processing Time | Adding a model to producing results shall be completed within the specified time. | Time from adding a model to producing results does not exceed 1 minute. | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | Reliability / Availability | The Web site shall run continuously without interruption. | Runs 7*24 hours, and unplanned downtime per year is no more than 30 hours. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-006 | Failover | The system shall quickly switch to a standby machine on failure. | Switches to a standby machine within 0.5 hours after failure. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-007 | Maintainability / Reliability | The system shall satisfy repairability and fault-free operation requirements. | Mean time to repair does not exceed 0.5 hours, and mean time between failures is no less than 4000 hours. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-008 | Consistency | When multiple administrators modify models simultaneously, the system shall keep final displayed content consistent. | No content conflict, confusion, or loss occurs. | High | Test |
| NFR-009 | Correctness | The system shall correctly display model structures and evolution relationships and return correct search results. | No exception occurs during user use; model structures and evolution relationships are displayed correctly; search returns correct results. | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | Authentication Security | User login shall use two-factor authentication. | OAuth 2.0 or a similar security protocol is used. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-011 | Data Encryption | Sensitive data shall be encrypted during transmission and storage. | TLS encryption is used and PCI DSS is satisfied. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-012 | Model Encryption | Models in the database shall be stored encrypted. | Symmetric encryption algorithms such as AES and DES are used. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-013 | Access Control | Before login, the system shall reject all actions that modify website content. | Before successful account login, users cannot upload/modify models or define evolution details. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Privilege Control | Non-administrator accounts shall not delete or modify any approved model. | Deletion/modification requests from non-administrators for approved models return rejection results. | High | Test |
| NFR-015 | Spoofing Protection | The system shall detect or block forged login data. | When accessed by an impersonated user, the system detects or blocks forged login data. | High | Test |
| NFR-016 | Tamper Protection | The system shall protect uploaded models and evolution details from arbitrary tampering or deletion. | Unauthorized tampering or deletion operations are rejected and do not affect data integrity. | High | Test |
| NFR-017 | Privacy Compliance | The system shall follow local privacy regulations to protect personal data. | A privacy policy is provided and user consent is obtained. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-018 | License Compliance | Third-party libraries, plugins, and tools shall comply with open-source licenses. | They do not infringe others' intellectual property rights. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-019 | Maintainability | When a system fault occurs, it shall be possible to quickly locate and resolve the problem. | Fault location and resolution processes are executable; code is easy to understand, modify, and enhance. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-020 | Code Consistency | Code in the same module shall be written in the same format. | Variable names are semantically related, structure and presentation are separated, HTML is used for structure, and CSS is used for layout and presentation. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-021 | Portability / Responsive UI | The Web interface shall adapt to different operating systems, browsers, and electronic devices. | It adjusts according to operating system, screen size, screen orientation, and similar environments, and Web content is clearly displayed. | High | Test |
| NFR-022 | Usability | Users shall clearly understand website functions and complete tasks according to prompts. | New users can complete tasks according to prompts; experienced users can complete tasks quickly and efficiently; detailed help documents are provided. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-023 | UI Aesthetics | System pages shall be coordinated, prominent, and easy to understand. | Navigation interfaces do not exceed 10; the main interface is prominent, side and bottom columns are clear, and long pages have bottom navigation. | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | The system adopts a B/S architecture, and users access a specific website through a browser. |
| C-002 | The hardware environment shall satisfy x86-64 or ARM CPU, at least 4 GB memory, at least 20 GB available disk, and at least 5 Mbps bandwidth. |
| C-003 | The operating system environment shall support Windows 10 or above 64-bit or Ubuntu 16.04 or above 64-bit, and satisfy cross-platform constraints such as Windows, macOS, and Linux. |
| C-004 | The runtime environment includes mainstream browsers such as Firefox, Chrome, and 360 Browser. |
| C-005 | There are no hardware interfaces. |
| C-006 | Software interfaces include Python libraries and browser Console. |
| C-007 | Communication interfaces include HTTP, HTTPS, TCP, IP, FTP, SMTP, and UDP. |
| C-008 | The databases are N
...[truncated]

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
' Directional setting (Top to Down)
top to bottom direction

' Styling for different layers
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor<<Frontend>> #e1f5fe
    BorderColor<<Frontend>> #01579b
    
    BackgroundColor<<Presentation>> #f3e5f5
    BorderColor<<Presentation>> #4a148c
    
    BackgroundColor<<Backend>> #fff3e0
    BorderColor<<Backend>> #e65100
}

skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor<<Storage>> #e8f5e9
    BorderColor<<Storage>> #1b5e20
}

' Layers / Subgraphs
package "Frontend Layer" {
    rectangle "React Framework" as React <<Frontend>>
    rectangle "React-Router: Page Navigation" as Router <<Frontend>>
    rectangle "Netron: Node Drawing Features" as NetronFE <<Frontend>>
}

package "Data Presentation Layer" {
    rectangle "RESTful API Design" as RESTAPI <<Presentation>>
    rectangle "Netron: Graph Representation Language" as GraphLang <<Presentation>>
}

package "Backend Layer" {
    rectangle "Flask: Backend Framework" as Flask <<Backend>>
    rectangle "SQLAlchemy: ORM Framework" as SQLAlchemy <<Backend>>
    rectangle "Netron: Model Conversion & Parsing" as NetronBE <<Backend>>
}

package "Storage / Database Layer" {
    database "MySQL: Relational Database" as MySQL <<Storage>>
    database "Neo4j: Graph Database" as Neo4j <<Storage>>
}

' Relationships
React --> Router
React --> NetronFE
React --> RESTAPI
NetronFE --> GraphLang
RESTAPI --> Flask
GraphLang --> Flask
Flask --> SQLAlchemy
Flask --> NetronBE
SQLAlchemy --> MySQL
Flask --> Neo4j

@enduml

samples.title: retool / direct/claude-sonnet-4-6

project_context.introduction:
### Product Scope

ReTool is a requirements management tool for continuously evolving software projects. It supports user management, project management, and requirements management. Requirements management includes requirement itemization, requirement structuring, requirement conflict detection, common requirement identification, requirement association analysis, and forward tracing from requirements to design modules and test cases. The system adopts a front-end/back-end separated mode; the client uses Vue.js and the server uses a microservice architecture.

This document follows the terminology definitions in the requirements specification for microservices, Flask, NLP, PyTorch, Vue.js, and MongoDB. The specification does not provide REST paths, database collection structures, or message protocol details.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for ReTool developers, testers, project managers, project leaders, ordinary project members, system administrators, requirements reviewers, and later architecture generation and acceptance testing workflows.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Performance | The time from user click to first interface display shall be controlled. | Time from click to first interface display does not exceed 1 second. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Reliability | The system mean time to failure shall satisfy the threshold. | Mean time to failure > 720 h. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-003 | Reliability | The system mean time to repair shall satisfy the threshold. | Mean time to repair < 30 min. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-004 | Reliability | The data loss rate after system failure shall conform to the specification threshold. | Data loss rate after failure < 99%; this threshold is preserved according to the original source wording and should be confirmed during review. | Medium | Analysis |
| NFR-005 | Reliability | The system shall have automatic release and fault removal capability after errors occur. | After fault injection, the system can automatically recover or enter a recoverable state. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Reliability | User-uploaded information shall be persisted quickly. | Time for saving uploaded user information to disk < 1 s. | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Resource Usage | System memory usage shall be controlled during runtime. | Memory usage < 80%. | High | Test |
| NFR-008 | Resource Usage | System CPU usage shall be controlled during runtime. | CPU usage < 80%. | High | Test |
| NFR-009 | Reliability | The database shall be backed up periodically and support offsite disaster recovery. | The database is backed up periodically, with three-copy offsite disaster recovery backup. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-010 | Security | The login system shall comply with Web security specifications. | Login Web interfaces pass security specification checks. | High | Test |
| NFR-011 | Privacy | The system shall protect user privacy information and personalized information. | Privacy and personalized data are protected by access control and protection measures. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-012 | Security | The system shall identify illegal user inputs and malicious behavior. | Illegal inputs and malicious behavior are identified and recorded. | High | Test |
| NFR-013 | Security | The system shall prevent requirement input from causing database injection attacks. | Requirement input content is restricted and validated, and injection attacks are blocked. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Security | Front-end code shall reduce the risk that captured request packets lead to requirement information copying or tampering. | Front-end code uses protection strategies such as minimization and mixing/obfuscation. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-015 | Security | The system shall ensure functional module security. | Network transmission, data storage, file storage, and open Web interfaces have security measures. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-016 | Security | The system shall establish permissions for developers and sensitive commands. | Developer and sensitive command permissions can be checked. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-017 | Maintainability | Software structure and documents shall support performance adjustment. | The system has good structure and complete documents, and performance adjustment points can be identified. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-018 | Maintainability | Documents shall be clear, readable, and uniformly written. | Documents pass standardization and readability checks. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-019 | Maintainability | Server deployment and logs shall support issue tracing. | Deployment process is concise and standardized, and logs are persistently saved, readable, and traceable. | High | Test |
| NFR-020 | Maintainability | The product shall reserve upgrade interfaces and upgrade space. | Design descriptions reflect upgrade interfaces and extension space. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-021 | Usability | Interaction design shall reduce user thinking and operation costs. | Users can obtain target information in three clicks or fewer, and important information is presented on the main interface or in obvious locations. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-022 | Usability | The system shall reduce user input and support personalized experience. | Selection lists and search input autocomplete are used, personalized information is recorded, and direct login is supported on revisits. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-023 | UI Design | Interface design shall be unified, adaptive, and compliant with Web design specifications. | Flat style is unified, screen size is adaptive, visual effect is harmonious, icons are concise and intuitive, and colors and text satisfy the specification requirements. | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Requirement changes must strictly follow the requirement change process, and the requirements specification must be reasonably modified and reviewed. |
| C-002 | The overall tool adopts a front-end/back-end separated mode. |
| C-003 | The front end shall use a lightweight, community-supported, and maintainable framework; the product description explicitly states that the client uses Vue.js. |
| C-004 | The server uses a microservice architecture. |
| C-005 | During runtime, the system needs to limit simultaneous access volume; when the peak is exceeded, some user processes are blocked until the current carrying capacity can handle new processes. |
| C-006 | Gateway, user management service, project management service, and requirements management service run on a local computer. |
| C-007 | The algorithm service runs on a server because of machine performance requirements. |
| C-008 | When users cannot connect to the network, all requirements cannot be realized. |
| C-009 | Client hardware shall satisfy Intel Core i7-6700HQ CPU, more than 100 MB available storage, complete network support, and at least 8 GB memory. |
| C-0
...[truncated]

samples.title: eivas / direct/gpt-5

project_context.introduction:
### Product Scope

EIVAS is a Web application for epidemiological investigation scenarios. Through formatted data entry, storage, query, and visual association display, the system assists epidemiological form fillers in collecting case information and assists epidemiological analysts in analyzing case itineraries, associated cases, overall outbreaks, and statistical characteristics.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for software designers, developers, testers, system users, and project stakeholders.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Robustness | The system shall handle input cases outside the specified standard requirements. | Abnormal input shall not cause the system to crash. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Performance | The system shall support concurrent submission requests. | When 100 users submit requests simultaneously, page response time is less than 1 second. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Reliability / Availability | The system shall be able to run stably for long periods and support fast deployment switching. | When an unexpected event occurs, the system switches to the backup server within 20 minutes. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-004 | Security / Privacy | Query requests involving user personal information shall be encrypted. | Personal information query requests must be encrypted; the specification does not specify an encryption algorithm or protocol. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-005 | Resource Protection | The system shall cool down IP addresses that submit frequent query requests within a short period. | Frequently querying IP addresses are cooled down to avoid excessive server resource consumption and impact on normal investigation reporting and analysis. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Compatibility | The system shall support operation on various operating systems and browsers. | Core flows can be completed on target operating system and browser combinations; specific versions are to be defined by the project. | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Maintainability | The system code architecture shall support later maintenance. | Supports bug fixing, function modification, and new function addition; the team uses a unified coding style and adds code comments. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-008 | Usability | The system interface shall be concise and clear for professional epidemiological workers. | The interface primarily targets user understanding and use and avoids excessive artistic design. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-009 | Operational Usability | The system shall provide operational assistance for grassroots workers who lack software experience. | The system includes many operation prompts, error-operation feedback, and may include user documentation. | High | Demonstration |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | The system product form is a Web application. |
| C-002 | The scope of investigation reporting information shall refer to cited materials such as infectious disease information reporting management specifications. |
| C-003 | The system operating environment needs to cover various operating systems and browsers; specific target versions are not provided in the specification. |
| C-004 | Query requests involving personal information must be encrypted, and frequent query IP addresses must be cooled down. |
| C-005 | When an unexpected event occurs, the system shall switch to the backup server within 20 minutes. |
| C-006 | The user interface shall target understanding and use by professional epidemiological workers and remain concise and clear. |
| C-007 | The system shall provide operation prompts and error feedback for grassroots workers and may provide user documentation. |

### Operating Environment

The system is provided as a Web application and is required to run on various operating systems and browsers. Specific browser versions, server operating systems, database products, and deployment environments are not specified in the specification.

### Software/API Interfaces

| Interface | Requirement Summary |
| --- | --- |
| Front-end page to back-end database interface | Front-end pages transmit analyst or filler requests to the back-end database through interfaces, and the back-end database extracts information and feeds it back to front-end components. |
| Database storage interface | After the reporting form is submitted, case investigation information is sent to the back-end database for storage and analysis. |
| Algorithm module interface | The algorithm module calculates risks, clusters cases, and generates corresponding charts based on case investigation information. |

### Communication Interfaces

The system is a Web application. The specification only states that front-end pages interact with the back-end database through interfaces, and does not specify protocols, ports, message formats, authentication methods, or encrypted transport protocols. Query requests involving personal information must be encrypted.

### Assumptions and Dependencies

| ID | Assumption / Dependency |
| --- | --- |
| AD-001 | The system depends on a back-end database to store data required for cases, itineraries, and association analysis. |
| AD-002 | The system depends on an algorithm module to generate regional flow maps and case clustering graphs. |
| AD-003 | The system needs map or equivalent geographic visualization capability to display locations and itinerary paths. |
| AD-004 | The specification does not specify identity authentication, permission model, database type, map service provider, or deployment topology. |

samples.title: easylatex / openhands/gpt-5

project_context.introduction:
### Product scope
EasyLaTex is an online system for converting mathematical formulas, tables, images, CSV/Excel/PDF files, and handwritten inputs into LaTex code. The system also provides real-time rendering, project/workspace management, collaboration, version control, comments, and export capabilities.

### Intended audience
- Product managers and requirements reviewers validating the EasyLaTex requirement set.
- Developers implementing user management, file processing, AI recognition, rendering, collaboration, and version control modules.
- Test engineers deriving verification scenarios from functional, interface, performance, compatibility, and security requirements.
- Architecture and evaluation agents that need structured requirements for architecture view generation.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Quality attribute | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | For common handwritten formulas, standard formula images, and regular CSV/Excel/PDF files, LaTex conversion accuracy shall be at least 88%. | Accuracy | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | The LaTex rendering module shall keep rendering latency below 2 seconds. | Performance | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Each user operation such as page navigation or loading shall respond within 2 seconds. | Performance | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | The system shall concurrently process at least 100 user requests. | Scalability | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | The system shall support normal access through Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari on PC and mobile browsers. | Compatibility / portability | High | Demonstration |
| NFR-006 | File input compatibility shall include at least JPG, PNG, PDF, CSV, and Excel `.xlsx`. | Compatibility | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Generated LaTex results shall be compatible with mainstream LaTex editors and compilation environments such as Overleaf and TeXstudio. | Compatibility | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-008 | User information including email, username, and password shall be encrypted during storage and transmission. | Security / privacy | High | Inspection |
| NFR-009 | Project collaboration shall enforce permission control with at least project owner and collaborator roles. | Security / access control | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | The system shall provide defenses against common Web vulnerabilities including SQL injection, XSS, and CSRF. | Security | High | Security test |
| NFR-011 | The user interface shall be concise and consistent with user operation habits so users can quickly get started. | Usability | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-012 | The system shall use modular design, standardized API design, complete interface documentation, unit tests, integration tests, and deployment documentation to support maintainability and iteration. | Maintainability | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Users are assumed to use modern browsers that support WebSocket technology. |
| C-002 | The AI model is assumed to reach at least 90% image recognition accuracy during design and development. |
| C-003 | Upload and download workflows depend on stable user network connections. |
| C-004 | Identity security depends on the existing user authentication mechanism. |
| C-005 | Minimum client hardware includes Intel Core i3 or equivalent CPU, 4 GB RAM, 20 GB free disk space, integrated graphics, and a 1024x768 display. |
| C-006 | Recommended client hardware includes Intel Core i5 or higher CPU, 8 GB RAM or more, 50 GB free disk space with SSD recommended, graphics acceleration, and 1920x1080 or higher display. |
| C-007 | Supported operating systems include Windows 10 or higher, macOS 10.14 or higher, and Ubuntu 18.04 or higher. |
| C-008 | Supported database management systems include MySQL 8.0 or higher, PostgreSQL 12 or higher, or another compatible relational database. |
| C-009 | Supported web servers include Apache HTTP Server 2.4 or higher and Nginx 1.18 or higher. |
| C-010 | Development/runtime dependencies include Python 3.8 or higher, Node.js 14.x or higher, and TeX Live, MiKTeX, or another compatible LaTex engine. |
| C-011 | External application interfaces use HTTP/REST API. |
| C-012 | The system shall follow privacy-protection regulations such as GDPR and other data-protection regulations. |

### Operating environment
The system is intended to run through modern browsers that support WebSocket. Target clients include Windows, macOS, and Linux environments, with Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge. Server-side and development dependencies include relational databases, web servers, Python, Node.js, and a compatible LaTex engine.

### Software/API interfaces
The system shall expose HTTP/REST APIs for user registration, login, user information management, project management, file upload, download, collaboration sharing, real-time preview, version management, and comments. It shall also provide internal APIs for user verification, file storage, image recognition, LaTex generation, rendering, collaboration management, and version control.

### Communication interfaces
The specification explicitly identifies HTTP/REST API for external module interactions. It also assumes WebSocket-capable browsers, supporting web interaction scenarios where real-time preview or collaborative updates may require browser communication support.

### Assumptions and dependencies
- Users operate through modern WebSocket-capable browsers.
- AI model image recognition accuracy is expected to reach at least 90%.
- User network connections are stable enough for uploads and downloads.
- Identity security depends on the existing user authentication mechanism.
- Initial user scale and storage demand are relatively small.

It also explicitly addresses key non-functional requirements and constraints, such as real-time interaction via a WebSocket gateway (FR-013, C-001), REST API exposure (C-011), and the separation of relational and file-based data stores (FR-015, DR-004).

There are no components that contradict the SRS.

Decoupling the file processing pipeline into distinct services (Parser, Recognition, Generator, Rendering) allows each stage to be scaled and maintained independently, which is a sound strategy for a system with AI/ML components.

samples.title: s000057_94d93adc / direct/qwen3-coder-480b-a35b-instruct

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS defines the checked software requirements for Soundzone, a web application supported by the referenced repository snapshot. The specification is limited to behaviors, interfaces, and constraints supported by the repository artifacts and accepted human review decisions.

### Product scope
Soundzone is a web application in which users can:
- upload sounds,
- play sounds,
- follow other users,
- browse or access followed users' sounds through follow-related views or feeds, and
- scrub playback by clicking a waveform.

The product uses a React/Redux frontend, a Node/Express backend, PostgreSQL for application data, and Cloudinary for audio and image storage.

### Intended audience
This document is intended for:
- maintainers of the frontend and backend application,
- architects generating component and interface views,
- testers validating supported behaviors, and
- integrators working with storage and API boundaries.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Reliability | The system shall maintain playback continuity across supported page changes during active audio playback. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Playback consistency | The system shall maintain one managed current playback item at a time when users switch from one sound to another. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Storage architecture | The system shall store media assets in Cloudinary and store media URL references rather than raw media binaries in PostgreSQL. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-004 | Usability | User-facing forms shall provide validation feedback and error handling during data entry and submission. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-005 | Configurability | The frontend shall support runtime configuration through environment values for backend API access and Cloudinary integration. | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | The application is constrained to a React/Redux frontend architecture. |
| C-002 | The server-side application is constrained to a Node/Express backend. |
| C-003 | Persistent application data is constrained to PostgreSQL storage. |
| C-004 | Audio and image media storage is constrained to Cloudinary integration. |
| C-005 | Frontend-backend interaction is constrained to configured API-base-URL-driven HTTP requests. |
| C-006 | Media upload flow is constrained such that the frontend uploads media directly to Cloudinary before backend persistence of associated data. |

### Operating environment
| Aspect | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Client runtime | The product shall operate as a browser-based web application implemented with React and Redux. |
| Server runtime | The product shall use a Node/Express backend. |
| Data platform | The product shall use PostgreSQL for application data persistence. |
| Media platform | The product shall use Cloudinary for hosted audio and image assets. |
| Frontend configuration | Frontend runtime depends on configured environment values including an API base URL, a Cloudinary URL, and a Cloudinary upload preset. |

### Software/API interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Backend application API | The frontend shall call backend APIs through a configured API base URL. |
| User/auth API | The backend API shall support user registration, login, and user information retrieval flows. |
| Follow API | The backend API shall support follow and unfollow operations. |
| Feed API | The backend API shall support retrieval of follow-related feed data and user sound lists. |
| Sound API | The backend API shall support sound detail retrieval, sound creation, and sound deletion. |
| Cloudinary API | The frontend shall call Cloudinary upload endpoints directly for audio and image uploads. |
| Database interface | The backend shall persist application records and Cloudinary URL references in PostgreSQL. |

### Communication interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Frontend-backend communication | The frontend shall communicate with the backend using fetch-based HTTP requests. |
| Frontend-Cloudinary communication | The frontend shall communicate with Cloudinary using HTTP requests for direct media upload. |
| Backend-database communication | The backend shall communicate with PostgreSQL for persistence of application records and media URL references. |

### Assumptions and dependencies
| ID | Statement |
|---|---|
| A-001 | Media asset handling depends on Cloudinary-hosted storage and returned media URLs. |
| A-002 | Frontend behavior depends on Redux-managed state and action flows. |
| A-003 | Frontend application operations depend on backend HTTP APIs. |
| A-004 | Playback persistence depends on centralized playback state or component coordination across page or component changes. |

samples.title: s000013_b5fd7337 / metagpt-custom/claude-sonnet-4-6

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose
This SRS defines the final checked requirements for a containerized song mood storage, retrieval, and search web application. It is prepared as a clean requirements input for downstream architecture diagram generation.

### 1.2 Product Scope
The system is a multi-tier web application that allows users to request song mood information. It provides:
- A browser-accessible React frontend.
- A FastAPI backend for song lookup and storage workflows.
- Elasticsearch storage for song records and associated mood values.
- Kibana as an auxiliary UI for inspecting stored Elasticsearch data.
- Genius API integration for searching songs that are not already stored.
- Dockerized deployment with separate container services for frontend, backend, Elasticsearch, and Kibana.

The system stores and retrieves song-mood associations for songs whose mood is already available. A runtime mood classification or model-inference component is not specified as an in-scope requirement unless added separately.

The project dataset scope is English songs. This is a dataset and project-scope constraint, not a runtime language-rejection requirement.

### 1.3 Intended Audience
- End users requesting song mood information through the web application.
- Developers maintaining the React frontend and FastAPI backend.
- Operators deploying and updating the containerized services.
- Testers validating lookup, storage, and external API behavior.
- Architecture reviewers generating or validating architecture diagrams.

### 1.4 Terminology
| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Song record | A stored song data item containing song identity and lyric metadata. |
| Mood association | A stored relationship between a song and an already available mood value. |
| Known song | A song that already has a stored record in Elasticsearch. |
| Missing song | A requested song that is not found in Elasticsearch and must be searched through the Genius API. |
| Auxiliary data UI | Kibana, used for inspecting data stored in Elasticsearch. |
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Quality Attribute | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | The system shall be deployable as a multi-tier Dockerized application with separate container services for frontend, backend, Elasticsearch, and Kibana. | Deployability / portability | High | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | Stored song-mood associations shall be retrievable from Elasticsearch for later requests. | Retrieval efficiency | Medium | Test |
| NFR-003 | The frontend shall be accessible through a web browser during development and operation. | Browser compatibility | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-004 | The backend shall use Elasticsearch as the authoritative store for persisted song-mood associations. | Data consistency | High | Inspection |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | Frontend technology shall be React. |
| C-002 | Backend technology shall be FastAPI. |
| C-003 | Persistent song and mood storage shall use Elasticsearch. |
| C-004 | Kibana shall be used as the auxiliary UI for Elasticsearch data inspection. |
| C-005 | The backend shall depend on the Genius API for songs not found in Elasticsearch. |
| C-006 | The deployment shall include separate Docker container services for frontend, backend, Elasticsearch, and Kibana. |
| C-007 | The Genius API shall be modeled as an external dependency rather than an internal container service. |
| C-008 | The project dataset scope shall be English songs. |
| C-009 | A runtime classifier, model server, or inference algorithm shall not be assumed unless specified by a separate requirement. |

### 2.4 Operating Environment
- Web browser for frontend access.
- Docker-based runtime for the application stack.
- React frontend container.
- FastAPI backend container.
- Elasticsearch container.
- Kibana container.
- External network access from the backend to the Genius API.

### 3.3 Communication Interfaces
| ID | Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|---|
| COM-001 | Browser access | Users shall access the frontend through standard browser web access. |
| COM-002 | Internal service communication | Frontend, backend, Elasticsearch, and Kibana shall communicate across the Dockerized application network according to their configured endpoints. |
| COM-003 | External API communication | The backend shall communicate with the Genius API as an external service. |

### 2.5 Assumptions and Dependencies
- Elasticsearch is required for stored song and mood retrieval.
- The Genius API is required for searching songs that are not stored.
- Kibana depends on Elasticsearch availability.
- The backend depends on Elasticsearch availability for known-song lookup.
- The backend depends on Genius API availability for missing-song search.
- English-only scope applies to the project dataset used by the application and analysis.

samples.title: Campus_Helper / openhands/claude-sonnet-4-6

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
Its purpose is to provide structured input for architecture generation, test design, and requirements traceability for the Campus Helper platform.

### Product Scope

The Campus Helper platform is a campus trading platform based on a WeChat Mini Program. It serves campus takeout/express pickup, immediate takeout transfer, and second-hand information publishing. Through a publish-and-accept-order model, the platform supports information flow for errand products and second-hand product transactions and aims to provide a more standardized and reliable way to obtain transaction information.

This document uses the specification definitions of platform, product, errand product, second-hand product, user, guest, seller, customer, order, errand order, second-hand order, tag, administrator, log, order status, publish product, search product, and QPS.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for platform users, software developers, requirements analysts, testers, architecture generation workflows, and requirements review workflows. Target end users include students, teachers, and family members; management users are platform administrators.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Metric / Acceptance | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Security | The system shall provide program security protection capability and identify human-machine and malicious behavior. | Human-machine and malicious behavior recognition rate is greater than 98%. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Security | The system shall prevent SQL injection and validate input parameters on the server side. | SQL injection attacks are blocked; client bots cannot easily obtain data. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Security | The system shall require user login authentication and implement permission control by user type. | Unauthenticated or unauthorized users cannot illegally access data. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Privacy / Integrity | The system shall protect user privacy information and personalized information, and review data stored in the database. | A review mechanism exists before or during data storage; privacy data must not be accessed by unauthorized users. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-005 | Performance | The system shall support concurrent requests during peak periods. | It supports more than 500 to 3000 people simultaneously initiating upload, browse, download, and other requests. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Performance | The system shall satisfy response time requirements. | In 95% of cases, normal-period response time is no more than 1.5 seconds and peak-period response time is no more than 4 seconds. | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Performance | The system shall satisfy off-peak search response time requirements. | Off-peak searches by specific number and name conditions return results within 3 seconds. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-008 | Capacity | The system shall satisfy user volume, data volume, and storage capacity requirements. | It supports 10,000 users and GB-level data; database table rows do not exceed 100,000, maximum database capacity does not exceed 100GB, and disk capacity is at least 20GB. | High | Analysis |
| NFR-009 | Usability | The system interface shall maintain a unified overall style. | Icons are concise and intuitive, colors balance warm and cool tones while highlighting key points, and font size, spacing, and color fit reading habits. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-010 | Usability | System function entries shall be easy to find. | Functional operations must not be hidden too deeply, and users can easily find expected operations. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-011 | Learnability | The system shall support user self-learning. | Online help, navigation, wizards, and similar means help users learn to use the system. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-012 | Usability | System interaction logic shall be simple and clear. | Skilled users can complete operations more quickly. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-013 | Reliability | The system shall identify illegal input and try to keep other modules running during unexpected faults. | Illegal input is identified; a single module fault should not make all functions unavailable. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Maintainability | System module integration shall be decoupled, and documentation shall be clear, readable, standardized, and unified. | Module responsibility boundaries are inspectable, and documentation complies with project norms. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-015 | Testability | The system shall allow product function tests with a small dataset. | Testers can cover core functional flows with small-scale data. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-016 | Scalability | The product shall leave upgrade interfaces and upgrade space. | Architecture and interface design preserve upgrade extension points. | Medium | Analysis |
| NFR-017 | Fault Recovery | The system shall satisfy hardware fault handling time limits. | If the server crashes, restart the server and system within 30 minutes; if disk, CPU, host, or other hardware is damaged, replace related parts within 12 hours. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-018 | Fault Recovery | The system shall satisfy software fault handling time limits. | If the system has an error, handle it, fix the bug, and redeploy within 2 hours; if memory is insufficient, scale capacity within 6 hours according to resource conditions. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-019 | Observability | The system shall support monitoring and logs needed for fault localization. | CPU, memory, I/O, connection count, file descriptors, service logs, interface response time, QPS, error codes, database load, slow queries, cache, message queue, and storage metrics can be monitored. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-020 | Incident Management | The system shall support fault recovery and retrospective processes. | Service rollback, restart, and emergency update are supported; difficult faults require a caseStudy document. | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | The platform shall provide services based on the WeChat Mini Program development framework. |
| C-002 | The client runtime environment shall satisfy Android 5.0 or above, 4GB or more RAM, and 64GB or more disk capacity. |
| C-003 | The client WeChat version shall be 8.0 or above. |
| C-004 | The system architecture shall follow a four-layer division: presentation layer, business layer, data layer, and data entity layer (database). |
| C-005 | The main service objects are limited to students, teachers, and family members. |
| C-006 | Requirements and testing work shall refer to the national standards, WeChat Mini Program platform specifications, and Web coding specifications listed in the specification. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Client operating system | Android 5.0 or above. |
| Client hardware | 4GB or more RAM and 64GB or more disk capacity. |
| WeChat version | WeChat 8.0 or above. |
| Server environment | The specification does not specify server operating system, database product, cloud service, or
...[truncated]

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
' --- Configuration & Styles ---
skinparam topboxSelection true
skinparam Direction top to bottom
skinparam packageStyle rectangle
skinparam shadowing false

' Define Styles based on original ClassDefs
<style>
    .ui {
        BackgroundColor #ccff90
        LineColor #33691e
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .display {
        BackgroundColor #fff3e0
        LineColor #e65100
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .business {
        BackgroundColor #b2dfdb
        LineColor #004d40
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .dataLogic {
        BackgroundColor #e1bee7
        LineColor #4a148c
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .storage {
        BackgroundColor #bbdefb
        LineColor #0d47a1
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .env {
        BackgroundColor #cfd8dc
        LineColor #37474f
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .crosscut {
        BackgroundColor #ffcdd2
        LineColor #b71c1c
        LineThickness 2
    }
</style>

' --- Layer 1: UI Layer ---
package "Mini-program UI" as UI_Layer <<ui>> {
    rectangle "Wxml" as WXML <<ui>>
    rectangle "Wxss" as WXSS <<ui>>
    rectangle "JavaScript" as JS <<ui>>
}

' --- Layer 2: Display Layer ---
package "Display Layer" as Display_Layer <<display>> {
    rectangle "Template Engine Rendering\n(pages)" as Render <<display>>
    rectangle "wx.request Interaction" as Interact <<display>>
    rectangle "POST Request" as ReqPost <<display>>
    rectangle "GET Request" as ReqGet <<display>>
}

' --- Layer 3: Business Layer ---
package "Business Layer" as Business_Layer <<business>> {
    package "Core Services" as Main_Services {
        rectangle "Help Goods Service" as Svc_Goods <<business>>
        rectangle "Order Mgmt Service" as Svc_Order <<business>>
        rectangle "Personal Center Service" as Svc_User <<business>>
        rectangle "Admin Mgmt Service" as Svc_Admin <<business>>
    }

    package "Functional Modules" as Modules {
        rectangle "Hot Items Module" as Mod_Hot <<business>>
        rectangle "Create Order Module" as Mod_CreateOrder <<business>>
        rectangle "Favorites Module" as Mod_Fav <<business>>
        rectangle "Modify User Info Module" as Mod_UserInfo <<business>>
        rectangle "Goods Category Module" as Mod_Cat <<business>>
        rectangle "Order Record Module" as Mod_OrderRec <<business>>
        rectangle "Platform Mgmt Module" as Mod_Platform <<business>>
        rectangle "......" as Mod_More <<business>>
    }
}

' --- Cross-Cutting Concerns ---
rectangle "Log Recording" as Logs <<crosscut>>
rectangle "Permission Control" as Auth <<crosscut>>

' --- Layer 4: Data Layer ---
package "Data Layer" as Data_Logic_Layer <<dataLogic>> {
    rectangle "Stored Procedures" as StoredProc <<dataLogic>>
    rectangle "Custom Functions" as CustomFunc <<dataLogic>>
    rectangle "Data Cache" as DataCache <<dataLogic>>
    rectangle "Data Backup" as Backup <<dataLogic>>
    rectangle "Data Recovery" as Recovery <<dataLogic>>
    rectangle "Read/Write Database" as RW_DB <<dataLogic>>
}

' --- Layer 5: Database ---
package "Database" as Database_Storage <<storage>> {
    database "MySQL" as MySQL_Main <<storage>>
}

' --- Layer 6: Runtime Environment ---
package "Runtime Environment" as Runtime_Env <<env>> {
    node "Tencent Cloud\nLightweight App Server" as Server <<env>>
    node "MySQL 106.55.224.139\n2 Cores 4GB" as ServerSpec <<env>>
}

' --- Relationships ---
UI_Layer --> Display_Layer
Display_Layer --> Business_Layer
Interact -- ReqPost
Interact -- ReqGet

Business_Layer --> Data_Logic_Layer
Data_Logic_Layer --> Database_Storage
Runtime_Env --> Database_Storage

' Cross Cutting Links (Dotted)
Business_Layer .. Logs
Business_Layer .. Auth
Data_Logic_Layer .. Logs
Data_Logic_Layer .. Auth
Display_Layer .. Logs

@enduml

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS defines the checked software requirements for selected communication, build, and verification behaviors documented in the `ParaStation/psmpi` repository for sample `s000072_b56aad92`.

### Product scope
This specification covers:
- manual verification workflows for embedded `hwloc` testing,
- documented communication-subsystem design responsibilities for progress, connection establishment, and one-to-many transfer,
- documented behavior of the specific send-state-machine path described in `send-sm` notes,
- conditional build and dependency constraints for the embedded Libfabric verbs provider.

This specification does not treat design-discussion notes as confirmed implemented public APIs unless separately verified.

### Intended audience
- Maintainers and integrators of psmpi communication components
- Test engineers validating embedded `hwloc` packaging and communication behavior
- Build and deployment engineers integrating optional verbs-based transport dependencies
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Compatibility | When building or enabling the embedded Libfabric verbs provider, the build shall require `libibverbs` version `1.1.8` or newer and `librdmacm` version `1.0.16` or newer. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | Build compatibility | When compiling the embedded Libfabric verbs provider from source, the build environment shall provide matching header files for `libibverbs` and `librdmacm`. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-003 | Deployability | When required libraries or headers are not in default paths, the build and deployment environment shall allow their locations to be supplied through `CFLAGS`, `LDFLAGS`, and `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-004 | Verifiability | Embedded `hwloc` verification shall be executable manually and shall not depend on inclusion in `make check`. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-005 | Documentation clarity | Design-note-derived communication behaviors shall be treated as documented design responsibilities unless implementation is separately confirmed. | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | Embedded `hwloc` tests are manual and are not part of `make check`. |
| C-002 | The preferred embedded `hwloc` test entry point is `./run-embedded-tests.sh <tarball>`. |
| C-003 | The alternative embedded verification workflow requires renaming the extracted top-level directory to `hwloc-tree/` before running `./autogen.sh`, `./configure`, and `make`. |
| C-004 | When the embedded Libfabric verbs provider is enabled, verbs transport uses the Linux Verbs API and depends on `librdmacm` for communication management and `libibverbs` for control and data transfer operations. |
| C-005 | When the embedded Libfabric verbs provider is enabled, verbs-provider support requires `libibverbs >= 1.1.8` and `librdmacm >= 1.0.16`. |

### Operating environment
- Shell-based build and test environment using commands such as `./run-embedded-tests.sh`, `./autogen.sh`, `./configure`, and `make`
- Linux Verbs environment when the embedded Libfabric verbs provider is built or enabled
- Communication stack context that may integrate with transport layers such as OFI/libfabric or UCX

### Software interfaces
- When the embedded Libfabric verbs provider is enabled, interface with the Linux Verbs API
- When the embedded Libfabric verbs provider is enabled, use `librdmacm` for communication management and `libibverbs` for control and data transfer
- The communication design notes describe responsibilities for initialization, finalization, progress advancement, blocking progress, connection establishment, and one-to-many transfer; these are documented design responsibilities rather than confirmed implemented public APIs

### Communication interfaces
- One-to-many scatter-style transfer is documented in the communication design notes
- The specific send-state-machine path interacts with a network device and network send descriptors

### Assumptions and dependencies
- Embedded `hwloc` verification is manual and is not part of `make check`.
- The alternative embedded `hwloc` workflow assumes the extracted top-level source directory is renamed to `hwloc-tree/`.
- The verbs-related constraints in this SRS apply only when building or enabling the embedded Libfabric verbs provider.
- Non-default library and header locations may need to be supplied through build and runtime environment variables.

@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
top to bottom direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #34495E
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

rectangle "Applications\nHPC, AI, ML, Big Data" as Applications

package "Programming Models and Frameworks" as Frameworks {
    rectangle "MPI\nMPICH, Open-MPI" as MpiApps
    rectangle "PGAS / SHMEM\nOpenSHMEM, UPC, CAF, X10, Chapel" as PgasApps
    rectangle "Task Runtimes\nParsec, OCR, Legion" as TaskApps
    rectangle "AI / ML Frameworks\nTensorFlow, Burst Buffer" as MlApps
}

package "UCX" as Ucx {
    package "UCP Protocols\nHigh Level API" as Ucp {
        rectangle "Transport Selection" as TransportSelection
        rectangle "Cross-Transport Multi-Rail" as MultiRail
        rectangle "Fragmentation" as Fragmentation
        rectangle "Software Protocols" as SoftwareProtocols
        rectangle "Message Passing API\nTag Matching, Rendezvous" as MessagePassingDomain
        rectangle "PGAS API\nRMAs, Atomics" as PgasDomain
        rectangle "Task Based API\nActive Messages" as TaskDomain
        rectangle "I/O API\nStream" as IoDomain
    }

    package "UCT Hardware Transports\nLow Level API" as Uct {
        rectangle "RDMA Verbs Transport\nInfiniBand, ROCE, OPA" as RdmaTransport
        rectangle "RC" as Rc
        rectangle "UD" as Ud
        rectangle "DCT" as Dct
        rectangle "Gemini / Aries Transport\nGNI" as GniTransport
        rectangle "Intra-node Host Memory\nSYSV, POSIX, KNEM, CMA, XPMEM" as HostMemoryTransport
        rectangle "GPU Transport\nNVIDIA CUDA, AMD ROCm" as GpuTransport
        rectangle "Ethernet Transport\nTCP/IP Sockets" as EthernetTransport
    }

    package "UCS Services\nCommon Utilities" as Ucs {
        rectangle "Utilities" as Utilities
        rectangle "Data Structures" as DataStructures
        rectangle "Memory Management" as MemoryManagement
    }

    package "UCM Memory Services" as Ucm {
        rectangle "Memory Hooks" as MemoryHooks
    }
}

package "Drivers and Kernels" as DriverLayer {
    rectangle "RDMA Verbs Driver" as RdmaDriver
    rectangle "Cray Driver" as CrayDriver
    rectangle "OS Kernel\nHost Memory" as HostKernel
    rectangle "ROCm / CUDA" as GpuRuntime
    rectangle "OS Kernel\nEthernet" as EthernetKernel
    rectangle "OS Kernel\nMemory Hooks" as MemoryKernel
}

rectangle "Hardware" as Hardware

Applications --> MpiApps
Applications --> PgasApps
Applications --> TaskApps
Applications --> MlApps

MpiApps --> MessagePassingDomain
PgasApps --> PgasDomain
TaskApps --> TaskDomain
MlApps --> IoDomain

MessagePassingDomain --> TransportSelection
PgasDomain --> TransportSelection
TaskDomain --> TransportSelection
IoDomain --> TransportSelection
TransportSelection --> MultiRail
MultiRail --> Fragmentation
Fragmentation --> SoftwareProtocols

SoftwareProtocols --> RdmaTransport
SoftwareProtocols --> GniTransport
SoftwareProtocols --> HostMemoryTransport
SoftwareProtocols --> GpuTransport
SoftwareProtocols --> EthernetTransport

RdmaTransport --> Rc
RdmaTransport --> Ud
RdmaTransport --> Dct

RdmaTransport --> RdmaDriver
GniTransport --> CrayDriver
HostMemoryTransport --> HostKernel
GpuTransport --> GpuRuntime
EthernetTransport --> EthernetKernel
MemoryHooks --> MemoryKernel

Ucp --> Ucs : common utilities
Uct --> Ucs : utility services
Uct --> Ucm : memory hooks

RdmaDriver --> Hardware
CrayDriver --> Hardware
HostKernel --> Hardware
GpuRuntime --> Hardware
EthernetKernel --> Hardware
MemoryKernel --> Hardware
@enduml

samples.title: AwesomeMusic / direct/claude-sonnet-4-6

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
Its purpose is to provide structured input for architecture generation, test design, and requirements traceability for an AI music creation and sharing platform.

### Product Scope

The AI music creation and sharing platform is a Web system for music enthusiasts. After registration, users can use the platform for AI music creation, score generation, score upload and sharing, favorites, follows, recommendations, search, hot charts, and downloads. The system contains three modules: Web frontend, Web backend, and algorithm service. The algorithm service generates music from user-provided source files and includes online music generation and offline model iterative upgrade services.

This document uses the specification definitions of MQ, score, track seed, and midi music. A score is an abc-format plain-text file that can generate midi music. A track seed is the beginning part of an abc score and is less than 10 seconds long. Midi music is a music file playable by midi-capable players.

### Intended Audience

This document is intended for customers, product managers, developers, testers, and downstream architecture generation and requirements review workflows. Target end users are music enthusiasts, especially users interested in AI music creation, music sharing, and novel approaches to music creation.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality | Requirement | Fit Criterion | Priority | Verification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NFR-001 | Performance | Average account registration latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Performance | Average login latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-003 | Performance | Average score favorite latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-004 | Performance | Average follow-user latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-005 | Performance | Average score upload latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 3000ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-006 | Performance | Average model favorite latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-007 | Performance | Average Web-side score generation latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-008 | Performance | Average score download latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 3000ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-009 | Performance | Average recommendation retrieval latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 500ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-010 | Performance | Average score search latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 1000ms. | High | Test |
| NFR-011 | Performance | Average hot-chart viewing latency shall satisfy the performance table. | Average latency <= 200ms. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-012 | Performance | Algorithm training for generation models shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-013 | Performance | Algorithm score generation shall satisfy throughput and latency requirements. | Throughput 5ops, average latency <= 1min. | High | Test |
| NFR-014 | Performance | Classifying all scores shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-015 | Performance | Song recommendation shall complete daily. | Throughput 1/day, average latency <= 1.5h. | High | Test |
| NFR-016 | Scalability | The Web system shall support the preset concurrent access throughput. | Concurrent access throughput is no less than 2000QPS. | High | Test |
| NFR-017 | Scalability | The Web system shall support transaction change throughput. | Transaction changes are no less than 100TPS. | High | Test |
| NFR-018 | Usability | System interactions shall be suitable for music production beginners. | Interactions are simple and understandable and do not require professional function design. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-019 | Usability | The system shall provide user instructions, prompts, and guidance. | Comprehensive instructions, prompts, and guidance exist to help users get started quickly. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-020 | Security | The gateway shall isolate internal and external network boundaries. | Internal systems are not exposed externally due to loose security configuration. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-021 | Reliability | Important data shall be stored in multiple replicas. | Important data is stored in 3 replicas. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-022 | Availability | The system shall remain available under partial failures. | The system remains usable when some internal nodes are upgraded, crash, or experience a certain level of network partition. | High | Test |
| NFR-023 | Maintainability | Code shall be readable. | Code follows naming conventions, and names correspond to actual meaning. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-024 | Maintainability | Code shall avoid excessive coupling. | Code supports later iterative optimization, extension of existing functions, and addition of new functions. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-025 | Load Balancing | The system shall maintain stable response and reasonable resource utilization under high access volume. | Load balancing technology is provided when user access volume is high. | High | Test |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
| --- | --- |
| C-001 | Frontend languages are HTML, CSS, and JavaScript; the frontend framework is Bootstrap. |
| C-002 | Backend language is Golang; backend framework is Gin. |
| C-003 | Webpage response time shall be within 1 second. |
| C-004 | Music generation model training time shall be within 2 hours. |
| C-005 | The algorithm model shall be able to generate enough varieties of music styles. |
| C-006 | Generated music quality shall be above the baseline. |
| C-007 | Algorithm model response time shall be controlled within 1 minute. |
| C-008 | Product interfaces shall reflect user friendliness and have a certain degree of aesthetics. |
| C-009 | Project R&D shall strictly follow the project management process to avoid phase delays affecting later development. |
| C-010 | System hardware environment consists of 4 virtual machines, each with CPU 4 * 2400MHz, 8GB memory, and 200GB storage. |
| C-011 | System software environment includes Ubuntu 18.04 LTS, Hadoop 3.2.2, RabbitMQ 3.8.14, etcd 3.4.15, and Nginx 1.19.8. |
| C-012 | Communication protocols are HTTPS and TCP/IP. |
| C-013 | Task metadata must conform to database constraints and limit task size. |
| C-014 | When task submission fails, erroneous data needs to be automatically skipped and retried. |
| C-015 | All requirements listed in use-case diagrams are planned for the current version and do not need to be assigned to future versions. |

### Operating Environment

| Environment | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Hardware | 4 virtual machines, each with CPU 4 * 2400MHz, 8GB memory, and 200GB storage. |
| Operating system | Ubuntu 18.04 LTS. |
| Software interfaces | Apache Hadoop 3.2.2, RabbitMQ 3.8.14, etcd 3.4.15, and Nginx 1.19.8. |
| Communication protocols | HTTPS and TCP/IP. |
| Frontend technology |

samples.title: s000003_e847b2a1 / direct/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose
This SRS defines the final checked requirements for the Prediction Cards system. It is structured as a clean requirements input for downstream architecture diagram generation.

### 1.2 Product Scope
Prediction Cards is a blockchain-backed prediction market and NFT application. The system includes:
- A frontend application for wallet-connected prediction creation, prediction viewing, outcome NFT fund operations, withdrawal, and reward claiming.
- An on-chain PredictionCards smart contract deployed on the Aeternity blockchain and located through an AENS name.
- An oracle service that registers and maintains an Aeternity oracle, checks pending oracle queries, obtains price-comparison results, and submits oracle responses.
- A prediction-processing service that drives closed predictions into the outcome determination workflow and completes result processing after oracle responses are available.
- Prediction media storage for outcome NFT images or animations.
- A local contract integration-test environment with CORS/proxy support for browser-style contract testing.

### 1.3 Intended Audience
- Product owners and maintainers.
- Frontend and smart-contract developers.
- Oracle-service and prediction-processing-service developers.
- Test engineers and integration engineers.
- Architecture reviewers generating or validating system diagrams.

### 1.4 Terminology
| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Prediction Cards | Product and codebase name; "card" is treated as a product/UI/NFT metaphor, not as a separate multi-prediction domain entity. |
| Prediction | A market-like event with an asset, target price, prediction time range, and two possible outcome NFTs. |
| Outcome NFT | One of the prediction outcome assets, represented by result categories such as `higher` and `lower_or_equal`. |
| Prediction participant | A wallet-connected user who creates, views, funds, rents, withdraws from, or claims rewards related to predictions and outcome NFTs. |
| Oracle service | The service responsible for maintaining the Aeternity oracle and submitting result responses for oracle queries. |
| Prediction-processing service | The service responsible for initiating oracle queries for closed predictions and driving on-chain result processing after oracle responses are available. |
| AENS name | The Aeternity naming mechanism used to locate the deployed contract. |
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality Attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Interoperability | The frontend integration shall operate with an Aeternity wallet client and Aeternity SDK-based contract interaction components. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | Portability | The oracle service shall support both development-mode execution and packaged execution. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-003 | Configurability | The oracle service shall externalize required runtime properties through mandatory runtime configuration. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-004 | Local test web compatibility | The local contract integration-test environment shall support browser cross-origin access with configured methods and headers. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-005 | Scheduled processing availability | The oracle service and prediction-processing service shall perform periodic processing rather than relying only on manual invocation. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-006 | Oracle availability | The system shall maintain oracle availability before oracle registration expires by checking validity and extending it when configured thresholds are reached. | High | Test |
| NFR-007 | Time interpretation correctness | The system shall define and verify the timezone interpretation rule for frontend local-time input to avoid prediction time shifts across runtime environments. | Medium | Analysis |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | The oracle service is constrained to a Quarkus-based implementation and runtime model. |
| C-002 | The on-chain prediction logic is constrained by the Aeternity blockchain, the Sophia smart-contract runtime, the AENS contract-location mechanism, and the responsibility split between the oracle service and prediction-processing service. |
| C-003 | Frontend contract interaction is constrained to Aeternity wallet and SDK components. |
| C-004 | Oracle-service operation is constrained by complete runtime configuration before startup. |
| C-005 | Oracle registration, validity thresholds, validity-extension strategy, scheduling periods, oracle fees, node connection, compiler-service connection, network environment, and key configuration are constrained by runtime configuration. |
| C-006 | The local CORS/proxy configuration shall not be treated as a production nginx or production reverse-proxy requirement without additional deployment specification. |
| C-007 | The term "card" shall not be modeled as an independent data entity or multi-prediction container unless a future domain model explicitly defines such an entity. |
| C-008 | Frontend local-time input shall have an explicit timezone interpretation rule before production use. |

### 2.6 Operating Environment
- Browser frontend using React and Aeternity wallet integration.
- Aeternity blockchain runtime for the on-chain PredictionCards contract.
- AENS contract-location mechanism for resolving the deployed contract.
- Quarkus-based oracle service.
- Prediction-processing service for scheduled closed-prediction handling.
- External price provider used by oracle outcome determination.
- Prediction media storage, such as IPFS-compatible storage.
- Local contract integration-test environment with Aeternity node, compiler, index, debug endpoint, and test proxy/CORS configuration.

### 3.2 Software/API Interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Frontend wallet interface | The frontend prediction API shall use a connected wallet as its contract interaction dependency. |
| Frontend context interface | The frontend shall provide prediction operation capabilities to child components through a provider/context mechanism. |
| Frontend-contract interface | The frontend shall submit prediction creation, read prediction state, and perform fund/reward operations through the on-chain contract. |
| AENS contract-location interface | The frontend and services shall locate the deployed on-chain contract through the configured AENS name. |
| Prediction-processing-service contract interface | The prediction-processing service shall initiate oracle queries for closed predictions and complete result processing after oracle responses are available. |
| Oracle-service Aeternity oracle interface | The oracle service shall maintain the Aeternity oracle and submit result responses for pending oracle queries. |
| Oracle-service price-provider interface | The oracle service shall obtain external price information needed for price-comparison outcome determination. |
| Frontend media-storage interface | The frontend shall load or reference prediction outcome NFT media from prediction media storage. |
| Local test proxy interface | The local contract integration-test environment shall expose the configured test services with browser-compatible CORS behavior. |

### 3.3 Communication Interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Wallet-mediated blockchain calls | Frontend contract operations shall be submitted through wallet-mediated Aeternity interactions. |
| Scheduled service processing | Oracle and prediction-processing service behavior shall run periodically according to runtime configuration. |
| Oracle query/response communication | The oracle service shall read pending oracle queries and submit result responses in the expected oracle response format. |
| Media retrieval | Prediction media references shall be retrievable by the frontend from media storage. |
| Local CORS communication | The local integration-test proxy shall allow configured browser cross-origin methods and headers for test flows. |

### 2.7 Assumptions and Dependencies
- Prediction participants have access to a compatible wallet.
- On-chain contract interaction depends on the Aeternity SDK and wallet client.
- The deployed contract is reachable through its AENS name.
- The oracle service has complete runtime configuration before startup.
- The prediction-processing service can discover closed predicti
...[truncated]

}

rectangle "User" as User #FFFFFF
rectangle "Frontend" as Frontend #FFFFFF
rectangle "IPFS" as IPFS #D8E8FA

User --> Frontend

PredictionProcess --> PredictionContract : poll for predictions with state "CLOSED"\ncall function "ask_for_winning_option"\ncall function "process_oracle_response" if oracle has responded

OracleService --> PredictionContract : poll for oracle queries\nget price from coingecko API\ndetermine outcome\nrespond to query with outcome (higher/lower)

Frontend --> PredictionContract : create prediction\ndeposit / rent NFT\nwithdraw / claim rewards
PredictionContract --> Frontend : get data\nvia dry-runs

Frontend --> IPFS : load GIFs of NFTs
@enduml

samples.title: s000026_5d153cf4 / metagpt-custom/gpt-5

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose

This Software Requirements Specification defines the checked requirements for a Google Kubernetes Engine deployment that distributes Google Cloud Pub/Sub topic messages to web clients over WebSockets.

The document is prepared for architecture view generation and omits review notes, provenance fields, and supporting-material identifiers.

### 1.2 Product Scope

The product is a WebSocket adapter and deployment package that:

- Mirrors messages from a configured Google Cloud Pub/Sub topic to connected WebSocket clients.
- Lets web clients receive topic data without directly using the Pub/Sub API or SDK.
- Runs as a containerized runtime in Google Kubernetes Engine.
- Exposes the adapter through a Kubernetes `LoadBalancer` service.
- Uses a Kubernetes ConfigMap value to bind a deployment instance to one Pub/Sub topic.
- Uses a `websocketd` bridge with a Node.js `pulltop` process to serve topic data to clients.
- Supports horizontal pod autoscaling through Kubernetes autoscaling configuration.

Each deployment instance binds one externally reachable WebSocket endpoint to one configured Pub/Sub topic. Multiple topics shall be served by multiple independently configured deployment instances.

### 1.3 Intended Audience

The intended audience includes deployment operators, Kubernetes operators, GCP project administrators, web-client integrators, testers, and architecture evaluators.

### 1.4 Definitions and Abbreviations

- GCP: Google Cloud Platform.
- GKE: Google Kubernetes Engine.
- HPA: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler.
- Pub/Sub: Google Cloud Pub/Sub.
- SRS: Software Requirements Specification.
- VM: Virtual machine.
- WebSocket: Full-duplex network communication protocol used by connected clients.
project_context.asr:
## 6. Non-Functional Requirements

### NFR-001 Horizontal Scalability

The deployment shall support horizontal pod scaling through Kubernetes HPA configuration.

**Metric:** The HPA targets the adapter Deployment with min replicas `1`, max replicas `2`, and CPU utilization target `80`.

### NFR-002 Subscription Efficiency

The adapter shall support many WebSocket clients while using one Pub/Sub subscription per running VM or pod instance.

**Metric:** For `N` running instances and `M` connected clients, subscription consumption scales with `N` rather than `M`.

### NFR-003 Client Data Continuity

The adapter shall provide recent cached messages so client displays can show meaningful data during low-traffic periods.

**Metric:** A client can receive up to the last 10 cached messages for the configured topic.

### NFR-004 Deployment Compatibility

The adapter runtime shall be deployable as a container exposing port `8080` behind a Kubernetes `LoadBalancer` service exposing TCP port `80`.

**Metric:** The running service maps TCP `80` to adapter port `8080`.

### NFR-005 Client Simplicity

Web clients shall receive mirrored Pub/Sub topic data through WebSockets without direct Pub/Sub API or SDK integration.

**Metric:** Client integration requires the WebSocket endpoint and does not require Pub/Sub client credentials.

## 7. System Constraints

### CON-001 GCP Project Constraint

Deployment requires a GCP project with billing enabled.

### CON-002 Topic Access Constraint

Deployment requires read access to the configured Pub/Sub topic. The topic may be in another project if the deployment identity can read it.

### CON-003 Subscription Permission Constraint

Deployment requires permission to create Pub/Sub subscriptions.

### CON-004 GKE Deployment Constraint

The adapter shall run in Google Kubernetes Engine.

### CON-005 LoadBalancer Service Constraint

External access shall be provided through a Kubernetes `LoadBalancer` service using TCP port `80` and adapter port `8080`.

### CON-006 Runtime Composition Constraint

The checked runtime architecture shall include `pulltop`, `exec.sh`, and `websocketd` as runtime elements.

### CON-007 Topic Configuration Constraint

The configured topic value shall be passed from Kubernetes configuration into the adapter runtime through the `SYMBOL` environment variable.

### CON-008 Autoscaling Metric Constraint

Connected-client-count autoscaling shall not be assumed unless the deployment includes an explicit metric path for connected client count.

### 2.4 Operating Environment

The product shall operate in a GCP project with billing enabled and a GKE cluster available. It shall run as a containerized workload exposed through a Kubernetes `LoadBalancer` service using TCP port `80` and adapter port `8080`.

The deployment shall include a Kubernetes ConfigMap for the topic identifier, a Kubernetes Deployment for adapter pods, a Kubernetes Service for external access, and an HPA for horizontal scaling of the Deployment.

### 2.5 Assumptions and Dependencies

- The configured Pub/Sub topic is readable by the deploying user or service account.
- The deployment identity can create Pub/Sub subscriptions.
- The configured topic value is injected into the runtime as the `SYMBOL` environment variable.
- Web clients can establish WebSocket connections to the exposed endpoint.
- Horizontal scaling by connected-client count is not assumed unless an explicit connection-count metric path is configured.
