@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #333333
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #226655
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFF99
    BorderColor #226655
    BorderThickness 1
}

rectangle "Application" as Application #99FF66

package "QAPM_SDK" as QapmSdkPackage {
    rectangle "QAPM-SDK" as QapmSdk
    rectangle "QAPM_Collect" as QapmCollect
    rectangle "QAPM_Storage" as QapmStorage
    rectangle "QAPM_Report" as QapmReport
}

package "QAPM_Plugin" as QapmPluginPackage {
    rectangle "QAPM_Plugin" as QapmPlugin #66CCCC
    rectangle "Plugin init compile" as PluginInitCompile #66CCCC
}

Application --> QapmSdk : Import QAPM_SDK
QapmSdk --> QapmCollect : API
QapmSdk --> QapmStorage : API
QapmSdk --> QapmReport : API

Application --> QapmPlugin : apply QAPM_Plugin
QapmPlugin --> PluginInitCompile : initialize and compile
@enduml

samples.title: s000038_2c7731fc / metagpt-custom/claude-sonnet-4-6

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose
This SRS defines checked requirements for the IceFireDB decentralized database infrastructure components covered by the reviewed scope. It is intended to provide a clean requirements input for downstream architecture diagram generation, implementation review, and verification planning.

### 1.2 Product Scope
IceFireDB is a decentralized database infrastructure project composed of multiple subprojects. This SRS covers:
- IceFireDB-Redis-Proxy as a decentralized Redis proxy that synchronizes Redis instructions between networked agents and writes data to standalone or clustered Redis storage.
- IceFireDB-PubSub as a decentralized publish/subscribe system compatible with Redis publish/subscribe usage.
- IceFireDB-SQLite only at the limited level of MySQL-protocol-style result data handling. Packet-level result parsing is treated as internal data handling, not as a top-level product feature.

Detailed SQLProxy and NoSQL requirements are outside this checked SRS scope. Redis command support requirements are limited to Redis Proxy behavior.

### 1.3 Intended Audience
- Product owners and maintainers of IceFireDB components.
- Integrators deploying Redis-backed or Redis-protocol-compatible services.
- Operators of Redis standalone, Redis cluster, and decentralized Pub/Sub deployments.
- Test and QA engineers verifying repository behavior.
- Architects generating or reviewing software architecture artifacts from requirements.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality Attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Compatibility | Redis Proxy shall be compatible with both standalone and clustered Redis deployments. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Compatibility | PubSub shall be compatible with Redis publish/subscribe usage patterns so Redis Pub/Sub-style applications can migrate to it. | High | Test / Demonstration |
| NFR-003 | Reliability | For clustered Redis deployments, Redis Proxy shall define and validate cluster-state and failover behavior before production use. | Medium | Inspection / Test |
| NFR-004 | Network interoperability | PubSub shall operate across same-network, cross-network, and NAT/private-network node topologies. | High | Test |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | Redis Proxy deployments are constrained to Redis-backed storage modes represented as standalone or clustered Redis. |
| C-002 | PubSub protocol compatibility is constrained to Redis publish/subscribe semantics. |
| C-003 | PubSub peer discovery and routing are constrained to Kademlia DHT with libp2p/IPFS-style peer discovery and routing mechanisms; this should not be interpreted as file-system IPFS storage behavior. |
| C-004 | Release and compliance review shall resolve differences between project-level license declarations and individual file headers before distribution decisions are made. |
| C-005 | Detailed SQLProxy and NoSQL component requirements are outside this checked SRS scope. |
| C-006 | Internal SQL/MySQL packet parsing behavior shall not be treated as a top-level product feature unless separate product requirements define it. |

### 2.4 Operating Environment
| Environment | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Redis standalone deployment | Redis Proxy shall be able to operate with a single Redis storage target. |
| Redis cluster deployment | Redis Proxy shall be able to operate with clustered Redis storage. |
| Multi-node peer network | PubSub shall support nodes deployed on the same network or on different networks. |
| NAT/private-network topology | PubSub shall support communication where participating nodes are behind NAT or located in private networks. |
| SQL/MySQL protocol client path | SQLite-related internal data handling shall be able to process packetized SQL/MySQL-style result data. |

### 3.2 Software/API Interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Redis data source interface | Redis Proxy shall interoperate with standalone and clustered Redis storage modes. |
| Redis command/proxy interface | Redis Proxy shall accept Redis instructions or commands for synchronization and storage forwarding. |
| Redis publish/subscribe interface | PubSub shall support Redis publish/subscribe protocol semantics for compatible client interaction. |
| Internal SQL/MySQL result data interface | The SQLite-related client path shall handle MySQL-protocol-style result metadata, rows, warning counts, and status values as internal data-handling behavior. |

### 3.3 Communication Interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Decentralized Redis agent network | Redis agents shall synchronize Redis instructions across a networked topology. |
| P2P Pub/Sub network | PubSub nodes shall communicate across same-network, cross-network, and NAT/private-network conditions. |
| Peer discovery and routing | PubSub shall use Kademlia DHT with libp2p/IPFS-style peer discovery and routing. |

### 2.5 Assumptions and Dependencies
| ID | Assumption / Dependency |
|---|---|
| AD-001 | Redis Proxy depends on Redis storage being available in standalone or cluster mode. |
| AD-002 | Redis Proxy synchronization depends on participating Redis agents being networked and configured to join the decentralized middleware network. |
| AD-003 | PubSub depends on decentralized peer discovery, routing, and message dissemination mechanisms. |
| AD-004 | PubSub clients are expected to use Redis publish/subscribe semantics. |
| AD-005 | SQL result data handling depends on packetized SQL/MySQL-style response data being available to the internal client path. |

samples.title: s000040_337afcbb / metagpt-custom/gpt-5

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose
This SRS defines checked requirements for PlugIt, a framework for portable integration of user-interface-oriented micro-services. The document is prepared as a clean requirements input for downstream architecture diagram generation, implementation review, and verification planning.

### 1.2 Product Scope
PlugIt provides a framework for integrating multiple micro-services through a common proxy-mediated experience while keeping service data and processes separated. The covered scope includes:
- PlugIt Proxy and PlugIt Service topology.
- Service creation and environment-specific configuration.
- PlugIt Proxy API endpoint configuration.
- Programmatic PlugIt API client access.
- Database-backed services with versioned schema upgrades.
- Example mail-driven processing in the standalone proxy workflow.

Mail-driven processing is example/standalone proxy scope and is not a required capability of every core PlugIt service. Specific end-user screen layouts and detailed interaction patterns are not specified.

### 1.3 Intended Audience
- Service developers creating PlugIt-based services.
- Integrators connecting services to a PlugIt Proxy API.
- Operators and administrators configuring databases, private settings, and deployment environments.
- Maintainers of database-backed or mail-integrated PlugIt services.
- Architects generating or reviewing architecture artifacts from requirements.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality Attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Portability | The system shall support deployment of services in multiple environments by externalizing environment-specific settings into configuration files. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | Maintainability | Database schema evolution shall be managed through versioned upgrade files so schema updates can be applied consistently across developer and production environments. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-003 | Confidentiality | Private deployment settings shall be kept in local configuration excluded from source control to reduce unintended publication of private settings. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-004 | Reliability | In example/standalone proxy scope, mail processing shall avoid acting on messages with invalid routing hashes or auto-response indicators. | High | Test |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | A database technology must be selected and provisioned externally before database-backed service operation; supported examples include MySQL and SQLite. |
| C-002 | A service that integrates through PlugIt depends on a configured PlugIt Proxy API endpoint URL. |
| C-003 | Network access control configuration is constrained by the `PI_ALLOWED_NETWORKS` CIDR list where that setting is used. |
| C-004 | Mail-driven processing requirements are constrained to the example/standalone proxy workflow unless explicitly promoted to core framework scope. |
| C-005 | Detailed end-user interface layouts and interaction patterns are outside this SRS scope. |

### 2.4 Operating Environment
| Environment | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Proxy environment | The deployment shall provide a reachable PlugIt Proxy API endpoint. |
| Service environment | PlugIt Services shall be configured with service-specific settings and deployment-specific local settings. |
| Database environment | A database technology shall be selected, provisioned, and configured before database-backed service use. Examples include MySQL and SQLite. |
| Internal network topology | PlugIt Proxy and PlugIt Services may be deployed inside an internal network while external access reaches services through the proxy. |
| Standalone proxy mail environment | Example mail processing requires access to inbound email messages and mail routing settings. |

### 3.3 Communication Interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| External-to-proxy access | External users or systems shall access PlugIt services through the PlugIt Proxy. |
| Proxy-to-service communication | PlugIt Proxy shall communicate with PlugIt Services through the PlugIt protocol. |
| Service-to-database communication | Each database-backed PlugIt Service shall use its own configured database connection. |
| Email intake | In example/standalone proxy scope, inbound email messages shall be parsed, validated, conditionally processed, and conditionally removed from the mailbox. |

### 2.5 Assumptions and Dependencies
| ID | Assumption / Dependency |
|---|---|
| AD-001 | A PlugIt Proxy API endpoint is available before a service can integrate through the proxy. |
| AD-002 | Database creation, user creation, and connection configuration are handled before database-backed service operation. |
| AD-003 | Private settings are maintained in local configuration and are not published through source control. |
| AD-004 | The protocol and implementation are draft-level and may contain issues or instability. |
| AD-005 | Mail processing behavior applies to the standalone proxy example workflow unless a product design explicitly promotes it to core framework behavior. |

PlugItService3 --> Db3 : service data
@enduml

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS specifies the externally observable requirements for Kangrand, a command-line Pollard's kangaroo interval ECDLP solver for SECP256K1. The specification is limited to behavior and interfaces supported by the available repository documentation and architecture materials.

### Product Scope
The product solves interval discrete logarithm problems on SECP256K1 using Pollard's kangaroo with distinguished points, supports resumable work through saved work files, and supports distributed execution with servers, clients, and offline work-file merging.

### Intended Audience
This document is intended for:
- Operators running the solver locally or in distributed mode
- Integrators using its file or network interfaces
- Test engineers validating documented behavior
- Architects deriving system structure from repository-supported behavior

### References
- Repository: `iceland2k14/Kangrand`
- Repository documentation: `README.md`
- Architecture diagram: `DOC/architecture.jpg`
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality Attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Memory efficiency | The system shall use the distinguished-point method to reduce stored walk data by storing only distinguished points selected by the configured criterion. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | Resource tunability | The system shall allow operators to trade memory use against computational overhead through distinguished-point sizing. | Medium | Analysis |
| NFR-003 | Memory management | When wsplit mode is used, the system shall bound in-memory hash-table growth by resetting the hash table after each backup cycle. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-004 | Security | The distributed server shall not be relied on to authenticate clients or protect exposure on public networks. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-005 | Portability of saved work | The system shall allow a work file to be resumed or merged for the same key and range across different hardware, different distinguished-point bit counts, or different kangaroo-count configurations. Any resulting overhead is an operational note and not an acceptance criterion. | Low | Demonstration |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | The supported cryptographic problem scope is interval ECDLP on SECP256K1. |
| C-002 | The product is operated through command-line options and files; no graphical interface is specified. |
| C-003 | Distributed deployment has no authentication mechanism and therefore must be operator-controlled if network exposure is a concern. |
| C-004 | Merging split work files may require the expected RAM at merge time even if wsplit reduced RAM during collection. |
| C-005 | When a distributed server is restarted with a different configuration, especially when the solving range or target key changes, the operator must stop existing clients; otherwise clients may automatically reconnect and submit wrong points that do not match the new configuration. |

### Operating Environment
- Command-line execution environment
- File-based operation using ASCII input files and work files
- Optional networked distributed environment with servers and clients
- CPU or GPU-enabled command-line execution environments

### Communication Interfaces
The system shall support a client/server communication model in which clients connect to a specified server for distributed execution.

The distributed server shall have no built-in authentication mechanism.

### Assumptions and Dependencies
- The solver is specific to SECP256K1.
- Distributed deployments depend on an operator-managed network because the server has no authentication mechanism.
- Continued use of a saved work file across different hardware or different distinguished-point settings is supported for the same key and range, but may introduce additional overhead.

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #5A7FA3
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

package "Kangrand Distributed Pollard Kangaroo" as Kangrand {
    package "Distributed Search Group A" as SearchGroupA {
        rectangle "Server\n-wsplit" as ServerA
        rectangle "Client A1" as ClientA1
        rectangle "Client A2" as ClientA2
        rectangle "Client A3" as ClientA3
        database "Kangaroo file A1" as KangarooFileA1
        database "Kangaroo file A2" as KangarooFileA2
        database "Kangaroo file A3" as KangarooFileA3
        database "Work files\nHashTable A" as WorkFilesA
        database "Result.txt A" as ResultA
    }

    package "Distributed Search Group B" as SearchGroupB {
        rectangle "Server\n-wsplit" as ServerB
        rectangle "Client B1" as ClientB1
        rectangle "Client B2" as ClientB2
        rectangle "Client B3" as ClientB3
        database "Kangaroo file B1" as KangarooFileB1
        database "Kangaroo file B2" as KangarooFileB2
        database "Kangaroo file B3" as KangarooFileB3
        database "Work files\nHashTable B" as WorkFilesB
        database "Result.txt B" as ResultB
    }

    rectangle "Merger" as Merger
    database "Master WorkFile\nHashTable" as MasterWorkFile
}

ServerA --> ClientA1 : distribute range work
ServerA --> ClientA2 : distribute range work
ServerA --> ClientA3 : distribute range work
ClientA1 --> KangarooFileA1 : save kangaroo state
ClientA2 --> KangarooFileA2 : save kangaroo state
ClientA3 --> KangarooFileA3 : save kangaroo state
ServerA --> WorkFilesA : wsplit backups\nreset hash table
ServerA --> ResultA : solved key output

ServerB --> ClientB1 : distribute range work
ServerB --> ClientB2 : distribute range work
ServerB --> ClientB3 : distribute range work
ClientB1 --> KangarooFileB1 : save kangaroo state
ClientB2 --> KangarooFileB2 : save kangaroo state
ClientB3 --> KangarooFileB3 : save kangaroo state
ServerB --> WorkFilesB : wsplit backups\nreset hash table
ServerB --> ResultB : solved key output

ServerA .. ServerB : parallel distributed search
WorkFilesA --> Merger : merge work files
WorkFilesB --> Merger : merge work files
ResultA --> Merger : collision result
ResultB --> Merger : collision result
Merger --> MasterWorkFile : master work file\nmerged hash table
@enduml

completeness.reasoning: The diagram successfully represents all major functional areas and entities described in the SRS. It includes components for the core solver, command-line interface, distributed operation (client/server), and the complete offline workflow (inspection, merging, backups). Abstractions like the `ExecutionContext` for GPU support and `BackupManager` for wsplit behavior are logical and demonstrate a comprehensive understanding of the requirements.

faithfulness.reasoning: The architecture is largely faithful to the SRS, correctly modeling the client-server relationship, the CLI as the primary interface, and the various file-based interactions. However, it contains a significant error by showing a relationship `inputhandler --> workfiles`. The SRS specifies that the `InputFileHandler` processes the initial problem definition (FR-001), while resuming from work files is a separate function that explicitly does not require the original input file (FR-005). This misrepresents a key workflow.

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The architecture is well-reasoned and demonstrates strong design principles. The decomposition into logical layers (Interface, Core, Persistence, Distribution) enforces a clear separation of concerns. This modularity directly supports key architecturally significant requirements, such as enabling distributed operation through a dedicated layer and managing persistence concerns separately from the core solving logic. The structure is robust and scalable.

traceability.reasoning: Traceability between requirements and architectural components is very strong. Nearly every functional requirement (FR) can be mapped directly to a specific component or a clear interaction between components (e.g., FR-008 to `OfflineMerger`, FR-011 to `DistributedClient`/`DistributedServer`). Non-functional requirements like memory management (NFR-003) and security (NFR-004) are also addressed through corresponding components (`BackupManager`) or the deliberate absence of components (no authentication manager).

samples.title: s000033_b99c1106 / direct/deepseek-v3.2

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose
This Software Requirements Specification defines normalized requirements for an Airflow and Spark integration environment packaged with Docker Compose. The system enables a data engineer to run and observe Spark workloads from Airflow, use an interactive Jupyter/PySpark environment, and run selected bundled Spark distribution commands.

### 1.2 Scope
The product is an integrated Docker environment for Airflow-driven Spark job orchestration. Its core scope includes:
- Docker Compose deployment of Airflow, Postgres, Spark standalone master, Spark workers, and Jupyter/PySpark.
- Airflow DAG-driven Spark job submission to the Spark standalone cluster.
- Shared access to DAG files, Spark application files, notebooks, and input data files across the relevant containers.
- Airflow, Spark master, Jupyter, Spark cluster, and Postgres access through configured ports.
- Inclusion of PySpark in the Airflow image so Spark submission can run from the Airflow machine.

The packaged Spark distribution also provides inherited Spark capabilities, including the Python Spark shell, bundled example runner, Spark-supported master URL forms, and ML examples that load `libsvm` data. These inherited capabilities are retained as secondary requirements and should not be treated as the system's primary product differentiator.

### 1.3 Intended Audience
- Data engineers scheduling Spark jobs through Airflow.
- Platform engineers operating the Docker Compose environment.
- Developers using Jupyter/PySpark for interactive Spark work.
- Testers validating Spark job submission, container startup, and exposed service ports.
- Architecture-generation tools consuming a clean SRS without review metadata.

### 1.4 Definitions and Abbreviations
| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Airflow | Workflow scheduler used to define and run DAGs that submit Spark jobs. |
| DAG | Directed acyclic graph defining an Airflow workflow. |
| Spark standalone cluster | Spark deployment mode with a master and worker services. |
| Spark master | Cluster manager endpoint reachable at `spark://spark:7077`. |
| Spark worker | Service that executes Spark workloads assigned by the Spark master. |
| `SparkSubmitOperator` | Airflow operator used to submit Spark applications. |
| `spark_default` | Airflow Spark connection used by the DAG to reach the Spark cluster. |
| PySpark | Python interface for Spark. |
| `MASTER` | Environment variable used by Spark commands to select an execution backend. |
| `libsvm` | Dataset format used by bundled Spark ML examples. |
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Quality attribute | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | The packaged Spark runtime shall be compatible with Spark `2.4.5` built for Hadoop `2.6`. | Compatibility | High | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | The Airflow-side Python environment shall use pinned dependency versions for `celery==4.1.1`, `kombu==4.2.0`, `tornado==5.1.1`, `werkzeug==0.16.0`, and `SQLAlchemy==1.3.15`, and shall include `pyspark`. | Reproducibility | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-003 | The configured project deployment shall verify Spark submission against the standalone master at `spark://spark:7077`; other Spark master URL forms shall be treated as inherited Spark compatibility rather than independently deployed project targets. | Portability | Medium | Test |
| NFR-004 | The environment shall support worker-count scaling by allowing additional Spark worker services to follow the existing worker configuration pattern. | Scalability | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-005 | The environment shall provide operational observability through Airflow task logs, the Spark master UI, and container logs. | Observability | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-006 | Spark RPC authentication, RPC encryption, local storage encryption, and SSL are disabled in the provided local Compose configuration; the environment shall therefore be treated as a local development/testing setup unless security settings are changed. | Security | High | Inspection |

## 8. Constraints

| ID | Constraint | Type |
|---|---|---|
| C-001 | The Airflow service depends on Postgres being available. | Runtime dependency |
| C-002 | Spark submission inside Airflow depends on `pyspark` being installed in the Airflow Python environment. | Runtime dependency |
| C-003 | The configured project Spark cluster target is `spark://spark:7077`. | Runtime configuration |
| C-004 | The packaged Spark distribution is constrained to Spark `2.4.5` with Hadoop `2.6`. | Compatibility |
| C-005 | Airflow web UI, Spark master UI, Jupyter, Spark application UI, Spark cluster, and Postgres access depend on the configured host port mappings. | Network |
| C-006 | The documented Spark CLI entry points inherited from the bundled Spark distribution are `./bin/pyspark` and `./bin/run-example`. | Interface |
| C-007 | Mesos, YARN, and generic `spark://...` target forms are inherited Spark target syntax unless this environment provides matching deployed infrastructure. | Scope |
| C-008 | The local Compose configuration disables several Spark security features and is therefore not suitable as-is for a hardened production deployment. | Security |

### 2.4 Operating Environment
| Environment element | Required configuration |
|---|---|
| Docker Compose | Uses Compose file format `3.7` to run the multi-container environment. |
| Postgres | Runs PostgreSQL `9.6`, stores Airflow metadata, and exposes port `5432`. |
| Airflow webserver | Runs the `docker-airflow-spark:latest` image, uses LocalExecutor mode, depends on Postgres, and exposes port `8282`. |
| Spark master | Runs `bitnami/spark:latest`, uses `SPARK_MODE=master`, exposes web UI port `8181` and cluster port `7077`. |
| Spark workers | Run `bitnami/spark:latest`, use `SPARK_MODE=worker`, connect to `spark://spark:7077`, and use configured worker memory and core settings. |
| Jupyter/PySpark | Runs `jupyter/pyspark-notebook`, exposes notebook port `8888`, and exposes Spark application UI ports `4040-4080`. |
| Airflow Python environment | Includes pinned Airflow-side dependencies and `pyspark`. |
| Bundled Spark runtime | Includes Spark `2.4.5` built for Hadoop `2.6`. |

### 3.3 Communication Interfaces
| ID | Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|---|
| CI-001 | Airflow to Postgres | Airflow shall depend on and communicate with Postgres for metadata persistence. |
| CI-002 | Airflow to Spark master | Airflow shall submit Spark jobs to the Spark master at `spark://spark:7077`. |
| CI-003 | Spark master to workers | Spark workers shall register with and receive work from the Spark master. |
| CI-004 | Host to service UIs | The host shall access Airflow, Spark master, Jupyter, and Spark application UIs through mapped ports. |
| CI-005 | Spark command target selection | Inherited Spark commands shall accept supported master target strings through the `MASTER` variable. |

### 2.6 Assumptions and Dependencies
| ID | Assumption or dependency |
|---|---|
| AD-001 | Docker and Docker Compose are available on the host machine. |
| AD-002 | The Airflow Docker image is built before starting the Compose environment. |
| AD-003 | The `spark_default` Airflow connection is configured with host `spark://spark` and port `7077` before the Spark DAG is run. |
| AD-004 | Mounted directories provide consistent paths for DAGs, Spark applications, notebooks, and input data across Airflow, Spark, and Jupyter containers. |
| AD-005 | Spark worker services can reach the Spark master through the Compose network. |
| AD-006 | Jupyter token retrieval depends on the Jupyter container logs. |

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
top to bottom direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #6A00FF
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #888888
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #AAAAAA
    BorderThickness 1
}

rectangle "Data Engineer" as DataEngineer

package "Docker Compose Environment" as ComposeEnvironment {
    rectangle "Postgres" as Postgres
    rectangle "Airflow\n(Spark Driver)" as Airflow
    rectangle "Jupyter Notebook\n(Spark)" as JupyterNotebook
    rectangle "Cluster Manager\n(Spark Standalone)" as ClusterManager

    package "Spark Workers" as SparkWorkers {
        rectangle "Worker 1" as Worker1
        rectangle "Worker 2" as Worker2
        rectangle "Worker 3" as Worker3
        rectangle "Worker N" as WorkerN
    }
}

DataEngineer --> Postgres : access
DataEngineer --> Airflow : schedule and observe DAGs
DataEngineer --> JupyterNotebook : interactive notebooks
Airflow <--> Postgres : metadata
Airflow --> ClusterManager : Spark job submission
ClusterManager --> Worker1 : dispatch workloads
ClusterManager --> Worker2 : dispatch workloads
ClusterManager --> Worker3 : dispatch workloads
ClusterManager --> WorkerN : dispatch workloads
@enduml

samples.title: s000038_2c7731fc / direct/gpt-5

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose
This SRS defines checked requirements for the IceFireDB decentralized database infrastructure components covered by the reviewed scope. It is intended to provide a clean requirements input for downstream architecture diagram generation, implementation review, and verification planning.

### 1.2 Product Scope
IceFireDB is a decentralized database infrastructure project composed of multiple subprojects. This SRS covers:
- IceFireDB-Redis-Proxy as a decentralized Redis proxy that synchronizes Redis instructions between networked agents and writes data to standalone or clustered Redis storage.
- IceFireDB-PubSub as a decentralized publish/subscribe system compatible with Redis publish/subscribe usage.
- IceFireDB-SQLite only at the limited level of MySQL-protocol-style result data handling. Packet-level result parsing is treated as internal data handling, not as a top-level product feature.

Detailed SQLProxy and NoSQL requirements are outside this checked SRS scope. Redis command support requirements are limited to Redis Proxy behavior.

### 1.3 Intended Audience
- Product owners and maintainers of IceFireDB components.
- Integrators deploying Redis-backed or Redis-protocol-compatible services.
- Operators of Redis standalone, Redis cluster, and decentralized Pub/Sub deployments.
- Test and QA engineers verifying repository behavior.
- Architects generating or reviewing software architecture artifacts from requirements.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality Attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Compatibility | Redis Proxy shall be compatible with both standalone and clustered Redis deployments. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Compatibility | PubSub shall be compatible with Redis publish/subscribe usage patterns so Redis Pub/Sub-style applications can migrate to it. | High | Test / Demonstration |
| NFR-003 | Reliability | For clustered Redis deployments, Redis Proxy shall define and validate cluster-state and failover behavior before production use. | Medium | Inspection / Test |
| NFR-004 | Network interoperability | PubSub shall operate across same-network, cross-network, and NAT/private-network node topologies. | High | Test |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | Redis Proxy deployments are constrained to Redis-backed storage modes represented as standalone or clustered Redis. |
| C-002 | PubSub protocol compatibility is constrained to Redis publish/subscribe semantics. |
| C-003 | PubSub peer discovery and routing are constrained to Kademlia DHT with libp2p/IPFS-style peer discovery and routing mechanisms; this should not be interpreted as file-system IPFS storage behavior. |
| C-004 | Release and compliance review shall resolve differences between project-level license declarations and individual file headers before distribution decisions are made. |
| C-005 | Detailed SQLProxy and NoSQL component requirements are outside this checked SRS scope. |
| C-006 | Internal SQL/MySQL packet parsing behavior shall not be treated as a top-level product feature unless separate product requirements define it. |

### 2.4 Operating Environment
| Environment | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Redis standalone deployment | Redis Proxy shall be able to operate with a single Redis storage target. |
| Redis cluster deployment | Redis Proxy shall be able to operate with clustered Redis storage. |
| Multi-node peer network | PubSub shall support nodes deployed on the same network or on different networks. |
| NAT/private-network topology | PubSub shall support communication where participating nodes are behind NAT or located in private networks. |
| SQL/MySQL protocol client path | SQLite-related internal data handling shall be able to process packetized SQL/MySQL-style result data. |

### 3.2 Software/API Interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Redis data source interface | Redis Proxy shall interoperate with standalone and clustered Redis storage modes. |
| Redis command/proxy interface | Redis Proxy shall accept Redis instructions or commands for synchronization and storage forwarding. |
| Redis publish/subscribe interface | PubSub shall support Redis publish/subscribe protocol semantics for compatible client interaction. |
| Internal SQL/MySQL result data interface | The SQLite-related client path shall handle MySQL-protocol-style result metadata, rows, warning counts, and status values as internal data-handling behavior. |

### 3.3 Communication Interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Decentralized Redis agent network | Redis agents shall synchronize Redis instructions across a networked topology. |
| P2P Pub/Sub network | PubSub nodes shall communicate across same-network, cross-network, and NAT/private-network conditions. |
| Peer discovery and routing | PubSub shall use Kademlia DHT with libp2p/IPFS-style peer discovery and routing. |

### 2.5 Assumptions and Dependencies
| ID | Assumption / Dependency |
|---|---|
| AD-001 | Redis Proxy depends on Redis storage being available in standalone or cluster mode. |
| AD-002 | Redis Proxy synchronization depends on participating Redis agents being networked and configured to join the decentralized middleware network. |
| AD-003 | PubSub depends on decentralized peer discovery, routing, and message dissemination mechanisms. |
| AD-004 | PubSub clients are expected to use Redis publish/subscribe semantics. |
| AD-005 | SQL result data handling depends on packetized SQL/MySQL-style response data being available to the internal client path. |

@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #333333
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #444444
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #444444
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #444444
    BorderThickness 1
}

package "Web2 application stack" as Web2Stack {
    rectangle "Applications" as Web2Applications
    rectangle "Platform" as Web2Platform
    rectangle "Processing\nBusiness Logic" as Web2Processing
    rectangle "File System\nLarge Files and Media" as Web2FileSystem
    database "BlockChain Database\nTransactions, Contracts,\nCertificates and Receipts" as Web2BlockchainDatabase
    rectangle "IceFireDB" as Web2IceFireDB
}

package "Web3 application stack" as Web3Stack {
    rectangle "Applications" as Web3Applications
    rectangle "Platform" as Web3Platform
    rectangle "Decentralized Processing\nBusiness Logic" as Web3Processing
    rectangle "Decentralized File System\nLarge Files and Media" as Web3FileSystem
    database "BlockChain Database\nTransactions, Contracts,\nCertificates and Receipts" as Web3BlockchainDatabase
    rectangle "IceFireDB" as Web3IceFireDB
}

Web2Applications --> Web2Platform
Web2Platform --> Web2Processing
Web2Processing --> Web2FileSystem
Web2Processing --> Web2BlockchainDatabase
Web2BlockchainDatabase --> Web2IceFireDB

Web3Applications --> Web3Platform
Web3Platform --> Web3Processing
Web3Processing --> Web3FileSystem
Web3Processing --> Web3BlockchainDatabase
Web3BlockchainDatabase --> Web3IceFireDB

Web2IceFireDB ..> Web3IceFireDB : decentralized database support
@enduml

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS defines the checked software requirements for `OAID/Tengine`. It covers the documented example workflows, exposed APIs, model serialization and conversion behavior, core module structure, and supported build and deployment characteristics.

### Product Scope
Tengine Lite is an inference-oriented software product that provides:
- Example applications for classification, detection, segmentation, pose estimation, landmark detection, and character recognition tasks
- C, C++, and Python-facing API surfaces
- Core architecture modules for device, scheduler, operator, and serializer responsibilities
- A serializer that decodes binary `tmfile` model data
- Model conversion tooling, including a Linux pre-compiled tool and a browser-local WebAssembly-based online converter
- Cross-platform compilation based on CMake

### Intended Audience
This document is intended for:
- Developers integrating Tengine Lite through C, C++, or Python APIs
- Users running the provided example applications
- Architects and maintainers deriving system structure from the repository
- Test engineers validating documented product behavior
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality Attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Portability | The system shall support quick cross-platform compilation based on CMake. | High | Inspection |
| NFR-002 | Compatibility | Tengine Lite shall remain compatible with the original Tengine C API for documented example usage. | High | Demonstration |
| NFR-003 | Local processing behavior | The online model conversion tool shall convert models locally in the browser based on WebAssembly, and the documentation shall state that no private data will be uploaded. | Medium | Demonstration |
| NFR-004 | Platform compatibility | The pre-compiled model conversion tool shall be provided for Linux systems. | Medium | Inspection |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | Compilation is constrained to a CMake-based build approach for the documented cross-platform compilation path. |
| C-002 | The pre-compiled model conversion tool is constrained to Linux systems. |
| C-003 | The online model conversion tool depends on browser WebAssembly support and executes conversion locally in the browser. |
| C-004 | Example execution depends on the presence of required local image and model assets under the project root folder. |

### Operating Environment
- Cross-platform build environments using CMake
- Linux environments for the pre-compiled model conversion tool
- Web browsers with WebAssembly support for the online model conversion tool
- Local execution environments with access to required model and image assets

### Communication Interfaces
- No external network communication interface is required for the online model conversion workflow.
- The browser-based conversion workflow shall execute locally in the browser through WebAssembly.

### Assumptions and Dependencies
- Example execution depends on required image and model assets being available in the expected local project paths
- Model conversion depends on either the Linux prebuilt tool or a browser capable of local WebAssembly execution
- Native integration depends on the provided library and API surfaces being available in the build environment

top to bottom direction

package "Tengine Lite" as Tengine {
    package "Convert Tool" as ConvertTool {
        rectangle "TensorFlow" as TensorFlow
        rectangle "TF Lite" as TfLite
        rectangle "PyTorch / ONNX" as PytorchOnnx
        rectangle "MXNet" as Mxnet
        rectangle "Caffe" as Caffe
        rectangle "Darknet" as Darknet
        rectangle "MegEngine" as MegEngine
        rectangle "OneFlow" as OneFlow
        rectangle "PaddlePaddle" as PaddlePaddle
        rectangle "Others" as OtherModels
    }

    package "Serializer" as SerializerLayer {
        rectangle "TensorFlow Serializer" as TensorFlowSerializer
        rectangle "TF Lite Serializer" as TfLiteSerializer
        rectangle "ONNX Serializer" as OnnxSerializer
        rectangle "MXNet Serializer" as MxnetSerializer
        rectangle "Caffe Serializer" as CaffeSerializer
        rectangle "Darknet Serializer" as DarknetSerializer
        rectangle "ncnn Serializer" as NcnnSerializer

        rectangle "MLIR Serializer" as MlirSerializer
        rectangle "Others Serializer" as OtherSerializer
        rectangle "tmfile Serializer" as TmfileSerializer
    }

    package "Engine" as EngineLayer {
        rectangle "Graph Partition" as GraphPartition
        rectangle "Device Allocator" as DeviceAllocator
        rectangle "Exec Scheduler" as ExecScheduler
        rectangle "Low Bit Quantize" as LowBitQuantize
        rectangle "CPU Backend" as CpuBackend
        rectangle "GPU Backend" as GpuBackend
        rectangle "NPU Backend" as NpuBackend
    }

    package "Runtime Optimize" as RuntimeOptimize {
        rectangle "Winograd" as Winograd
        rectangle "SGEMM" as Sgemm
        rectangle "Mix Bit Calculation" as MixBitCalculation
        rectangle "CUDA / Vulkan / OpenCL" as GpuRuntime
        rectangle "TIM-VX / OpenDLA Plugin" as TimvxOpenDla
        rectangle "SIMD" as Simd
        rectangle "Assembly" as Assembly
        rectangle "Auto Kernel Plugin" as AutoKernelPlugin
        rectangle "TensorRT / ACL Plugin" as TensorRtAcl
        rectangle "Amlogic / RK / NXP / JLQ\nNPU and NVDLA" as NpuRuntime
        rectangle "ARM / X86 / RISCV / MIPS" as CpuTargets
        rectangle "NVIDIA / Mali / Adreno" as GpuTargets
    }
}

project_context.introduction:
### Purpose
This SRS defines the checked and normalized software requirements for `s000091_f459979d`. It captures the repository-supported behavior of a system for LwM2M-oriented device-management scenarios that combines authenticated management APIs, blockchain-backed storage of client/bootstrap information, and LwM2M node JSON handling.

### Product scope
The product is a backend-oriented system that supports:
- management-side access-controlled user operations,
- storage and retrieval of client/bootstrap information through blockchain integration,
- LwM2M-related data parsing and handling, and
- broader LwM2M device-management and bootstrap scenarios supported by the repository context.

This SRS is limited to behavior that is supported by the provided implementation and review-backed corrections. It does not restate deployment architecture components item by item.

### Intended audience
This document is intended for:
- maintainers of the backend, blockchain-integration, and LwM2M-related components,
- architects deriving component and interface views,
- integration engineers connecting management, blockchain, and LwM2M services, and
- test engineers validating externally observable behavior.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Security | User-management endpoints shall enforce token-based access control before performing retrieval or creation operations. Requests that fail validation shall not perform the protected operation and shall return the corresponding error status. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Compatibility | Blockchain integration shall be compatible with an Ethereum-compatible node reachable via HTTP through Web3j. | Medium | Inspection |
| NFR-003 | Data integrity | LwM2M node JSON processing shall reject structurally invalid payloads rather than silently accepting them. Invalid node elements, and `instances` objects missing required `id`, shall cause parsing failure. | Medium | Test |
| NFR-004 | Interoperability | Smart-contract string-field exchange shall use fixed 32-byte ASCII-compatible encoding on submission and ASCII decoding on retrieval to match contract interaction requirements. | High | Inspection |

## 7. System Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | Protected user operations are constrained by token validation using `JwtUtility.isValidToken(auth, 1)`. |
| C-002 | Blockchain connectivity is constrained to Web3j using HTTP transport to an Ethereum-compatible node. |
| C-003 | Blockchain client/bootstrap operations are constrained by smart-contract interaction patterns for retrieving all clients, adding client data, and retrieving a client by endpoint. |
| C-004 | LwM2M JSON handling is constrained by Leshan/LwM2M node and resource model types. |
| C-005 | Contract-exchanged string fields are constrained to fixed 32-byte ASCII-compatible encoding and decoding behavior. |

### Operating environment
| Environment aspect | Supported statement |
|---|---|
| Backend runtime | The system operates in a Java backend environment. |
| Web framework | Protected management operations are exposed through Spring HTTP controller behavior. |
| Blockchain integration | Blockchain connectivity uses Web3j with HTTP transport to an Ethereum-compatible node. |
| LwM2M integration | LwM2M data handling uses Leshan/LwM2M model types and node structures. |

### Software/API interfaces
| Interface | Requirement summary |
|---|---|
| User HTTP API | The User API shall support user retrieval and user creation operations, both access-controlled based on the Authorization header. |
| Blockchain client/bootstrap interface | The system shall interact with blockchain-backed client/bootstrap storage through contract operations for retrieving all clients, adding client data, and retrieving a client by endpoint. |
| Ethereum connectivity interface | The system shall connect to an Ethereum-compatible node through Web3j using HTTP transport. |
| LwM2M node deserialization interface | The system shall accept JSON input and convert it into typed LwM2M node structures. |

### Communication interfaces
| Interface | Requirement summary |
|---|---|
| Backend API communication | Management operations shall be exposed through HTTP request/response interactions. |
| Blockchain communication | Blockchain node communication shall use Web3j HTTP transport. |

### Assumptions and dependencies
| ID | Statement |
|---|---|
| A-001 | Protected user-management operations depend on Authorization-header token validation results. |
| A-002 | Blockchain-backed client/bootstrap operations depend on Ethereum connectivity through Web3j HTTP transport and smart-contract methods. |
| A-003 | LwM2M node parsing depends on JSON payloads matching the expected node/object/value structure rules. |
| A-004 | Client/bootstrap data exchanged with the blockchain depends on fixed 32-byte ASCII-compatible field encoding and decoding. |

@startuml

left to right direction

    BorderThickness 1

    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

package "LwM2M Blockchain Platform" as Platform {
    rectangle "External Applications\nusing LwM2M" as ExternalApps
    rectangle "Secure API\nAuthenticated HTTP endpoints" as SecureApi
    rectangle "LwM2M Server\nLeshan server component" as Lwm2mServer
    rectangle "LwM2M Client\nDevice 1" as Lwm2mClientOne
    rectangle "LwM2M Client\nDevice 2" as Lwm2mClientTwo
    rectangle "LwM2M Bootstrap Server" as BootstrapServer
    rectangle "Management Web UI" as ManagementUi
    cloud "Blockchain Network\nEthereum / Web3j" as BlockchainNetwork
    database "BootstrapStore\nSmart Contract" as BootstrapStore
}

ExternalApps <--> SecureApi : access LwM2M client data\nclient management
SecureApi --> Lwm2mServer : protected operations
ExternalApps --> ManagementUi : critical events

ManagementUi --> SecureApi : register LwM2M clients\nmanage users
ManagementUi --> BlockchainNetwork : access control\ncritical events

Lwm2mServer <--> Lwm2mClientOne : LwM2M secure connection
Lwm2mServer <--> Lwm2mClientTwo : LwM2M secure connection
Lwm2mClientTwo <--> BootstrapServer : LwM2M client bootstrapping

Lwm2mServer <--> BlockchainNetwork : external application\nreliable access control
BootstrapServer <--> BlockchainNetwork : bootstrapping\nreliable information

BlockchainNetwork --> BootstrapStore : getAllClients
BlockchainNetwork --> BootstrapStore : addClient
BlockchainNetwork --> BootstrapStore : getClient
SecureApi --> BlockchainNetwork : Web3j HTTP service
BootstrapServer --> SecureApi : LwM2M node JSON\nbootstrap data
@enduml

samples.title: s000026_5d153cf4 / direct/claude-sonnet-4-6

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose

This Software Requirements Specification defines the checked requirements for a Google Kubernetes Engine deployment that distributes Google Cloud Pub/Sub topic messages to web clients over WebSockets.

The document is prepared for architecture view generation and omits review notes, provenance fields, and supporting-material identifiers.

### 1.2 Product Scope

The product is a WebSocket adapter and deployment package that:

- Mirrors messages from a configured Google Cloud Pub/Sub topic to connected WebSocket clients.
- Lets web clients receive topic data without directly using the Pub/Sub API or SDK.
- Runs as a containerized runtime in Google Kubernetes Engine.
- Exposes the adapter through a Kubernetes `LoadBalancer` service.
- Uses a Kubernetes ConfigMap value to bind a deployment instance to one Pub/Sub topic.
- Uses a `websocketd` bridge with a Node.js `pulltop` process to serve topic data to clients.
- Supports horizontal pod autoscaling through Kubernetes autoscaling configuration.

Each deployment instance binds one externally reachable WebSocket endpoint to one configured Pub/Sub topic. Multiple topics shall be served by multiple independently configured deployment instances.

### 1.3 Intended Audience

The intended audience includes deployment operators, Kubernetes operators, GCP project administrators, web-client integrators, testers, and architecture evaluators.

### 1.4 Definitions and Abbreviations

- GCP: Google Cloud Platform.
- GKE: Google Kubernetes Engine.
- HPA: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler.
- Pub/Sub: Google Cloud Pub/Sub.
- SRS: Software Requirements Specification.
- VM: Virtual machine.
- WebSocket: Full-duplex network communication protocol used by connected clients.
project_context.asr:
## 6. Non-Functional Requirements

### NFR-001 Horizontal Scalability

The deployment shall support horizontal pod scaling through Kubernetes HPA configuration.

**Metric:** The HPA targets the adapter Deployment with min replicas `1`, max replicas `2`, and CPU utilization target `80`.

### NFR-002 Subscription Efficiency

The adapter shall support many WebSocket clients while using one Pub/Sub subscription per running VM or pod instance.

**Metric:** For `N` running instances and `M` connected clients, subscription consumption scales with `N` rather than `M`.

### NFR-003 Client Data Continuity

The adapter shall provide recent cached messages so client displays can show meaningful data during low-traffic periods.

**Metric:** A client can receive up to the last 10 cached messages for the configured topic.

### NFR-004 Deployment Compatibility

The adapter runtime shall be deployable as a container exposing port `8080` behind a Kubernetes `LoadBalancer` service exposing TCP port `80`.

**Metric:** The running service maps TCP `80` to adapter port `8080`.

### NFR-005 Client Simplicity

Web clients shall receive mirrored Pub/Sub topic data through WebSockets without direct Pub/Sub API or SDK integration.

**Metric:** Client integration requires the WebSocket endpoint and does not require Pub/Sub client credentials.

## 7. System Constraints

### CON-001 GCP Project Constraint

Deployment requires a GCP project with billing enabled.

### CON-002 Topic Access Constraint

Deployment requires read access to the configured Pub/Sub topic. The topic may be in another project if the deployment identity can read it.

### CON-003 Subscription Permission Constraint

Deployment requires permission to create Pub/Sub subscriptions.

### CON-004 GKE Deployment Constraint

The adapter shall run in Google Kubernetes Engine.

### CON-005 LoadBalancer Service Constraint

External access shall be provided through a Kubernetes `LoadBalancer` service using TCP port `80` and adapter port `8080`.

### CON-006 Runtime Composition Constraint

The checked runtime architecture shall include `pulltop`, `exec.sh`, and `websocketd` as runtime elements.

### CON-007 Topic Configuration Constraint

The configured topic value shall be passed from Kubernetes configuration into the adapter runtime through the `SYMBOL` environment variable.

### CON-008 Autoscaling Metric Constraint

Connected-client-count autoscaling shall not be assumed unless the deployment includes an explicit metric path for connected client count.

### 2.4 Operating Environment

The product shall operate in a GCP project with billing enabled and a GKE cluster available. It shall run as a containerized workload exposed through a Kubernetes `LoadBalancer` service using TCP port `80` and adapter port `8080`.

The deployment shall include a Kubernetes ConfigMap for the topic identifier, a Kubernetes Deployment for adapter pods, a Kubernetes Service for external access, and an HPA for horizontal scaling of the Deployment.

### 2.5 Assumptions and Dependencies

- The configured Pub/Sub topic is readable by the deploying user or service account.
- The deployment identity can create Pub/Sub subscriptions.
- The configured topic value is injected into the runtime as the `SYMBOL` environment variable.
- Web clients can establish WebSocket connections to the exposed endpoint.
- Horizontal scaling by connected-client count is not assumed unless an explicit connection-count metric path is configured.

project_context.introduction:
### 1.1 Purpose
This SRS defines checked requirements for the IceFireDB decentralized database infrastructure components covered by the reviewed scope. It is intended to provide a clean requirements input for downstream architecture diagram generation, implementation review, and verification planning.

### 1.2 Product Scope
IceFireDB is a decentralized database infrastructure project composed of multiple subprojects. This SRS covers:
- IceFireDB-Redis-Proxy as a decentralized Redis proxy that synchronizes Redis instructions between networked agents and writes data to standalone or clustered Redis storage.
- IceFireDB-PubSub as a decentralized publish/subscribe system compatible with Redis publish/subscribe usage.
- IceFireDB-SQLite only at the limited level of MySQL-protocol-style result data handling. Packet-level result parsing is treated as internal data handling, not as a top-level product feature.

Detailed SQLProxy and NoSQL requirements are outside this checked SRS scope. Redis command support requirements are limited to Redis Proxy behavior.

### 1.3 Intended Audience
- Product owners and maintainers of IceFireDB components.
- Integrators deploying Redis-backed or Redis-protocol-compatible services.
- Operators of Redis standalone, Redis cluster, and decentralized Pub/Sub deployments.
- Test and QA engineers verifying repository behavior.
- Architects generating or reviewing software architecture artifacts from requirements.
project_context.asr:
## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Quality Attribute | Requirement | Priority | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Compatibility | Redis Proxy shall be compatible with both standalone and clustered Redis deployments. | High | Test |
| NFR-002 | Compatibility | PubSub shall be compatible with Redis publish/subscribe usage patterns so Redis Pub/Sub-style applications can migrate to it. | High | Test / Demonstration |
| NFR-003 | Reliability | For clustered Redis deployments, Redis Proxy shall define and validate cluster-state and failover behavior before production use. | Medium | Inspection / Test |
| NFR-004 | Network interoperability | PubSub shall operate across same-network, cross-network, and NAT/private-network node topologies. | High | Test |

## 7. Constraints

| ID | Constraint |
|---|---|
| C-001 | Redis Proxy deployments are constrained to Redis-backed storage modes represented as standalone or clustered Redis. |
| C-002 | PubSub protocol compatibility is constrained to Redis publish/subscribe semantics. |
| C-003 | PubSub peer discovery and routing are constrained to Kademlia DHT with libp2p/IPFS-style peer discovery and routing mechanisms; this should not be interpreted as file-system IPFS storage behavior. |
| C-004 | Release and compliance review shall resolve differences between project-level license declarations and individual file headers before distribution decisions are made. |
| C-005 | Detailed SQLProxy and NoSQL component requirements are outside this checked SRS scope. |
| C-006 | Internal SQL/MySQL packet parsing behavior shall not be treated as a top-level product feature unless separate product requirements define it. |

### 2.4 Operating Environment
| Environment | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Redis standalone deployment | Redis Proxy shall be able to operate with a single Redis storage target. |
| Redis cluster deployment | Redis Proxy shall be able to operate with clustered Redis storage. |
| Multi-node peer network | PubSub shall support nodes deployed on the same network or on different networks. |
| NAT/private-network topology | PubSub shall support communication where participating nodes are behind NAT or located in private networks. |
| SQL/MySQL protocol client path | SQLite-related internal data handling shall be able to process packetized SQL/MySQL-style result data. |

### 3.2 Software/API Interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Redis data source interface | Redis Proxy shall interoperate with standalone and clustered Redis storage modes. |
| Redis command/proxy interface | Redis Proxy shall accept Redis instructions or commands for synchronization and storage forwarding. |
| Redis publish/subscribe interface | PubSub shall support Redis publish/subscribe protocol semantics for compatible client interaction. |
| Internal SQL/MySQL result data interface | The SQLite-related client path shall handle MySQL-protocol-style result metadata, rows, warning counts, and status values as internal data-handling behavior. |

### 3.3 Communication Interfaces
| Interface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Decentralized Redis agent network | Redis agents shall synchronize Redis instructions across a networked topology. |
| P2P Pub/Sub network | PubSub nodes shall communicate across same-network, cross-network, and NAT/private-network conditions. |
| Peer discovery and routing | PubSub shall use Kademlia DHT with libp2p/IPFS-style peer discovery and routing. |

### 2.5 Assumptions and Dependencies
| ID | Assumption / Dependency |
|---|---|
| AD-001 | Redis Proxy depends on Redis storage being available in standalone or cluster mode. |
| AD-002 | Redis Proxy synchronization depends on participating Redis agents being networked and configured to join the decentralized middleware network. |
| AD-003 | PubSub depends on decentralized peer discovery, routing, and message dissemination mechanisms. |
| AD-004 | PubSub clients are expected to use Redis publish/subscribe semantics. |
| AD-005 | SQL result data handling depends on packetized SQL/MySQL-style response data being available to the internal client path. |

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #333333
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #444444
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #444444
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #444444
    BorderThickness 1
}

package "Web2 application stack" as Web2Stack {
    rectangle "Applications" as Web2Applications
    rectangle "Platform" as Web2Platform
    rectangle "Processing\nBusiness Logic" as Web2Processing
    rectangle "File System\nLarge Files and Media" as Web2FileSystem
    database "BlockChain Database\nTransactions, Contracts,\nCertificates and Receipts" as Web2BlockchainDatabase
    rectangle "IceFireDB" as Web2IceFireDB
}

package "Web3 application stack" as Web3Stack {
    rectangle "Applications" as Web3Applications
    rectangle "Platform" as Web3Platform
    rectangle "Decentralized Processing\nBusiness Logic" as Web3Processing
    rectangle "Decentralized File System\nLarge Files and Media" as Web3FileSystem
    database "BlockChain Database\nTransactions, Contracts,\nCertificates and Receipts" as Web3BlockchainDatabase
    rectangle "IceFireDB" as Web3IceFireDB
}

Web2Applications --> Web2Platform
Web2Platform --> Web2Processing
Web2Processing --> Web2FileSystem
Web2Processing --> Web2BlockchainDatabase
Web2BlockchainDatabase --> Web2IceFireDB

Web3Applications --> Web3Platform
Web3Platform --> Web3Processing
Web3Processing --> Web3FileSystem
Web3Processing --> Web3BlockchainDatabase
Web3BlockchainDatabase --> Web3IceFireDB

Web2IceFireDB ..> Web3IceFireDB : decentralized database support
@enduml
